**Build**: 5392 jobs green (was 5393 before; âˆ’1 from the deleted file). No new files created. No salvage migrated. The dissolution is the textbook case CLAUDE.md describes â€” a Bridge file synthesizing connections no paper made, decorated with `rfl`-on-stipulated-data theorems whose deletion costs nothing because nothing consumes them.

## [0.230.451] - 2026-04-27

### Klecha â†” Abusch ULC convergence + Abusch substrate refactor (mirror of 0.230.450)

A 4-agent deep audit of the Abusch substrate (`Theories/Semantics/Tense/TemporalDeRe.lean` + relevant sections of `Tense/Basic.lean` + `Phenomena/TenseAspect/Studies/Abusch1997.lean`) flagged the same wrapper-alias issues 0.230.450 deleted on the Klecha side, plus two new findings: a misnamed `Abusch1997.lean` study file (its content is Schlenker/Kaplan context-tower material with zero theorems anchored to anything Abusch-distinctive), and a ULC misattribution â€” the *presuppositional implementation* attributed by linglib to Abusch is actually Heim 1994's manuscript "Comments on Abusch's theory of tense", not Abusch herself.

This commit lands the medium-scope fixes recommended by the audit: substrate trim mirroring 0.230.450's Klecha-side cleanup, attribution corrections, and the kernel-checked Klecha â†” Abusch ULC equivalence theorem the prior commit's docstring prose had been claiming. The misnamed `Abusch1997.lean` rename + modal-layer ULC lift (the audit's "wide" recommendations) are deferred.

**Bibliography hygiene** (M-P1):
- New `@incollection{heim-1994-comments}` entry for *Comments on Abusch's Theory of Tense*, in Kamp (ed.) *Ellipsis, Tense and Questions*, Dyana-2 Esprit Basic Research Project 6852 Deliverable R2.2.B (URL: `https://semanticsarchive.net/Archive/Tg5ZmI1N/heim-comments-Abusch-94.pdf`). The existing `heim-1994` entry is a different paper (the IATL interrogatives one) â€” Heim's commentary on Abusch needed its own bib slot per linguistics audit M1. (Initially typed as `@unpublished` with an MIT-manuscript note; corrected after primary-source verification confirmed it was distributed as a Dyana-2 deliverable, listed in Abusch 1997's reference list.)
- Fixed `abusch-1997.sources` from non-existent `Theories/Semantics/Tense/Abusch.lean` to the actual files (`TemporalDeRe.lean` + `Phenomena/TenseAspect/Studies/Abusch1997.lean`).
- Fixed `von-stechow-2009.sources` from non-existent `Theories/Semantics/Tense/VonStechow.lean` to `FeatureChecking.lean` + the (misnamed) `Abusch1997.lean` (whose actual content is von Stechow's relation transmission).

**`Theories/Semantics/Tense/TemporalDeRe.lean` trim** (M-P2): 189 LOC â†’ 176 LOC (smaller delete than the Klecha trim because the docstring expanded with attribution + faithfulness-gap notes). Same wrapper-alias pattern 0.230.450 deleted on the Klecha side, applied here:
- Deleted `def abuschiULC := upperLimitConstraint` (1-line same-arity alias) + `theorem abuschiULC_eq_ulc := Iff.rfl` (textbook "encoding conclusions as definitions" anti-pattern from CLAUDE.md). Zero external consumers (verified).
- Deleted `def relationTransmission := updateTemporal g evalIdx matrixFrame.eventTime` (just `updateTemporal` with reordered args) + 2 helper theorems (`relationTransmission_shifts_eval`, `relationTransmission_is_evalTime_update`) that were rebadges of `Core.VarAssignment.update_lookup_same` through the wrapper. Zero external consumers (verified).
- Deleted `theorem abusch_blocks_forward_shift` (1-line forward through `ulc_blocks_forward_shift` from `Tense/Basic.lean` â€” three layers of indirection over `not_lt.mpr`).
- Dropped unused `_hPast` parameter in `abusch_derives_shifted` (audit: parameter named for theoretical content the proof never uses; same anti-pattern rejected for Kennedy 2007 in commit `27942198`).
- Converted 3 surviving bare `simp` calls to `simp only` (per CLAUDE.md proof-style hierarchy).
- File docstring rewritten: presuppositional ULC attributed to `@cite{heim-1994-comments}`, not Abusch; explicit "Modal-layer ULC formulation" note flagging the value-level `â‰¤` reduction as a deferred faithfulness gap; "Intensional Isomorphism" added to limitations list (Abusch's *other* constraint, ruling out unrepresentable acquaintance relations â€” entirely absent from linglib).

**`Phenomena/TenseAspect/Studies/HeimKratzer1998.lean` cleanup** (M-P3): dropped dead `open Semantics.Tense.TemporalDeRe` at line 373 â€” the two `abusch_derives_*` theorems below didn't actually consume any TemporalDeRe substrate symbol (mirror of 0.230.450's HK1998 dead-open finding for Klecha-side `ModalTense` open). Reframed the section docstring: theorems verify Reichenbach-frame projections of the data-file frames; wiring through TemporalDeRe substrate would require reshaping `embeddedSickSimultaneous`/`embeddedSickShifted` (currently standalone `ReichenbachFrame â„¤` instances) as `embeddedFrame matrixSaid â€¦` applications. Deferred.

**ULC attribution corrections** (M-P4): linguistics audit (with Heim 1994 MS PDF evidence) found that `Theories/Semantics/Tense/Basic.lean آ§Upper Limit Constraint` and `Core/Modality/HistoricalAlternatives.lean آ§"Klecha 2016: ULC derived from history structure"` both attributed the *presuppositional T-node construal* to Abusch 1997. Per Abusch 1997 آ§7 (p. 25, primary source), Abusch states ULC informally: "the now of an epistemic alternative is an upper limit for the denotation of tenses". Per Abusch 1997 fn 20, Heim 1994 proposes the presuppositional construal, which Abusch endorses ("I find all of this convincing, and consequently accept Heim's presuppositional construal of the ULC"). Updated both docstrings to reflect the layered attribution: ULC stated by `@cite{abusch-1997}` آ§7; presuppositional construal due to `@cite{heim-1994-comments}`, endorsed by Abusch 1997 fn 20. Added explicit modal-layer faithfulness gap note: the value-level `â‰¤` reduction strips the modal-alternative quantification Abusch's formulation carries (the "now of an *epistemic alternative*" quantifies over doxastic alternatives). Initial draft attributed the original ULC to "Abusch 1988 MS p. 24"; corrected after primary-source verification â€” the 1988 paper is a different WCCFL paper, and "p. 24" in Heim 1994 refers to a manuscript draft of what became the 1997 publication, not the 1988 paper.

**آ§5c. Klecha â†” Abusch ULC predicate convergence** (M-P5): the headline theorem this audit thread was set up to land. Klecha 2016 آ§4.2 last paragraph explicitly characterizes his ULC derivation as "identical in spirit, if not in implementation, to Abusch (1997)". This commit makes the implementation-level equality kernel-checked:
```lean
theorem klecha_dox_iff_abusch_ulc {Time : Type*} [LinearOrder Time]
    (evalTime refTime : Time) :
    attitudeTemporalConstraint .doxastic evalTime refTime â†”
    upperLimitConstraint refTime evalTime :=
  Iff.rfl
```
Both sides reduce to `refTime â‰¤ evalTime` definitionally. The substantive content is in the *proof routes*: Klecha's path goes via `actualHistoryBase_time_actual` (`.2`-projection through situation-base membership; carries doxastic-alternative quantification at the substrate level via `WorldHistory W Time`); Abusch's path is the bare `abbrev` unfold (per Heim 1994 MS, the original 1988 MS formulation is presuppositional). The آ§5c docstring records the "spirit-vs-implementation" distinction Klecha names. Strict equivalence at the value level; **NOT strict at the modal layer** â€” Klecha's substrate carries doxastic alternatives via `WorldHistory`; Abusch's bare-`â‰¤` form has dropped them. Modal-layer `upperLimitConstraint` over `WorldHistory W Time` matching Abusch's original "now of an epistemic alternative" deferred for future work.

**What this commit explicitly does NOT do**, per the audit's "wide-but-deferred" recommendations:
- Rename `Phenomena/TenseAspect/Studies/Abusch1997.lean` â†’ `Schlenker2003.lean`. The misnaming is real (4-agent unanimous: file's content is ContextTower / Kaplan / Schlenker / von Stechow, with zero theorems anchored to anything Abusch-distinctive), but rename + content reorganization is a separate session.
- Lift `upperLimitConstraint` to a modal-layer formulation over `WorldHistory W Time` matching Abusch's original quantification over doxastic alternatives. The faithfulness gap is now flagged in 4 docstrings.
- Formalize the counterpart-relation isomorphism Abusch invokes in آ§12 for the double-access reading (the constraint that actual and belief worlds be temporally isomorphic, eliminating most of the 4 cells in the DAR diagram). Added to `TemporalDeRe.lean`'s limitations list. (Initial draft framed this as "Abusch's *Intensional Isomorphism* â€” second of a two-constraint pair"; corrected after primary-source verification â€” Abusch 1997 has ULC as the named main constraint, with the temporal-isomorphism counterpart-relation requirement as a downstream piece in آ§12, not a stand-alone second constraint.)
- Formalize Abusch's res-movement as an LF rewrite. The current `TemporalDeRe` structure captures only the *output* of res-movement (a back-shifted frame with a constraint tag), not the LF mechanism.
- Klecha-Ogihara contradiction theorem (cross-framework reconciler's other priority): substrate not in shape; deferred per audit's substrate-readiness assessment.

Audit attribution: 4-agent unanimous audit (mathlib-reviewer + linglib-integration-auditor + linguistics-domain-expert + cross-framework-reconciler), parallel to 0.230.450's Klecha-side audit. The mathlib reviewer flagged the wrapper aliases; the integration auditor flagged the dead `open` and the bib `sources` staleness; the linguistics expert (with Heim 1994 MS PDF evidence at semanticsarchive.net) flagged the presuppositional-ULC misattribution and the modal-layer faithfulness gap; the cross-framework reconciler classified the (Klecha, Abusch) pair as a hidden-agreement at the value level and named آ§5c as the priority theorem to land.

Build: full project (5393 jobs) green. 0.230.451

## [0.230.450] - 2026-04-27

### Klecha 2016 deep refactor â€” ULC derived from history substrate, layer violation fixed, mathlib-shape rename

A 4-agent deep audit (mathlib-reviewer + linglib-integration-auditor + linguistics-domain-expert + cross-framework-reconciler) of `Theories/Semantics/Tense/ModalTense.lean` flagged three structural issues: (1) the file's central architectural claim â€” "ULC derived from DOX's temporal character, not stipulated" (Klecha 2016 آ§4.2) â€” was unrealized: the proofs were `Iff.rfl` against a stipulated time-predicate, structurally indistinguishable from Abusch 1997's stipulated ULC; (2) a pre-existing layer violation in which `Core/Modality/HistoricalAlternatives.lean` imported `Theories/Semantics/Modality/TemporalConstraint.lean` (Core importing Theories, against the project layer discipline); (3) a pile of wrapper aliases doing no work (`embeddedRTConstraint` was a same-arity alias of `attitudeTemporalConstraint`, `modalEvalTimeShift` was `updateTemporal` with permuted args, the `combined_think_upper_limit` theorem carried an unused `_hShift` parameter, and 5 per-verb wrappers were 1-line renames of substrate lemmas).

This commit refactors the substrate so Klecha's derivation is kernel-checked, fixes the layer violation, trims the wrappers, and renames the file to mathlib shape.

**Substrate split** (fixing Core-imports-Theories): `Core/Modality/HistoricalAlternatives.lean` absorbs the framework-neutral history-time predicates that were stranded in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/TemporalConstraint.lean` â€” `isMaximalHistory`, `isActualHistory`, `isPastHistory`, `isFutureHistory`, `isProspectiveHistory` plus their 6 algebra theorems (complementarity, disjointness, inclusion). These are pure interval inequalities over `LE Time` / `LT Time` whose @cite{klecha-2016} attribution is for the terminology only (Klecha Definition 3); the mathematical content is just `â‰¤`, `<`, `>`, `â‰¥`. HA's situation-bases (`actualHistoryBase`, `futureHistoryBase`, `historicalBase`) are refactored to use the predicates by name, making the membership-implies-time-predicate connection true by construction (the bridge theorems become trivial `.2` projections). HA gains @cite{klecha-2016} and @cite{abusch-1997} in its header, plus a `## Klecha 2016: ULC derived from history structure` section explicitly attributing the substrate derivation. HA's import of TC is dropped (the layer violation), and HA gains explicit `import Mathlib.Data.Set.Basic` and `import Mathlib.Order.Defs.LinearOrder` (previously transitively inherited through TC).

**Theories/Semantics/Modality/TemporalConstraint.lean slimmed**: now imports HA upstream (correct layer order), drops the moved predicates, retains only the Klecha-namespace dispatch (`attitudeTemporalConstraint`, `doxConstrainsRT`, `cirConstrainsRT`, the small consequence lemmas, and the `Attitude` / `ModalFlavor` â†’ `ModalBaseKind` bridges). Adds two new theorems `attitudeTemporalConstraint_derived_doxastic` / `_derived_circumstantial` that make Klecha's central claim kernel-checked: `attitudeTemporalConstraint .doxastic s.time s'.time` follows by `.2` projection from `s' âˆˆ actualHistoryBase history s`. This is the formal contrast with Abusch's stipulated ULC â€” DOX-pronoun's lexical entry produces `actualHistoryBase` membership, and ULC is the time-component projection.

**Theories/Semantics/Tense/ModalTense.lean â†’ Theories/Semantics/Tense/Modality.lean** (mathlib-shape rename: `Topology/Algebra/Group.lean` convention applied â†’ "modality content as it bears on tense"; the old `ModalTense` portmanteau was non-mathlib and redundant with the `Tense/` parent directory). Trimmed 248 LOC â†’ 95 LOC by dropping wrappers per audit findings:
- `embeddedRTConstraint` (same-arity, same-arg-order alias of `attitudeTemporalConstraint`) â€” deleted (audit M1).
- `modalEvalTimeShift` (`updateTemporal` with permuted args) + `modalShift_gives_evalTime` (a Core lemma rebadged through the wrapper) â€” deleted (audit M2).
- `combined_think_upper_limit` (carrying unused `_hShift` parameter that was structurally provable from `evalTimeIndex_grounds_shift`) + `evalTimeIndex_grounds_shift` (literal rename of `evalTime_shifts_under_embedding` from `Core/Time/Tense.lean`) â€” deleted (audit M3 + S2).
- 5 per-verb wrappers (`think_blocks_future`, `think_permits_past`, `think_permits_simultaneous`, `hope_permits_future`, `hope_permits_past`) â€” all 1-line renames of substrate lemmas; deleted, consumers go through `dox_compatible_with_past`, `dox_incompatible_with_future`, `cir_compatible_with_future` directly (audit M1 cascade).
- `cir_past_impossible : آ¬(...)` renamed to `cir_past_iff_false : (...) â†” False` for matrix parallelism with the other three theorems (`dox_past_iff`, `dox_npst_iff`, `cir_npst_iff`, all `â†”`) (audit S1).
- Dropped dead `open Core.Time.Reichenbach` and redundant selective `open Core.Time.Tense (GramTense)` (audit S4).
- Docstring rewritten: the prior "What this file does NOT claim" disclaimer attributed eval-time-shifting to Abusch 2004 and Katz 2001 â€” the linguistics audit verified against the Klecha 2016 PDF that this is a misreading of آ§4.1 (Klecha's contrast is *constrain RT* vs *bind the open temporal argument*, not "shift EvalT"; Katz 2001 is the closer-to-shift target, Abusch 2004 is about non-finite-embedding orientation variation). Replaced with the correct constrain-vs-bind framing. Dropped @cite{abusch-2004} and @cite{katz-2001} (which didn't resolve in `references.bib` anyway, per linguistics audit M1). Dropped the `believe`/`pray` prose bullets (no Lean witness in the file, per audit M6). Qualified the "all of Table 1's predictions" claim (file's 4-cell matrix is DOX/CIR أ— PAST/NPST; Klecha's Table 1 is `have-to` أ— {past, present, future} أ— {epistemic, circumstantial} = 6 cells).

**Phenomena/TenseAspect/Studies/Klecha2016.lean updated**: 11 references to deleted/renamed symbols rewritten to substrate APIs (the 7 `embeddedRTConstraint` uses become `attitudeTemporalConstraint`; the 4 verb-flavored study theorems delegate to `dox_compatible_with_past`, `dox_incompatible_with_future`, `cir_compatible_with_future` directly; `cir_past_is_impossible` adapts to the renamed `cir_past_iff_false` via `.mp`). Added imports for `Core.Modality.HistoricalAlternatives` and the new `Tense.Modality` path. New آ§5b "ULC derivation through the situation-base substrate" demonstrates the kernel-checked derivation at â„¤ via two new theorems `ulc_via_history_base` and `future_via_history_base` â€” both delegate to `attitudeTemporalConstraint_derived_doxastic` / `_circumstantial`. The existing آ§5 `ulc_derived_from_dox : Iff.rfl` is kept (correct at the surface level); آ§5b shows the deeper substrate path that traces back to `actualHistoryBase` membership per Klecha eq 35a + Definition 3vi.

**HeimKratzer1998.lean cleanup**: Removed the dead `open Semantics.Tense.ModalTense` at line 438 (audit integration آ§5: theorem `klecha_derives_modalPast` doesn't use any symbol from that namespace; the open would have been broken by the rename anyway). Updated import to `Tense.Modality`.

**Stale `.lake` artifacts removed**: `.lake/build/ir/Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Tense/Klecha.{c,c.hash,setup.json}` (from a previous file rename pre-dating this work, flagged by the integration audit) and `.lake/build/ir/Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Tense/ModalTense.{c,c.hash,setup.json}` (from this rename).

What this commit explicitly does NOT do, deferred for future work: aspect (PRV/be-ing per Klecha eq 26) is not formalized; de se centered histories âں¨y, u, iâں© (Klecha آ§3.4 + Appendix B) are not formalized; SOT agreement as a transformation is not formalized; the cross-framework auditor's top-priority bridges (Klecha vs Ogihara contradiction theorem, Klecha vs Sharvit hidden agreement, Klecha vs Hacquard typed bridge, Klecha vs Condoravdi explicit refutation) remain as documented gaps for separate sessions; the bib hygiene fix for `abusch-2004` / `katz-2001` is rolled into the docstring rewrite (those references are dropped; bib entries can be added when a real consumer needs them).

Build: full project (1798 jobs) green. 0.230.450

### Minimalism â†’ Minimalist directory rename + Tier A audit cleanup

Following a 4-agent deep audit of `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/` (65 files, 21,645 LOC), this commit lands the user-approved Tier A hygiene fixes plus a preference-driven directory rename.

**Directory + namespace rename: `Minimalism` â†’ `Minimalist`** (mathlib-idiomatic adjectival naming; "Minimalist syntax" reads better than "Minimalism syntax").

3 directories renamed via `git mv`: `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/` â†’ `â€¦/Minimalist/`, `Theories/Interfaces/SyntaxPhonology/Minimalism/` â†’ `â€¦/Minimalist/`, `Theories/Interfaces/SyntaxSemantics/Minimalism/` â†’ `â€¦/Minimalist/`.

Identifier-level replacements across all .lean files: `import â€¦Minimalism` paths â†’ `â€¦Minimalist`; `namespace Minimalism` / `end Minimalism` / `open Minimalism` â†’ `Minimalist` (single + multi-namespace form); `Minimalism.X` qualified references (upper + lowercase + `_root_.`) â†’ `Minimalist.X`. Plus 87 `references.bib` `sources` paths and 2 docstring backtick mentions of `` `namespace Minimalism` ``. **Prose mentions of "Minimalism" as the framework name are LEFT alone** (e.g., "Minimalism, HPSG, CCG, DG" in cross-framework comparison docstrings) â€” appropriate since "Minimalism" is the noun-form of the framework name. ~205 files touched in total.

**Quotient.lean deletion** (mathlib-reviewer audit recommendation): the 195-LOC file existed solely to produce one arithmetic fact (`q.leafCount = T.leafCount - v.leafCount + 1`) for `Properties/NoComplexityLoss.lean`'s `quotient_deg_lt` and `sideward_2b_violates_ncl` theorems. Per mathlib idiom, refactored those two theorems to take the leafCount equality as a hypothesis directly: `(hq : q.leafCount = T.leafCount - v.leafCount + 1)` instead of `(hq : quotientTree T v = some q)`. Strictly more general â€” any quotient operation respecting the leaf-count identity discharges the hypothesis (including the new `Hopf/AdmissibleCut.lean` `CutShape.remainder` for the single-cut case). Eliminates the `quotientLeafToken` fake-LIToken soundness gap as a side-effect.

**Tier A audit fixes:**
- 2 wrong namespace roots fixed (`Syntax.Minimalist.X` â†’ `Minimalist.X` in `MinimalPronoun.lean`, `LongDistanceAgree.lean`); downstream consumers updated (Allotey2021, Ostrove2026, Landau2015).
- 9 docstring placement fixes: `/-` blocks at top-of-file (before imports, treated as regular comments by Lean) â†’ `/-! ... -/` blocks AFTER imports (proper Lean module docstrings), per CLAUDE.md `Lean Conventions`. Files: `Agree`, `Coreference`, `Inversion`, `RelativeClauses`, `SpeechActs`, `Scope`, `Counting`, `Algebra`, `Basic`. Done via Python script.
- 2 bare `simp` â†’ `simp only [...]` in `Hopf/Comul.lean:178`, `Properties/MinimalSearch.lean:39`. Per CLAUDE.md "Prefer `simp only [lemma1, lemma2]` over bare `simp`".
- 1 broken citation: `kayne-1993` was cited in `Copula.lean` but missing from `references.bib`. Added the article entry (Kayne 1993, *Toward a Modular Theory of Auxiliary Selection*, Studia Linguistica 47:3-31, with verified DOI `10.1111/j.1467-9582.1993.tb00837.x`).
- 13 files / 97 instances of `native_decide` â†’ `decide` mass-replaced (per CLAUDE.md "Prefer structural proofs over `native_decide`"). The largest offender was `CyclicAgree.lean` with 39 `native_decide` calls, followed by `Ellipsis/DeletionDomain.lean` (13) and `VerbalDecomposition.lean` (11).
- **Cascade-deletion of 7 `decide`-failing theorems**: `decide` did not reduce in the kernel for theorems involving recursive `label` / `classifyExternalMerge` evaluations (even with bumped `maxHeartbeats 800000`). Per user's explicit preference ("we can just delete"), removed the affected theorems and replaced with prose preserving the substantive Mأ¼ller arguments. Affected: `Labeling.lean`'s `freeRel_labelCat_gives_V`, `freeRel_labeling_conflict`, `coord_neither_selects`, `coord_labelCat_artifact`; `CombinationSchemata.lean`'s 3 sanity-check examples + `monovalent_classified_as_complement`; `Mueller2013.lean`'s `free_relative_labeling_conflict`, `coordination_labeling_failure`, `monovalent_verb_serialization_problem` (which composed the lower-level deletions).

**Earlier audit findings also landed** (post-Hopf rebuild, pre-rename):
- Misattributed "very mild" gloss in `Hopf/Defs.lean` corrected (M-C-B p. 25's phrase refers to set-vs-multiset clash, NOT to FreeMagma non-commutativity).
- `Lemma 1.5.1` citation was wrong (no such lemma â€” `Proposition 1.5.1` exists on p. 60 but is about cost functions). Replaced with `Definition 1.3.4 (p. 42)` â€” the correct M-C-B reference for "Merge uses خ”^d" â€” across `Hopf/AdmissibleCut.lean` and `Hopf/Comul.lean`.
- `mergeOp_im_matches_Step` `True`-stub was structurally wrong (per M-C-B Prop 1.4.2, Internal Merge is the COMPOSITION `M_{T/خ²,خ²} âˆک M_{خ²,1}`, not a single `mergeOp`). Replaced with documented gap explaining the composition pattern.
- False `cutForest_injective` claim removed: `cutForest` is genuinely NOT injective for trees with shared substructure (e.g., `T = .node X X` makes `bothRecurse cl cr` and `bothRecurse cr cl` produce the same multiset), and that's correct M-C-B behavior because the coproduct sums over **positional** cuts (`CutShape T`), not over value-deduplicated multisets. Replaced with an explicit docstring explaining the position-vs-value semantics.
- `DecoratedTree.leafCount` added to `Hopf/Defs.lean` with simp lemmas + `leafCount_pos` (genuinely useful infrastructure that emerged during the abandoned Quotient refactor).

**Build status:** 5393 jobs green. One pre-existing `sorry` in `Properties/BinaryOptimality.lean` (`nary_overgeneration`, blocked by mathlib-missing Jordan curve theorem â€” flagged in linguistics audit). 

## [0.230.448] - 2026-04-27

### Pesetsky 2013: deep Minimalist refactor â€” case stack as structural property of SyntacticObject (no side-map)

Headline structural change after a 3-round 4-agent audit cycle plus PDF verification. An earlier draft introduced `PesAssignment : List (LIToken أ— List POSCat)` as a paper-local side-map for case stacks. **That was reinvention.** The deeper grounding: case stacks are a *derived structural property* of the `SyntacticObject` itself, computed by recursion over the tree's `.node head body` constructors. **No external state. No side-map. No `Finsupp`** (mathlib's standard sparse-map type doesn't fit because `List POSCat` lacks a `Zero` instance, and the wrapper-type workaround was more cognitive load than payoff). The case stack at a leaf is `caseStackAt : SyntacticObject â†’ LIToken â†’ List POSCat` â€” pure structural recursion using only existing Minimalist substrate.

**Substrate consumed.** New imports: `Linglib.Theories.Syntax.Minimalist.Basic` (replaces the prior file's bespoke `PesTree`), `Linglib.Core.Case.Order` (for Caha containment-rank cross-framework agreement), `Linglib.Theories.Morphology.DM.Categorizer` (for the cross-categorial alignment with DM categorizers). Now using `Cat` (line 44 â€” `.N/.D/.V/.P` among 25+ constructors), `SyntacticObject := FreeMagma LIToken` (line 152), `merge` (line 206), `LIToken` (line 127, with `DecidableEq`), `mkLeaf : Cat â†’ SelStack â†’ Nat â†’ SyntacticObject` (line 219), `mkLeafPhon` (line 223), `LexicalItem.simple` (line 100), `Morphology.DM.Categorizer.{n, v, toCategory}` (Categorizer.lean:69-79). Drops the prior file's imports of `Theories.Interfaces.Morphosyntax.CaseContainment` and `Fragments.Slavic.Russian.Gender` (no longer needed after Tier C drops below).

**آ§1 POSCat as inductive with Cat injection.** Inductive `POSCat | N | D | V | P` (kept inductive over subtype-of-Cat for pattern-matching ergonomics â€” subtype would require unreachable totality branches for Cat's 25+ other constructors at every match site). New `POSCat.toCat : POSCat â†’ Cat` injection + `toCat_injective` proof witnesses the structural embedding into Minimalism. New `POSCat.ofCat? : Cat â†’ Option POSCat` partial inverse (the `none` cases are exactly the non-FA-bearing functional heads â€” DM categorizers `.n/.v/.a`, T, Voice, Place, Path, etc.) plus `ofCat?_toCat` round-trip. Adds `Fintype POSCat` derivation. The `.cases : POSCat â†’ Finset Core.Case` corrected to drop `.abl` from `.P` (Russian doesn't have a productive ablative; the productive obliques are DAT/INS/LOC matching `Fragments.Slavic.Russian.Case.caseInventory`); previous `.abl` inclusion was an audit-flagged round-2 leftover.

**آ§2 Case stack as structural recursion (the deep refactor).** Drops `PesAssignment` entirely â€” the paper-local side-map disappears. Replaced by `containsLeaf : SyntacticObject â†’ LIToken â†’ Bool` (decidable membership without materializing the linearization), `caseStackAt : SyntacticObject â†’ LIToken â†’ List POSCat` (structural recursion: leaf base case returns `[POSCat.N]` if the queried leaf is N-categorized â€” Primeval Genitive automatic; node case checks which side the leaf is in and prepends the head's POSCat to the body-side recursive result), and `surfaceCase : SyntacticObject â†’ LIToken â†’ Option Core.Case` (Russian One-Suffix Rule via `(caseStackAt so tok).head?.map POSCat.canonicalExemplar`).

**The two side-conditions of FA eq. (76) become structural facts** rather than passed parameters: (1) "خ± has satisfied its complementation requirements" â€” implicit (treated as always satisfied; modeling the gate would require a Bool-tagged Step extension or filtering the derivation history; flagged as out-of-scope); (2) "خ± is a designated feature assigner for خ²" â€” IS the `POSCat.ofCat? head.outerCat` test. Only `.N/.D/.V/.P` heads contribute case strata; T, Voice, n, v, a, etc. all fall to the `none` branch automatically. **No `faMergeStep` parameter to pass POSCat externally.**

**آ§3 noDeletionSpellOut** (renamed from `lardilSpellOut`). Honest about the limit: identity-on-stacks is "no deletion at all", not Lardil itself â€” Lardil has *partial* deletion per Ch 3 آ§3.2 (FUT only stacks outside GEN, not outside ACC). A faithful Lardil model is left for a follow-on `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Richards2013.lean`.

**آ§4 primevalGenitive simplification.** Drops the `LIToken أ— PesAssignment` tuple return â€” now just `String â†’ Nat â†’ SyntacticObject` returning a bare `mkLeafPhon .N [] w id` leaf. The `[POSCat.N]` initial stack is automatic from `caseStackAt`'s base case. Prevents the round-2 mathlib-reviewer's flagged `let (tok, asgn) := primevalGenitive w id` destructuring pattern entirely â€” there's no tuple to destructure.

**آ§6 NOM/ACC theorems on real Merge derivations.** `subject_surface_nom`, `object_surface_acc_unrestricted`, `subject_object_same_inner` rewritten as claims about `surfaceCase (merge dHead (.leaf nounTok)) nounTok`. Distinctness hypotheses (`idN â‰  idD` etc.) dropped entirely â€” LIToken `item` fields differ structurally between noun and head (different cats), so simp closes the inequalities even when ids coincide harmlessly. `subject_surface_nom` docstring corrected per direct PDF verification (book pp. 71-74): D *does* assign DNOM (p. 72: "I have never needed to assume that nominative morphology is assigned to the elements of DP by anything other than D itself"); the "default" framing (eq. 87 p. 73) is Schأ¼tzean â€” about T not overwriting D's prior assignment, NOT about D not assigning. `object_surface_acc_unrestricted` framing made honest about the assignment-vs-realization split: eq. (77) gates whether FA *applies*, eq. (80) gates whether VACC is *realized*; the file's `_unrestricted` version models neither layer.

**آ§7 cross-framework bridges.** New `POSCat.image_in_cat : Set.range POSCat.toCat = ({Cat.N, Cat.D, Cat.V, Cat.P} : Set Cat)` â€” the structural alignment with Minimalism stated as a Set-equality theorem. New `pesetsky_caha_compatible_prefix : pesetskyCore.filter (containmentRank آ· |>.isSome) = {.nom, .acc, .gen, .dat, .loc} := by decide` â€” **non-trivial cross-framework agreement** with @cite{caha-2009}: the two frameworks identify the same 5-case core, with Pesetsky additionally putting INST in `pesetskyCore` (off Caha's hierarchy). New `pesetskyCore_eq_biUnion_image (c : Core.Case) : c âˆˆ pesetskyCore â†” âˆƒ p : POSCat, c âˆˆ p.cases` records the literal-vs-biUnion-image equivalence as an iff theorem. **DM Categorizer bridges**: `primevalN_via_dm_categorizer : POSCat.N.toCat = Morphology.DM.Categorizer.toCategory .n := rfl` and `posCatV_via_dm_categorizer : POSCat.V.toCat = Morphology.DM.Categorizer.toCategory .v := rfl` â€” the structural alignment between Pesetsky's case-bearing categories and DM's lexical categorizers (@cite{harley-2014}, @cite{marantz-1997}). The Primeval Genitive Conjecture (eq. (6) p. 9) is the case-bearing reflection of root-level n-categorization. P and D are not DM categorizers (they are functional heads), so no analog applies for those POS categories.

**Tier C cleanups that fell out of the refactor.** Drops `PesAssignment` entirely. Drops `mutual` block + `assignDownList` + `PesTree.stack` (replaced by structural recursion). Drops `oneSuffix : List POSCat â†’ Option POSCat` and its two reduction lemmas (`List.head?` is the canonical mathlib operation). Drops `vraؤچ_morphological_class_unchanged` â€” `Phenomena/Gender/Studies/Kramer2020.lean:983-987` already proves the equivalent claim. Drops `caha_ranks_for_pesetsky_core_n_d_v` (3-rfl faأ§ade â€” replaced by the genuine `pesetsky_caha_compatible_prefix` agreement theorem). Replaces `RusNumClass.role_partitions_classes` 3-rfl conjunction with two iff lemmas `role_eq_nbrHead_iff` and `role_eq_quantHead_iff`.

**Build.** Pesetsky2013.lean's 894-job transitive closure passes clean (zero errors, zero warnings, zero sorries); `touch` + rebuild verified. File grew from 422 LOC (pre-refactor) â†’ 565 LOC â€” almost entirely module + per-section docstring expansion (the deep-refactor architecture commentary is substantial). Code-only LOC dropped substantially: the previous version's `PesTree`+`mutual` block, `oneSuffix` reduction lemmas, and three-rfl faأ§ade theorems are gone, replaced by ~25 LOC of structural recursion (`containsLeaf` + `caseStackAt`). File remains a leaf â€” no downstream consumers. Bibliography validator confirms cite keys all resolve.

## [0.230.447] - 2026-04-27

### M-C-B Hopf substrate: legacy deletion + mathlib-shape complete (Sessions 5+6+7)

Continuation of the multi-session sprint to rebuild the Connes-Kreimer-style bialgebra of binary nonplanar rooted decorated forests per @cite{marcolli-chomsky-berwick-2025} آ§1.2. This batch executes the user-approved mathlib-shape-first ordering (7 â†’ 8 â†’ 1 â†’ 2 â†’ 3 â†’ 4 â†’ 5+6) and lands the full legacy cleanup.

**(7) Sub-namespace rename `Minimalism â†’ Minimalism.Hopf`.** Resolves name conflict between new substrate (was `Minimalism.Hc`, `Minimalism.Forest`) and legacy (also `Minimalism.Hc`, `Minimalism.Forest` â€” different types, same name, only build-clean by accident). New substrate now in `Minimalism.Hopf` namespace; legacy in `Minimalism` (until deleted in 5+6).

**(8) Generic parameterization for upstream PR.** `SyntacticObjectH` (LIToken-monomorphic) â†’ `DecoratedTree (خ± : Type)` parameterized over leaf type. `Forest`/`Hc` parameterized over coefficient ring R AND leaf type خ±: `Forest خ± := Multiset (DecoratedTree خ±)`, `Hc R خ± := AddMonoidAlgebra R (Forest خ±)`. CutShape, cutForest, remainder, remainderDeletion, comulTree, comulForest, comulAlgHom, counit, comulTreeDel, comulForestDel, comulDelAlgHom â€” all parameterized. Linglib-specific aliases: `SyntacticObjectH := DecoratedTree LIToken`. This is the mathlib-PR shape â€” abstract types live in `Minimalism.Hopf` for now and would migrate to `Mathlib.Combinatorics.HopfAlgebra.ConnesKreimer.DecoratedForest` on upstream PR. **Universe polymorphism deferred** (kept as `Type`, not `Type*`) due to indexed-inductive universe-inference fights; mathlib upstream will need it but doesn't block the structural goal.

**(1) خ”^d (deletion coproduct).** Implemented per M-C-B Def 1.2.5 + 1.2.8(د‰=d). New `remainderDeletion : CutShape T â†’ Option (DecoratedTree خ±)` in AdmissibleCut.lean â€” Option because cutting both children of a node makes the parent collapse (root component disappears, hence None). Edge contraction handled in the recursive case: `bothRecurse cl cr` â†’ if one branch collapses to None, the other becomes the result; if both collapse, parent collapses. Faithful to M-C-B Def 1.2.5's "unique maximal binary rooted tree obtainable via contraction." New `comulTreeDel`, `comulForestDel`, `comulDelMonoidHom`, `comulDelAlgHom` in Comul.lean. `comulDelAlgHom : Hc R خ± â†’â‚گ[R] Hc R خ± âٹ—[R] Hc R خ±` is the algebra hom for خ”^d (multiplicative on forests; strict coassoc fails per Lemma 1.2.12 â€” but algebra-hom-ness is preserved).

**(2) خ´_{S,S'} matching + B grafting + Merge operator.** New file `Hopf/Merge.lean`. Per M-C-B Def 1.3.1 + 1.3.2 + 1.3.4: `gammaMatch S S' : Hc R خ± â†’â‚—[R] Hc R خ±` projects onto the {S, S'} basis element via `Finsupp.lsingle âˆک Finsupp.lapply`. `deltaMatch S S' = TensorProduct.map (gammaMatch S S') LinearMap.id` lifts to the left tensor channel. `graftBinaryAt S S'` replaces {S, S'} basis with .node S S' (Lemma 1.3.3 for binary Merge). `mergeOp S S' = âٹ” âˆک (B âٹ— id) âˆک خ´_{S,S'} âˆک خ”^d` â€” the linguistic Merge operator as a linear endomorphism of `Hc R خ±`. Multiplication-on-tensor lifted via `Algebra.TensorProduct.lmul'` from `Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Maps`.

**(3) MergeAction.lean rewrite.** Replaced legacy 335-LOC version (Workspace=List, MonoidAlgebra=FreeMonoid SO substrate) with a clean 130-LOC version on the new substrate. Bridge theorems `mergeOp_emR_matches_Step`, `mergeOp_emL_matches_Step` connecting `mergeOp` (Hopf-side, linear endomorphism of `Hc â„¤ LIToken`) to `Step.apply` (linguistic-side, concrete tree manipulation in `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Derivation.lean`). **Bridge proofs are sorry'd** with detailed proof-strategy docstrings (substantial linear-algebra-on-Finsupp work; deferred to a focused future session). The `mergeOp` definition is fully complete and usable; only the bridge-to-`Step.apply` proofs are pending. No external consumers of bridge theorems, so safe to ship as stub.

**(4) NoComplexityLoss.lean migration; Quotient.lean extraction.** Eliminated NoComplexityLoss.lean's dependency on legacy Hopf/Coproduct.lean. Extracted single-cut `quotientTree` + `quotientLeafToken` + `quotientTree_leafCount` machinery (~200 LOC of definitions and proofs) from legacy Coproduct.lean to new file `Linglib/Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Quotient.lean`. Migrated `em_contains_merge` + `im_contains_merge` workspace theorems from legacy Coproduct.lean to `Counting.lean` (where `emWorkspace`/`imWorkspace` are defined). NoComplexityLoss.lean now imports Counting + Quotient; no Hopf dependency.

**(5)+(6) Linglib.lean cleanup + `git rm` legacy.** Removed legacy `Hopf/Coproduct.lean` (532 LOC) and `Hopf/HopfAlgebra.lean` (864 LOC) imports from Linglib.lean; added new `Hopf/Merge.lean` import. Deleted both legacy files. Full project build passes 5394 jobs.

**Final Hopf area shape (5 files, all clean substrate):**
- `Hopf/Defs.lean` â€” `DecoratedTree خ±` + `Forest خ±` + `Hc R خ±` + `Mul`/`recOnMul`/`DecidableEq`/`Repr` instances + linglib alias `SyntacticObjectH := DecoratedTree LIToken` + `SyntacticObject.toH` bridge
- `Hopf/AdmissibleCut.lean` â€” `CutShape T` (5-constructor enum) + `cutForest` + `remainder` (with-trace) + `remainderDeletion` (with-rebinarization) + `Fintype` instance
- `Hopf/Comul.lean` â€” خ”^c (`comulAlgHom`) + خ”^d (`comulDelAlgHom`) + `counit`, all as algebra homs `Hc R خ± â†’â‚گ[R] _`
- `Hopf/Merge.lean` â€” `gammaMatch` + `deltaMatch` + `graftBinaryAt` + `mergeOp` per M-C-B Def 1.3.4
- `Hopf/MergeAction.lean` â€” bridge theorem statements (sorry'd) connecting `mergeOp` to `Step.apply`

**What remains for the full mathlib-PR readiness:** universe polymorphism (Type â†’ Type*); `Bialgebra Hc` typeclass instance (needs coassoc proof for خ”^c â€” Lemma 1.2.10, multi-week); Hopf algebra structure via the (1âˆ’خ±) quotient + antipode; promote `multiset_add_eq_zero_iff` upstream. None of these block the current state from being usable; they're the next sprint's targets.

## [0.230.446] - 2026-04-27

### Pesetsky 2013 study file: empirical correctness + faأ§ade cleanup (4-agent audit Tier A+B+C)

Four-agent audit (mathlib-reviewer + linglib-integration-auditor + linguistics-domain-expert + cross-framework-reconciler) of `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013.lean` (370 LOC) against the Pesetsky 2013 PDF (LI Monograph 66) found two empirical errors and one faأ§ade. The integration-auditor confirmed clean layer discipline, intentional `POSCat` paper-localization (CHANGELOG:13932), and correct `containmentRank` import; the mathlib-reviewer flagged `mutual` block, `toCase_injective` naming, missing `DecidableEq`, and a battery of `rfl`-restatement theorems; the cross-framework-reconciler catalogued silent divergences with sibling Case study files (Marantz 1991, Baker & Vinokurova 2010) and `Theories/Syntax/Case/{CaseFilter,Dependent,Licensing}.lean` (deferred Tier D).

**Tier A â€” empirical errors (the file said X, Pesetsky says آ¬X).** (1) `numeralCaseAssignment .paucal = .N` mis-located the source of GEN morphology on *dva stola*. Pesetsky Ch 6 eq. (61) p. 54-55: paucals are NBR base-merged inside NP with a numberless N; they **do not assign anything** â€” the noun's GEN is the *primeval* NGEN already present. The `paucal_stack_double_N` theorem `[.N, .N]` was an artifact of the file's model, not a Pesetsky prediction. Fix: drop `numeralCaseAssignment` + `applyNumeral` + the four false surface theorems entirely; replace with `RusNumClass.role : RusNumClass â†’ NumeralRole` (paucal=`nbrHead`, higher/quant=`quantHead`) faithful to Pesetsky's structural-position story; preserve numeral lexical entries with TODO note for promotion to a future `Fragments/Slavic/Russian/Numerals.lean`; surface-GEN prediction recorded in آ§5 docstring pointing back to `primeval_noun_surface_gen` from آ§4 (no separate numeral-indexed theorem needed because the file's apparatus has no way for the numeral to influence the head noun's stack). (2) `POSCat.P â†’ .dat` collapsed Pesetsky's most carefully-hedged distinction (p. 7: "only the distinctions among these oblique cases in Russian will fail to correspond to a traditional part-of-speech distinction" â€” P_OBL canonical, P_DAT one exemplar). Fix: add `POSCat.cases : POSCat â†’ Finset Core.Case` returning the full P-OBL series `{.dat, .inst, .loc, .abl}` for `.P`; preserve `POSCat.canonicalExemplar` (renamed from `toCase`) for the canonical-exemplar single-case projection.

**Tier B â€” faأ§ades and docstring honesty.** (1) `caha_containment_inverts_pesetsky` flagged independently by all three substantive agents: the proof was three `rfl`s on `containmentRank` constants with the "consistent under inversion" claim recorded in the docstring as editorial synthesis. Linguistics-domain-expert verified Pesetsky 2013 does **not** cite Caha 2009; per CLAUDE.md "no bridges" rule, this is original synthesis under cover of formalization. Fix: rename `caha_ranks_for_pesetsky_core_n_d_v` and drop the inversion claim from the docstring (the bare ranks remain available for inspection, with explicit disclaimer that the inversion question is taken up by neither author). (2) Wrong chapter attribution in "Out of scope": "Spell-Out / phase locality on adnominal case (Ch 8)" â€” adnominals are Ch 8 but the locality restrictions are آ§8.2-8.3, and آ§8.5 has the Loose-End "Prepositions That Appear to Assign NGEN" section that makes the dative-only collapse worse. Fix: tighten descriptive prose, add the آ§8.5 reference. (3) Missing citations: `marantz-1997` added (root + categorizer is the basis for "primeval genitive"). Pre-existing `@cite{schuetze-2001}` removed (no entry in `references.bib`; the Case Filter dissolution claim it supported was editorial per linguistics-domain-expert). Skipped `vergnaud-2006`, `emonds-1987`, `richards-2007` to avoid hallucinated bib entries (per CLAUDE.md anti-hallucination rule). (4) "Born genitive" â†’ "Primeval Genitive" terminology fix per Pesetsky eq. (6) p. 9: `bornGenitive â†’ primevalGenitive`, theorem `bare_noun_surface_gen â†’ primeval_noun_surface_gen`. The docstring now cites @cite{marantz-1997} for the categorizing-head architecture and notes the file's leaf-stack approximation (deriving from a real `Root â†’ CategorizedNoun` morphism flagged for future refinement).

**Tier C â€” code-quality (mathlib-reviewer).** (1) `POSCat.toCase_injective` was a 6-clause conjunction proved by `decide`; misnamed. Replaced with `POSCat.canonicalExemplar_injective : Function.Injective POSCat.canonicalExemplar` proved structurally (`intro a b h; cases a <;> cases b <;> first | rfl | cases h`). (2) Added `DecidableEq` derivation to `RusNumeral`. (3) Dropped unused `Inhabited POSCat` derivation. (4) Imports reordered alphabetically (Core < Fragments < Theories). (5) `lardil_russian_divergence` converted from bare `simp` to `simp only` with explicit lemma list per CLAUDE.md proof-style discipline. (6) `pesetskyCore` redefined as explicit `Finset` literal `{.gen, .nom, .acc, .dat, .inst, .loc, .abl}` (was `[POSCat.D, .V, .N, .P].map POSCat.toCase`) â€” Multiset reduction via `Quot.lift` blocked `decide` on `biUnion`-based formulation; new `pesetskyCore_eq_image` theorem proves `rfl`-equivalence to the union form (`POSCat.cases .N âˆھ ... âˆھ POSCat.cases .P`). (7) `inst_loc_outside_pesetsky` flipped to `russian_obliques_in_pesetsky_core` (the previous claim was wrong once the P-OBL series became the actual image).

**Mutual block decision (revised against mathlib-reviewer recommendation).** Mathlib-reviewer suggested replacing `mutual` with `kids.attach.map`. Tried; broke 12 `rfl` proofs in آ§4-آ§6 because `attach.map` compiles to well-founded recursion that doesn't reduce definitionally on `.leaf`. Reverted to `mutual` with a `/-! ... -/` block-docstring explaining the necessity (`PesTree` recurses through `List`; `rfl` proofs require `assignDown` to reduce on the leaf branch). Mathlib uses `mutual` for exactly this nested-list-recursion pattern when definitional reduction matters.

**`POSCat` localization tension (per cross-framework-reconciler).** Reconciler argued `POSCat` duplicates `Core/Lexical/UD.lean::UPOS` (which has NOUN/DET/VERB/ADP) and proposed promoting to `def UPOS.toPesetskyCase : UPOS â†’ Option Core.Case`. Integration-auditor cited CHANGELOG:13932 documenting an intentional prior decision to keep `POSCat` paper-local. Read: paper-locality preserved on the grounds that Pesetsky's POSCat is a *restricted* taxonomy with case-bearing semantics (4 categories that double as case strata, not a 17-tag universal POS inventory). Conflating loses the restriction; the docstring on `POSCat` and `POSCat.cases` is now explicit about the paper-local scope and the multi-case oblique class.

**Deferred to Tier D (separate sessions).** (1) Refutation theorems against `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Marantz1991.lean` (configurational ACC) and `BakerVinokurova2010.lean` (GEN-by-D-Agree) and `Theories/Syntax/Case/CaseFilter.lean` â€” concrete cross-framework disagreements that the file gestures at in prose. (2) `PesTree` â†’ `Core.Tree POSCat (String أ— List POSCat)` refactor per layered-grounding discipline. (3) `Theories/Morphology/CaseStacking.lean` substrate (`assigner : C â†’ ...` + `spellOut : List C â†’ List C`, with Russian = `head?.toList`, Lardil = `id`) â€” currently `lardilSpellOut := id` is a placeholder waiting on a missing `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Richards2013.lean` to consume it. (4) Ch 4-5 Number Mismatch puzzle (the original motivation of the monograph: *dva* triggers SG on noun but PL on demonstratives/adjectives) and the Lebanese Arabic # parallel (Ch 5.3) â€” separate study file to do justice to the structural derivation.

**Build.** Pesetsky2013.lean's 932-job transitive closure passes; full `lake build` (5396 jobs) passes â€” file is a leaf with no downstream consumers (`grep -rln "Phenomena.Case.Studies.Pesetsky2013"` returns only the file itself + the `Linglib.lean` import). Bibliography validator confirms all four `@cite{...}` keys (`pesetsky-2013`, `marantz-1997`, `caha-2009`, `richards-2013`) resolve.

## [0.230.445] - 2026-04-26

### Delete `Phenomena/Persuasion/Studies/CumminsFranke2021.lean`

Four-agent audit (mathlib-reviewer + linglib-integration-auditor + linguistics-domain-expert + cross-framework-reconciler) of the C&F 2021 study file (305 LOC) found a coherent set of issues whose root is a **Layered Grounding violation**: the file imported `moreThanMeaning` from `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic` and proved three theorems linking it to `atLeastMeaning`, then never consumed either â€” آ§2-3 stipulated raw â„ڑ probability literals (`p_mt100_givenG := 1`, `p_mt110_givenNotG := 1/12`, ...) whose derivations lived only in docstring prose. Module-docstring page citations were wrong (Eq. 17 is on p. 6, not p. 7; the paper has no آ§4/آ§5 numbering). Two REF data rows had factual errors against the paper: KCL recorded `actualRank=7` with `rankingMeasure="Power"` but @cite{cummins-franke-2021} p. 12 says KCL is 6th on power and 7th on quality; Essex's measure annotation conflated raw intensity rank (22) with the strategic "top 20 universities" interpretation (20). The آ§"Enrichment Width" function invented a `roundnessGrade â†’ Nat` mapping (20/15/10/4) that appears nowhere in C&F, while parallel infrastructure (`Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Precision.haloWidth`) already exists in linglib and is consumed by the same author's `Phenomena/Numerals/Studies/Cummins2015.round_wider_halo`. The paper's third hypothesis (H3 â€” "Avoid Argumentatively Unhelpful Statements"), the *disconfirmed* one that drives the General Discussion (p. 14-15), was entirely absent. Ten `native_decide` invocations should have been `decide` per CLAUDE.md.

**Why delete rather than overhaul.** A planned full rewrite (substrate `condProbUniform`/`bayesFactorUniform` + Finset-based derivation of the eight stipulated probabilities + H3 addition + data corrections) ran into a kernel-arithmetic wall: â„ڑ multiplication / division through `Rat.mul`/`Rat.inv` doesn't reduce structurally via `decide` even after `unfold`, forcing either `native_decide` (the very thing being eliminated) or `mkRat`-on-both-sides bookkeeping that becomes a wall of arithmetic over a 200-element discrete-uniform measure space â€” recomputing what C&F derives in two lines of prose. The paper has no theorem-shaped content beyond its definitions (Eq. 17, 25, 27, 28); the worked examples and REF case study are paper replication, not mathematical depth. With `grep -rln "CumminsFranke2021"` showing zero downstream consumers (only the file itself + its `Linglib.lean` import), and the substrate (`Theories/Pragmatics/RSA/ArgumentativeStrength.lean`) already standing on its own with consumers in `MacuchSilvaEtAl2024.lean`, `BarnettEtAl2022.lean`, `RSA/Speaker/CombinedUtility.lean`, and `RSA/Speaker/Noncooperative.lean`, the C&F study file added no load-bearing content â€” only a paper-replication marker that was wrong in three places and dead in five more.

**The natural substantive follow-up** is `Warstadt, Agha, Franke 2026` "Quantifying Epistemic Relevance" (Open Mind 10:502-546, DOI 10.1162/OPMI.a.332), which explicitly cites @cite{cummins-franke-2021} in its Bayes-factor lineage (p. 507) and runs a horse-race between five families of relevance measures â€” finding that **Bayes factor utility wins** as a predictor of human relevance judgments. WAF 2026 is the file that would justify substrate work on argStr (real comparative-measure theorems, second-order belief structures via beta distributions). The C&F file was ad-hoc replication; WAF 2026 is the validation that makes the framework load-bearing. Reserving substrate effort for that target.

**Three changes** in this commit: (1) `git rm Linglib/Phenomena/Persuasion/Studies/CumminsFranke2021.lean` (305 LOC removed); (2) `Linglib.lean` line 2334 import removed; (3) `references.bib` entry for `@cite{cummins-franke-2021}` updated `role = {formalized}` â†’ `role = {cited}`, `sources` field dropped (paper still cited from 4 substrate files + 2 sibling Persuasion studies; bib entry resolves cleanly). Bibliography regenerated (1774 entries, 7470 @cite refs scanned). Full `lake build Linglib` passes â€” substrate and consumer files were independent of the deleted file.

## [0.230.444] - 2026-04-26

### Polarity-marking refactor part 2: post-audit Tier Aâ€“D fixes

Second 4-agent audit of the `0.230.443` work (mathlib-reviewer + linglib-integration-auditor + linguistics-domain-expert + cross-framework-reconciler) flagged 4 tiers of follow-up: Tier A concrete bugs, Tier B faأ§ade-theorem cleanup, Tier C architectural improvements, Tier D sibling-framework engagement. All four tiers landed in this commit with build-clean verification at each step.

**Tier A â€” concrete bugs.** (1) `Fragments.Italian.PolarityMarking.siChe.environments` was `{.correction}`, falsified by @cite{garassino-jacob-2018} ex. 17 ("أˆ poi arrivato Gianni? â€” Sأ¬ che أ¨ arrivato" â€” a positive answer to a yes/no question, not a correction); now `{.correction, .contrast}` with cited justification. (2) Same bug in `Fragments.Spanish.PolarityMarking.siQue` per @cite{batllori-hernanz-2013} ex. 4-5 + G&J ex. 19; cognacy theorem `siChe.environments = siQue.environments := âں¨rfl, rflâں©` preserved by symmetric fix. (3) `GarassinoJacob2018.lean::algoDatum.availability` was `(false, false, true)` â€” over-claimed Italian non-availability of non-focal fronting, contradicted by G&J p. 232 prose ("typical of Spanish, but is also (more rarely) attested in Italian and Catalan"); now `(true, false, true)` with marginal-status documented; `syntactic_strategies_split_asymmetrically` theorem updated. (4) `TurcoBraunDimroth2014.lean::all_reversal_correction_only` was a universal claim that `.contrast âˆ‰ e.environments` for every polarity-reversal entry â€” falsified by the siChe/siQue fix; renamed `all_reversal_license_correction` and weakened to drop the contrast-exclusion clause (preserves the surviving Germanic-and-French-si generalization that polarity-reversal entries license correction). (5) `Linglib.lean` re-wiring: moved `TurcoBraunDimroth2014`, `GarassinoJacob2018`, `MaticNikolaeva2018` from the stranded l.2031-2033 Focus/Studies block into the alphabetical Polarity/Studies block at l.1359-1368. (6) Bib hygiene: dropped stale `Phenomena/Polarity/MarkingTypology.lean` from 3 entries' `sources` fields (the file doesn't exist; was a forward-looking reference); added `sources` field to `batllori-hernanz-2013` (now cited from 2 new locations).

**Tier B â€” faأ§ade-theorem cleanup.** (1) Deleted `gj_2018_endorses_mn_2018_salient_polarity : True := trivial` from G&J آ§4 â€” the docstring already records the alignment; `True := trivial` polluted the environment without adding greppable signal. (2) Deleted `french_si_dialogical_only` from G&J آ§5 â€” duplicated `french_lacks_siQueClass` per mathlib-audit redundancy flag. (3) Deleted `siChe_zero_occurrences_italian_europarl : ... := rfl` from G&J آ§2 â€” `rfl` on `some 0 = some 0`, the parent `italian_spanish_complementary_distribution` theorem already includes it as a conjunct. (4) Converted `mn_attested_structure_list_is_open : True := trivial` from a faأ§ade theorem to a substantive claim via new `mnOpenEndedWitnesses : List String := ["on the contrary", "just the opposite", "complement clauses with *it is true that p*"]` (the three M&N p. 15 explicitly-cited witnesses) + `mn_attested_list_strictly_grows : mnOpenEndedWitnesses â‰  [] := by decide` â€” the operational sense in which M&N's open-endedness claim holds against any closure attempt.

**Tier C â€” architectural.** (1) `MaticNikolaeva2018.lean::substrateBestEffort` now returns `Option PolarityMarkingStrategy` instead of `PolarityMarkingStrategy`. The 10 M&N structures that previously mapped to `.other` now map to `none`, exposing the dumping-ground claim *structurally* at the type level rather than via a `decide` count. The 3 non-isomorphism theorems updated correspondingly: `substrate_cannot_encode_germanEmphaticTun_and_englishVPFronting`, `fragment_data_lumps_emphaticDo_with_verumFocus`, `substrate_cannot_encode_at_least_ten`. (2) NEW `MNAttestedStructure.fromFragment : Option PolarityMarkingEntry` routes 4 M&N structures to actual existing Fragment entries (`englishEmphaticDo` â†’ `Fragments.English.PolarityMarking.emphaticDo`; `germanAccentOnAuxiliary` + `germanAccentOnLexicalVerb` â†’ `Fragments.German.PolarityMarking.verumFocus`; `germanAccentedDiscourseParticles` â†’ `Fragments.German.PolarityMarking.dochPreUtterance`); proven consistent with `substrateBestEffort` via `substrateBestEffort_agrees_with_fragments`. The non-isomorphism theorem `fragment_data_lumps_emphaticDo_with_verumFocus : emphaticDo.strategy = verumFocus.strategy := rfl` is now a claim about *real Fragment data* (English `emphaticDo` and German `verumFocus` collapse to the same substrate strategy), not an editorial fiction. (3) `Features/InformationStructure.lean::PolarityMarkingStrategy` docstring extended to include @cite{gutzmann-2015} as a third sibling framework in tension with the form-class encoding (alongside Hأ¶hle 1992 and Romero-Han 2004), with absolute-path cross-references to `Theories/Semantics/Mood/Gutzmann.lean` + `Phenomena/SentenceMood/Studies/Gutzmann2015.lean`.

**Tier D â€” sibling verum frameworks.** New آ§6 of `MaticNikolaeva2018.lean` (~80 LOC) extends the M&N argument from the substrate enum to two sibling frameworks already in linglib. (1) Romero-Han: new `inductive RHAnalysis | epistemicVerum` + `def romeroHanBestEffort : MNAttestedStructure â†’ Option RHAnalysis` mapping 7 prosodic-on-finite-verb structures (German + English + Serbian accent on auxiliary/finite verb + English emphatic *do*) to `some .epistemicVerum` and the other 11 to `none`; `theorem romeroHan_cannot_encode_at_least_eleven := by decide`. (2) Gutzmann 2015: new `inductive GutzmannDimension | deontic | epistemic | hearerKnowledge` + `def gutzmannBestEffort := fun _ => none` (constant-`none` because Gutzmann 2015's framework scope is sentence-mood-operator composition over clause types, not polarity-marking devices; the verum-specific Gutzmann work M&N actually cite â€” Gutzmann & Castroviejo Mirأ³ 2011 â€” is not formalized in linglib); `theorem gutzmann_2015_framework_does_not_apply : âˆ€ s, gutzmannBestEffort s = none := by intro _; rfl`. (3) Capstone theorem `mn_argument_extends_across_frameworks` proves the M&N coverage failures across all three formalized frameworks via a single `decide` (substrate â‰¥ 10 unencoded; R&H â‰¥ 11 unencoded; Gutzmann 2015 = 18 unencoded). The shared "Option-valued framework-fit mapping" pattern is acknowledged as a candidate typeclass (`FrameworkFit خ± := MNAttestedStructure â†’ Option خ±`); kept as separate `def`s for now so each framework's coverage profile stays visible at the def-site.

**Verification.** All 9 affected files (5 modified + 2 created from `0.230.443` + 2 cross-referenced sibling framework files) compile clean (980-job transitive closure). Bibliography validator: zero warnings on any of the 12 polarity-related cite keys (matic-nikolaeva-2018, garassino-jacob-2018, bernini-1995, poletto-zanuttini-2013, dimroth-sudhoff-2018, batllori-hernanz-2013, gutzmann-2015, romero-han-2004, hohle-1992, turco-braun-dimroth-2014, lohnstein-2018, seeliger-repp-2018); 7014 valid cites total (up from 7011), 457 unknowns all pre-existing/unrelated. bibliography.md regenerated. Two unrelated pre-existing full-build failures (`Core/Constraint/PermSubsetCombinatorics.lean` untracked, `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Hopf/AdmissibleCut.lean` untracked) confirmed via grep to have no import-edge to polarity work.

**LOC.** `MaticNikolaeva2018.lean` 221 â†’ 362 (+141 for آ§2b Fragment routing + آ§6 sibling-framework engagement + آ§4 substantive openness claim); `GarassinoJacob2018.lean` 306 â†’ 301 (-5 net: dropped 3 faأ§ade theorems + 1 algoDatum hedge + various docstring tightening); `Italian/PolarityMarking.lean` 81 â†’ 99 (+18 for siChe environments fix + extended docstring); `Spanish/PolarityMarking.lean` 53 â†’ 63 (+10 for siQue environments fix + docstring); `Features/InformationStructure.lean` +~10 (Gutzmann sibling-framework note in `PolarityMarkingStrategy` docstring); `TurcoBraunDimroth2014.lean` net unchanged (renamed theorem, dropped one conjunct); `Linglib.lean` net unchanged (3 imports moved); `references.bib` 3 stale sources dropped + 1 sources field added.

**Deferred.** (a) Promote `FrameworkFit خ± := MNAttestedStructure â†’ Option خ±` to a Lean typeclass with the 3 instances as `instance`s and the coverage theorem as a generic statement parameterized over `(f : FrameworkFit خ±)` â€” clean refactor visible from the آ§6 pattern, but earns its place only when a 4th instance arrives. (b) Refactor `Bool أ— Bool أ— Bool` projections in `GarassinoJacob2018.lean` to a named `RomanceAvailability` struct â€” flagged by mathlib reviewer; cosmetic only, deferred. (c) Rename `GJStrategy` / `MNAttestedStructure` etc. to drop the AuthorYear prefix (redundant with namespace) â€” also cosmetic, deferred. (d) Phase 5: study files for the remaining 6 Dimroth-Sudhoff 2018 chapters (bib entries already pre-added in `0.230.443`).

## [0.230.443] - 2026-04-26

### Polarity-marking refactor: Dimroth & Sudhoff (2018) volume engagement

One-layer-up refactor anchored on @cite{dimroth-sudhoff-2018}, *The Grammatical Realization of Polarity Contrast* (Linguistik Aktuell 249, DOI 10.1075/la.249), prompted by a 4-agent audit of `Fragments/Italian/PolarityMarking.lean` (mathlib-reviewer + linglib-integration-auditor + linguistics-domain-expert + cross-framework-reconciler) that found the file pattern-conformant but flagged systemic issues at the substrate (`Features/InformationStructure.lean`'s `PolarityMarkingStrategy` enum) and at the missing-study-file level. The refactor addresses citation hygiene, framework-commitment surfacing, and adds two study files for chapters of the source volume.

**Phase 0 â€” bib hygiene + Italian Fragment cleanup.** Added 10 new `references.bib` entries (umbrella @cite{dimroth-sudhoff-2018} + 7 chapter entries @cite{matic-nikolaeva-2018}/@cite{lohnstein-2018}/@cite{bacskai-atkari-2018}/@cite{ohl-2018}/@cite{gyuris-2018}/@cite{mueller-reichau-2018}/@cite{andorno-crocco-2018} + @cite{bernini-1995} + @cite{poletto-zanuttini-2013}); all chapter DOIs follow the verified `10.1075/la.249.NN[author-tag]` pattern (00-09 with intro=00); Bernini metadata transcribed from G&J's reference list (PDF p. 251); Poletto-Zanuttini DOI 10.1016/j.lingua.2012.10.016 confirmed via Elsevier landing page. Italian Fragment's bare-citation violations (`(Bernini 1995; Poletto & Zanuttini 2013)` not `@cite{}`-wrapped) fixed; the misleading "Table 1: 0 *sأ¬ che* vs 61 Spanish *sأ­ que*" corpus claim replaced with the **full** Table 1 data verified from PDF p. 239 (Italian: 6 polar LDs / 0 *sأ¬ che*; French: 4 LDs / NA; Spanish: 0 LDs / 61 *sأ­ que*) â€” the actual paper finding is the **complementary distribution** between LDs and *sأ­ que*, not the 0/61 sliver. Hedged the "same class as French *si*" claim per @cite{garassino-jacob-2018} fn 11 (French *si* is dialogically restricted, not a clause-initial construction comparable to *sأ¬ che*); hedged the "cleft-like construction" assertion to record both Bernini's cleft analysis and Poletto-Zanuttini's reduplication analysis as competing.

**Phase 1 â€” substrate framework-commitment flag.** Updated docstrings on `Features/InformationStructure.lean::PolarityMarkingStrategy` and `PolarityMarkingEntry` (no signature changes â€” type unchanged, 8 cross-language consumers unaffected) to flag that the form-class encoding aligns with the @cite{bluhdorn-lohnstein-2012}/@cite{sudhoff-2012}/@cite{turco-braun-dimroth-2014} tradition and is **explicitly rejected** by @cite{matic-nikolaeva-2018}, who argue PF is not encoded by forms but pragmatically conveyed in context (with @cite{garassino-jacob-2018} fn 13 endorsing the M&N view). Per CLAUDE.md "linglib's thesis is making theoretical incompatibilities visible", the substrate flag surfaces the commitment without resolving it; the formal contradiction-witness is anchored in the M&N study file (no bridge file).

**Phase 2 â€” `Phenomena/Polarity/Studies/GarassinoJacob2018.lean` (NEW, 226 LOC).** آ§1 records G&J's 8-strategy taxonomy (`inductive GJStrategy` â€” lexical adverb / affirmative particle / embedded-clause / elliptic embedding / non-focal fronting / faire-cleft / clitic dislocation / *sأ¬ che* class) â€” deliberately distinct from the substrate's 5-class enum, since G&J's carving cuts on syntactic structure rather than discourse function. آ§2 records Table 1 (p. 239) as `gjTable1 : List GJCorpusDatum` with `italian_spanish_complementary_distribution` theorem proving the 6/0/0/61 cross-cell pattern via `decide`. آ§3 hosts the **relocated** `siChe_siQue_cognates_at_encoding_level` theorem (moved from `TBD2014:446` since the cognacy claim chronologically anchors on Bernini 1995 / Batllori-Hernanz 2013 / G&J 2018, not TBD 2014). آ§4 records G&J fn 13's verbatim endorsement of M&N's salient-polarity view as documented provenance. آ§5 records G&J fn 11's distinction of French *si* (dialogically restricted) from *sأ¬ che* (clause-initial). Two cross-linguistic theorems via `decide`: `lexical_means_pan_romance` (4 lexical strategies available in all 3 languages) + `syntactic_strategies_split_asymmetrically` (each language licenses a different subset of syntactic strategies).

**Phase 3 â€” `Phenomena/Polarity/Studies/MaticNikolaeva2018.lean` (NEW, 161 LOC).** Encodes M&N's anti-denotational argument **without** stipulating salient polarity as a Prop (which would be the very form-meaning encoding M&N reject). آ§1 records 18 attested salient-polarity-conveying structures from M&N examples (2)â€“(4) covering German (7), English (7), and Serbian (4) â€” explicitly flagged as a finite proxy for an open-ended list. آ§2 defines `substrateBestEffort : MNAttestedStructure â†’ PolarityMarkingStrategy` showing the charitable mapping any denotational account must use. آ§3 proves three non-isomorphism theorems via `rfl`/`decide`: `substrate_encoding_not_injective` (German emphatic *tun* + English VP fronting collapse to `.other`), `verumFocus_collects_multiple_structures` (German auxiliary accent + English emphatic *do* both go to `.verumFocus`), `other_is_a_dumping_ground` (â‰¥9 of 18 M&N structures land in `.other`). آ§4 records the load-bearing open-endedness claim â€” the inductive type CAN'T faithfully express M&N's "the list seems to be open" point, so the theorem statement records the rejection of closure assumption rather than attempting to close the list. آ§5 records the G&J fn 13 cross-volume alignment.

**Cleanup.** Removed `italian_spanish_cognates` from `TurcoBraunDimroth2014.lean` (relocated, zero external consumers per grep). Wired both new study files into `Linglib.lean` after `TurcoBraunDimroth2014`. Updated existing `garassino-jacob-2018` bib entry: `role` cited â†’ formalized, sources updated to point at the new study file (and dropped the stale `Phenomena/Polarity/MarkingTypology.lean` path that pointed to a never-created file).

**Verification provenance.** Volume PDF (Linguistik Aktuell 249) read on 2026-04-26 to verify (a) chapter title + author + page range + DOI for all 9 chapter entries; (b) Bernini 1995 metadata transcribed from G&J references list p. 251; (c) Poletto-Zanuttini 2013 DOI confirmed via Elsevier landing page (200 OK); (d) G&J Table 1 corpus counts (PDF p. 239); (e) G&J fn 11 verbatim ("French *si*, unlike the corresponding forms in Spanish and Italian, is limited to dialogical contexts"); (f) G&J fn 13 verbatim endorsement of M&N (PDF p. 236); (g) M&N central thesis on denotational vs interpretational (PDF pp. 11-12) and 18-structure attested-list (M&N examples 2-4, PDF pp. 13-14).

**Build.** All 5 modified/created files compile (947-job transitive closure); bibliography validator reports zero warnings on any of the 10 new keys (7011 valid / 456 unknown â€” all unknowns pre-existing). Two unrelated full-build failures (`CoetzeePater2011.lean` modified concurrently in another session; `Hopf/AdmissibleCut.lean` untracked) have no import-edge to anything touched here.

**Deferred (audit-suggested, separable).** (a) Phase 4: promote `TurcoBraunDimroth2014::allEntries` + 4 generalization theorems to a top-level `Phenomena/Polarity/PolarityMarkingTypology.lean` per integration-auditor recommendation â€” the existing G&J bib entry's `Phenomena/Polarity/MarkingTypology.lean` source field anticipates this file (currently a dangling reference). (b) Phase 5: study files for the remaining 6 chapters of the volume (Lohnstein, Bacskai-Atkari, أ–hl, Seeliger-Repp already in bib, Gyuris, Mueller-Reichau, Andorno-Crocco) â€” bib entries pre-added, Hungarian/Russian Fragment data not yet present. (c) Phase 6: schema enhancement to distinguish syntactic position (clause-initial construction vs response particle vs sentence-medial discourse marker) â€” touches all 7 Fragment consumers.

## [0.230.442] - 2026-04-26

### M-C-B Hopf substrate Sessions 3-4: Defs.lean + AdmissibleCut.lean rework + new Comul.lean

Continuing the multi-session sprint to rebuild the Connes-Kreimer-style **bialgebra** (NOT Hopf yet â€” needs the (1âˆ’خ±) quotient per M-C-B Lemma 1.2.10) of binary nonplanar rooted syntactic forests, per @cite{marcolli-chomsky-berwick-2025} آ§1.2.

**`Defs.lean` rework** â€” source-verified against M-C-B 2025 آ§1.1.3.1 (p. 25): "These are genuinely multisets that *do not have* an equivalent description as sets." Switched `Forest := FreeMonoid SyntacticObjectH` (= `List`, planar/ordered, wrong) to `Forest := Multiset SyntacticObjectH` (unordered, faithful). Switched `Hc := MonoidAlgebra â„¤ Forest` to `Hc := AddMonoidAlgebra â„¤ Forest` (Multiset is `AddCancelCommMonoid`; convolution-on-AddMonoidAlgebra gives disjoint-union as algebra multiplication). Kept `def Hc` (not `abbrev`) to block mathlib's auto-derived trivial coalgebra (`CommSemiring.toCoalgebra` + `AddMonoidAlgebra.instCoalgebra` would give `خ”r = 1âٹ—r`, clashing with Connes-Kreimer). Forwarded `CommSemiring`/`CommRing`/`Algebra â„¤`/`FunLike` instances explicitly.

**`AdmissibleCut.lean` rework** â€” replaced sorry-laden `Finset SyntacticObjectH` representation (3 sorries: `decContains`, `remainder`, `Fintype`) with a structurally-recursive **4-way enum** `CutShape T`: `atLeaf | bothCut | onlyLeftCut cr | onlyRightCut cl | bothRecurse cl cr`. Antichain condition (no two cut edges on same root-leaf path, M-C-B Def 1.2.6) baked in by construction. `cutForest`/`remainder`/`DecidableEq`/`Fintype` defined sorry-free as structural recursions. Verified against M-C-B p. 46 worked example (eaten/the/apple): leaves ARE accessible terms because Def 1.2.2's "internal" means non-root (NOT non-leaf).

**`Comul.lean` (NEW)** â€” implements خ”^c per M-C-B Def 1.2.8: `comulTree T = T âٹ— 1 + خ£_{c : CutShape T} (cutForest c) âٹ— (remainder c)`. Forest extension `comulForest = Multiset.prod (F.map comulTree)` per M-C-B's `خ”^د‰(F) = âٹ”_a خ”(T_a)`. Lifted to **algebra hom** `comulAlgHom : Hc â†’â‚گ[â„¤] Hc âٹ—[â„¤] Hc` via `AddMonoidAlgebra.lift` (algebra-hom property needed for bialgebra structure per Lemma 1.2.10). Counit similarly as alg hom via the `if F = 0 then 1 else 0` monoid hom. Mathlib infra leveraged: `AddMonoidAlgebra.lift`, `Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Basic`, `Multiset.prod`. **Naming note**: `comulAlgHom` rather than `comul` to leave the short name available for the future Coalgebra typeclass field. **Limitation flagged**: `traceLeaf` placeholder fallback is unsound for iterated خ” (coassoc proofs); fix is mutual-induction `LeafToken : SyntacticObjectH`, deferred until Lemma 1.2.10 work.

3-agent post-Session-4 audit: docstrings downgraded "Hopf algebra" â†’ "bialgebra" per Lemma 1.2.10 (Hopf needs (1âˆ’خ±) quotient); `traceLeaf` fallback marked `-- UNSOUND for iterated خ”`; `Linglib.lean` import added; redundant `soToHc` dropped; bare `simp` in `comulForest_zero` tightened to `simp only [...]`. Deferred for upstream PR: universe polymorphism, generic parameterization `ConnesKreimer.BinaryForest R خ±`, `deriving DecidableEq` for CutShape, `namespace Minimalism.Hopf` rename. 1263 jobs green.

## [0.230.441] - 2026-04-26

### Bochnak 2015 آ§2.2 â€” 4-agent deep audit + substrate dissolution

4-agent deep audit (mathlib-reviewer + linguistics-domain-expert + integration-auditor + cross-framework-reconciler) of the just-shipped 0.230.436 `ConsistencyConstraints.lean` returned convergent verdicts: the substrate primitives belong in `Delineation.lean` alongside `IsMonotoneDelineation`; what remained in `ConsistencyConstraints.lean` after promoting the substrate was paper-anchor noise that earned no rent. Refactor:

**Substrate promoted into `Theories/Semantics/Comparison/Delineation.lean آ§13` "Faithfulness Predicates":**
- `IsSoundDelineation del R` (CC-b shape, generalising @cite{bochnak-2015} eq. 28b to abstract scalar relations) â€” now a `class` rather than `structure`, firing by typeclass synthesis. Mathlib pattern: same shape as `OrderHomClass` / `IsLeftCancelMul`.
- `IsCompleteDelineation del R` (the converse direction; NOT in Bochnak â€” closer to @cite{burnett-2017}'s *Plenitude* / *Granularity* axioms and @cite{klein-1980} coverage assumptions). Split out from the original `FaithfulDelineation` structure per linguistics-audit recommendation that the backward direction shouldn't be packaged with Bochnak's name.
- `comparativeSem_iff_of_sound_and_complete` â€” the bidirectional bridge.
- `instSoundMeasureDelineation` and `instCompleteMeasureDelineation` â€” measure-induced instance witnesses, now derived from `Hierarchy.lean`'s existing `ordering_implies_degree` and `degree_implies_ordering` (the integration auditor's convergent dedup recommendation). Eliminates duplicated structural reasoning. The complete-direction proof uses the `{a, b}` two-element comparison class from `degree_implies_ordering` rather than the linguistically-degenerate singleton `{y}` from the original draft (linguistics audit: Klein/Kennedy/Fara would object to singleton CCs).
- Cross-framework engagement docstring: `### Relation to Hierarchy.lean` (no duplication, factor through `ordering_iff_degree`), `### Relation to Kamp 1975` (existential dual of preorder universal), `### Relation to Fine 1975` (Kleinâ†”Fine duality via `monotone_comparative_superTrue`), `### Tension with Cobreros et al 2012` (CC-b is strict-classical, would over-generate under tolerant semantics on similar pairs). Per cross-framework auditor: linglib's interconnection-density thesis demands explicit engagement.

**`ConsistencyConstraints.lean` DELETED.** After the substrate promotion, what remained was: the `IsConsistencyConstraintB := IsSoundDelineation` cite-tag wrapper (mathlib-reviewer flagged as the "encoding conclusions as definitions" anti-pattern; replaced with a docstring comment in `Delineation.lean آ§13`), the `perContext_implies_R` one-line wrapper (subsumed by `IsSoundDelineation.sound`), and the footnote-11 counterexample (paper-anchored â€” moved to `Bochnak2015.lean آ§6`). Manifest entry removed; bib `sources` fields for `kennedy-2011`, `van-rooij-2011a`, `bochnak-2015` repointed to `Delineation.lean`.

**`Bochnak2015.lean` updates:**
- آ§3 `comparativeSem_measureDelineation_iff_degree` now a one-line corollary firing typeclass synthesis: `comparativeSem_iff_of_sound_and_complete (R := fun a b => height b < height a)`.
- NEW آ§6 `cc_b_requires_shared_class` â€” the load-bearing **footnote-11** counterexample (single-shared-CC requirement). Concrete 2-CC, 2-entity model: `Entity := Bool`, `del C x := x = true âˆ§ true âˆˆ C`, `R a b := a = true âˆ§ b = false`. Soundness holds; the SPLIT-CC weakening admits `(true, true)` via `Câ‚پ := {true}`, `Câ‚‚ := {false}`, while `R true true = false`. Paper-anchored here because the footnote is Bochnak-specific.
- Module docstring updated: آ§2.2 section now points at `Delineation.lean آ§13` substrate.

**Bib additions (PDF-verified DOIs):**
- `kennedy-2011`: Vagueness and Comparison, in @cite{kennedy-2011} *Vagueness and Language Use* (أ‰grأ© & Klinedinst eds., Palgrave 2011), DOI 10.1057/9780230299313_4.
- `van-rooij-2011a`: Implicit versus Explicit Comparatives, same volume, DOI 10.1057/9780230299313_3.
- Both required for the linguistics-audit-corrected attribution of CC-a/b: Bochnak's PDF p. 6:16 cites "(Klein 1980, 1991, Kennedy 2011, van Rooij 2011a)" for eq. 28, NOT Klein + Fara as the original 0.230.436 docstring had it. Fara 2000 is the right anchor for the **Similarity Constraint** (eq. 20), not for (28).

**Cite-key fixes from validator:**
- `@cite{burnett-2014}` â†’ `@cite{burnett-2017}` (the L&P 2014 paper's content is in the 2017 OUP book; only the book is in bib).
- `@cite{cobreros-egre-ripley-vanrooij-2012}` â†’ `@cite{cobreros-etal-2012}` (actual key in bib).

861 jobs in subgraph green. Ready for full build verify.

## [0.230.440] - 2026-04-26

### Bool dissolution: Question + Focus + Comparability layers (post-deep-audit)

After 4-agent deep audit (linguistics + mathlib-reviewer + integration-auditor + cross-framework-reconciler) flagged three parallel question substrates as the highest-leverage architectural issue, dissolved the legacy Bool-functional substrate `Core/Question/Predicate.lean::QuestionDen := (W â†’ Bool) â†’ Bool` together with `Theories/Semantics/Focus/Interpretation.lean::PropFocusValue` (same `(W â†’ Bool) â†’ Bool` shape) â€” both migrated to substrate-aligned `Set`-based representations across ~12 consumer files.

**Substrate migrations**:
- `PropFocusValue := (W â†’ Bool) â†’ Bool` â†’ `Set (Set W)` in `Theories/Semantics/Focus/Interpretation.lean`. `Squiggle.contrastSet`, `fip` (from `âˆ€ p, gamma p â†’ focusValue p` to `gamma âٹ† focusValue`), `qaCongruentWeak` (subset), `FocusResolution.{ordinary, comparisonClass, fip_subset, ordinary_in_C}`, `ClauseEmbedPred`, `IsFocusSensitive`, `IsNotFocusSensitive`, `liftNonFS`, `liftNonFS_not_fs` all migrated. Removed dead `hamblin_is_focus_type` theorem (vacuous after Bool removal).
- `Theories/Semantics/Focus/Comparability.lean::semanticallyIndependent`/`commonIntegrator`/`wellFormedAlts` migrated from `(W â†’ Bool)`/`List (W â†’ Bool)` to `Set W`/`List (Set W)`. Predicates rewritten using set inclusion.

**Consumer migrations** (12 files):
- `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Holmberg2016.lean`: `polar p worlds` Bool theorem replaced with substrate `both_alternatives_in_polar` over `Core.Question.polar`.
- `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/Roberts2012.lean`: type-identity theorem updated to `PropFocusValue D0World = Set (Set D0World)`.
- `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/Rooth1992.lean`: آ§2 propositions migrated `QAWorld â†’ Bool` â†’ `Set QAWorld`; آ§6 questions migrated `QuestionDen` â†’ `PropFocusValue` with set-literal `{pâ‚پ, pâ‚‚, ...}` notation; FIP proofs restructured around `Set.subset`.
- `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/Umbach2004.lean` (entire 544-LOC file): 6 propositions in آ§2, well-formedness proofs in آ§3, CDWorld propositions and `confirmDeny` in آ§5, `roomDishesEquiv` (now Prop-pair Setoid kernel), `singleContrast`, `contrastImplicitQ`, `correctionImplicitQ` in آ§6 â€” all Boolâ†’Set.
- `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/TurkHirsch2026.lean`: `PolarWorld â†’ Bool` propositions (`p`, `notP`, `mustP`) â†’ `Set PolarWorld`; `QuestionDen` â†’ `PropFocusValue`; alt-set theorems restructured.
- `Phenomena/Constructions/Studies/KayFillmore1999.lean`: `wxdyLiteralQ`/`wxdyIncredulityQ` migrated from Bool `Hamblin.which` API to substrate `Core.Question.{which, declarative}`.
- `Theories/Semantics/Focus/BackgroundedIslands.lean`: `extractionQ : QuestionDen` â†’ `PropFocusValue`; proof of `extraction_filler_is_focus_alternative` rewritten around set membership.
- `Theories/Interfaces/SyntaxSemantics/LeftPeriphery.lean`: `Hamblin.QuestionDen W` field type â†’ `Core.Question W` (substrate, not focus alias).
- `Theories/Semantics/Composition/Applicative.lean`: docstring update only (one comment reference).
- `Theories/Semantics/Attitudes/Preferential.lean`: 105 LOC of `toHamblin`/`fromHamblin`/`roundtrip_preserves_membership` bridges deleted (their entire purpose was bridging to the now-deleted `QuestionDen`); dead `Predicate.lean` import removed.
- `Theories/Semantics/Attitudes/Doxastic.lean`: dead `Predicate.lean` import removed.
