Cascading consumer updates in `Pylkkanen2008`, `SeeligerRepp2018`, `Tay2024`, `AkinboFwangwar2026`, `Fragments/Japanese/Prosody.lean`, `Fragments/Hausa/Tone.lean` (`= true := rfl` â†’ `:= by decide`; tuple-conjunct positions get `by decide`; computational `if`/`==` consumers wrap `decide`).

## [0.230.37] - 2026-04-19

### Removed
- **`Phenomena/WordOrder/Studies/Hudson1984.lean` deleted** along with its import from `Linglib.lean` and the orphan `@book{hudson-1984}` entry in `references.bib`. Hudson1984 was an early, redundant study file: its theorems duplicated `Theories/Syntax/DependencyGrammar/Core/NetworkIntegration.lean` (the canonical WG-network definition of subject-auxiliary inversion as subtype inheritance) and added no Phenomena-level data. The bib entry's DOI (`10.1093/oxfordhb/9780199738632.013.0019`) was hallucinated â€” that DOI points to a 2012 Oxford Handbook chapter, not the 1984 Blackwell book; the chain of `@cite{hudson-1984}` calls picked up its claims by reference rather than from the actual source. Per `CLAUDE.md` "Never cite specific page ranges from memory", the citations referencing "pp. 117-118" were also unverifiable.

### Changed
- **`@cite{hudson-1984}` â†’ `@cite{hudson-2010}`** in 7 source files (`Theories/Syntax/DependencyGrammar/{Coordination, LongDistance, Formal/NonProjective, Core/Basic, Core/LexicalRules, Core/NetworkIntegration}.lean`). The 2010 textbook (`hudson-2010`, doi:`10.1017/cbo9780511781964`) is the verified source covering the same WG content; the 1984 monograph entry is dropped.
- **`NetworkIntegration.lean`**: word-class string `interrogative_auxiliary` â†’ `inverted_auxiliary` (the term Hudson 2010 actually uses) and theorem renames `interrogative_aux_inherits_main_verb_slot` â†’ `inverted_aux_inherits_main_verb_slot`, `network_interrogative_aux_argStr` â†’ `network_inverted_aux_argStr`. Hallucinated "pp. 117-118" page citations and the fabricated AVM-style block quote dropped from the module docstring; replaced with a plain prose summary of the subtype-inheritance analysis.
- **`Fragments/English/Auxiliaries.lean`** consumer-list docstring updated to drop the deleted Hudson1984 study.

## [0.230.36] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/RonderosEtAl2024.lean` critical-review pass v2**: four fixes from a mathlib-PR-style review of v1 (0.230.35) against the actual @cite{ronderos-etal-2024} PDF. **(1) Hallucinated cite key**: `bierwisch-1987` (used in 2 places in `SedivyEtAl1999.lean` and propagated into the v1 Ronderos rewrite) replaced with the actual bib entry `bierwisch-1989` ("The Semantics of Gradation"); flagged by `python3 blog/scripts/gen_bibliography.py --check`. **(2) Empirically too-strong `material_effect_smaller`**: v1's `Cell` carried a `Language : english | hindi | hungarian` field and the interaction predicate quantified universally over languages â€” but Figure 3 of the paper shows English material contrast effect â‰ˆ English color contrast effect (0.24 vs 0.25), so universal pairwise dominance fails. The paper deliberately treats LANGUAGE as a clustering unit (random effect) rather than a typed predictor â€” cross-linguistic generalization is *methodology*, not paradigm structure. **Dropped `Language` enum entirely**; Cell is now `{ contrast : ContrastCondition; adjType : AdjType }`. **(3) Missing second empirical family**: the paper's آ§3 آ¶4 reports a SECOND finding beyond the contrast-effect family â€” in the No-Contrast condition, baseline total looks (target + cross-category competitor) for scalar adjectives are *lower* than for color (خ² = 0.25, z = 2.80, p < 0.01) and material (خ² = 0.24, z = 2.40, p < 0.05). v1 dropped this entirely. Added two new `SatisfiesRonderosPattern` fields: `scalar_baseline_lower_than_color` and `scalar_baseline_lower_than_material`, both built on a new paradigm-level predicate. **(4) New cross-study bridge `two_mechanisms_factorise`**: articulates the orthogonal-routes claim â€” `requiresComparisonClass` (semantic route, captured by Sedivy/Bierwisch's "scalar requires comparison-class binding") and `noiseDiscrimination` (perceptual route, captured by `RSA.Noise`'s color > size > material ordering) make independent predictions; Ronderos's pattern is the joint envelope. The theorem records the type-level identity `(d.requiresComparisonClass, d.noiseDiscrimination) = (sedivy_lookup d, ronderos_noise_lookup d)` for each `PropertyDomain`. Proof witness `trivialLooks` extended to provide target/competitor values in the no-contrast condition (color 5/5, scalar 1/4, material 6/2 â€” yielding scalar baseline sum 5 < color 10 and < material 8). Module docstring gains an "Open architectural threads" section flagging two deferred deeper integrations: (a) noise-perturbed `IncrementalSemantics` witness instantiating `SatisfiesRonderosPattern` directly from RSA + `RSA.Noise`; (b) `Theories/Semantics/Gradability` classification bridge (relative vs absolute scalar adjectives) so the scalar/material/color axis is theory-derived rather than enum-stipulated.
- **`Paradigms/VisualWorld.lean` آ§7 added**: paradigm-level lift for the baseline-restrictiveness pattern that the Ronderos finding instantiates. **`roleSum [Add R] [Zero R] (roles : List ObjectRole) (looks : LookProportion Cell R) (c : Cell) : R := (roles.map (fun r => looks r c)).sum`** â€” sums look proportions across a role list at a given cell; the natural building block for "total looks to inference-relevant referents" measures (target + cross-category competitor for adjective-conditioned displays). **`RoleSumLowerInBaselineWhen [HasContrastCondition Cell] [Add R] [Zero R] [LT R] (baseline : ContrastCondition) (roles : List ObjectRole) (P Q : Cell â†’ Prop) (looks : LookProportion Cell R) : Prop`** â€” the qualitative claim that, for every `P`-cell `cP` and `Q`-cell `cQ`, `roleSum roles looks (setContrast baseline cP) < roleSum roles looks (setContrast baseline cQ)`. Anchored on @cite{aparicio-xiang-kennedy-2015}, @cite{aparicio-2017} (which establish the scalar-adjective baseline-restrictiveness pattern Ronderos extends cross-linguistically); the paradigm contract documents this as a *recurring* visual-world pattern, not a Ronderos one-off. **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/SedivyEtAl1999.lean`**: `bierwisch-1987` â†’ `bierwisch-1989` in 2 places (lines 71, 289) â€” pre-existing hallucination, not introduced here. Build clean: 921 jobs through Ronderos2024, 2672 jobs through the entire Reference subtree (Sedivy + Ronderos + Cohn-Gordon all green).

### Added
- **Bibliography entries**: `aparicio-xiang-kennedy-2015` (SALT 25:413â€“432, doi:10.3765/salt.v25i0.3128 â€” visual-world processing of gradable adjectives, anchoring study for the baseline-restrictiveness pattern); `aparicio-2017` (PhD thesis, University of Chicago, *Processing context-sensitive expressions: The case of gradable adjectives and numerals* â€” extended the SALT 25 line of work). Both entries verified against the Ronderos paper's reference list (page 1226).

## [0.230.35] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Paradigms/VisualWorld.lean` آ§6**: stratified and effect-size predicates for multi-factor visual-world designs. **`contrastEffect [HasContrastCondition Cell] [Sub R] (role : ObjectRole) (looks : LookProportion Cell R) (c : Cell) : R`** projects out `looks role (setContrast .noContrast c) âˆ’ looks role (setContrast .contrast c)` â€” the paradigm-level shape of the "target advantage" / "competitor reduction" measure that downstream studies report; the lens laws on `setContrast` ensure this depends only on the non-contrast factors of `c`. **`ContrastReducesRoleLooksWhen [HasContrastCondition Cell] [LT R] (P : Cell â†’ Prop) (role : ObjectRole) (looks : LookProportion Cell R) : Prop`** + abbrev **`ContrastReducesCompetitorLooksWhen P looks`** restrict the contrast-effect claim to cells satisfying a sub-cell predicate `P` â€” needed because a 2 أ— *k* design (e.g. @cite{ronderos-etal-2024}'s 2 أ— 3 contrast أ— adjective-type) can have one stratum where the universal `ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` claim is empirically *false* (Ronderos's material adjectives). **`ContrastEffectLargerFor role P Q looks`** is the qualitative shape of an "X أ— condition" interaction: every `P`-cell's contrast effect strictly exceeds every `Q`-cell's. Universal quantification over both strata is the strongest qualitative reading; mean-over-mean readings need a real-valued aggregator (out of scope for the paradigm contract per `CLAUDE.md` Processing scope). **`ContrastReducesRoleLooks_iff_when_True`** records that the آ§5 universal-form predicate is the `P := True` case of the stratified version, so studies migrating to the stratified API can recover their existing universal claims by passing the trivial filter.
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/RonderosEtAl2024.lean`** rewritten end-to-end ("mathlib-quality" overhaul matching the @cite{cohn-gordon-goodman-potts-2019} / @cite{sedivy-etal-1999} treatment). **Deleted**: every `ClusterResult` / `TargetAdvantageResult` / `BaselineResult` Float-statistics struct (`cluster_color`, `cluster_scalar`, `cluster_material`, `cluster_interaction`, `targetAdv_*`, `baseline_*`, `Language.n`, `totalN`, `itemsPerType`, `totalTrials`); every `_significant` Bool theorem and every `native_decide` proof over Float literals; every `rfl`-over-stipulated-statistics theorem (`contrastive_inference_pattern`, `effect_size_ordering`, `discrimination_predicts_contrastive`, `noise_ordering_matches_effect_ordering`, `converging_evidence_with_kursat_degen`, `agrees_with_sedivy_on_scalar`, `disagrees_with_sedivy_on_color`, `two_routes`) â€” the "encoding conclusions as definitions" anti-pattern called out in `CLAUDE.md`. **Added**: `inductive AdjType := color | scalar | material` (with `Fintype` deriving) + `AdjType.toDomain` mapping each to its `Core.PropertyDomain` (color â†’ `.color`, scalar â†’ `.size`, material â†’ `.material`); `inductive Language := english | hindi | hungarian` (study-local factor with no paradigm consumer, scoped by future cross-linguistic stratification predicates); `structure Cell { contrast : ContrastCondition; adjType : AdjType; language : Language }` with `HasContrastCondition` lens (struct-update implementations + three `rfl` lens-law proofs) and `HasDisplayKind` projection (between-study constant `.objectArray`); explicit absence of `HasTask` instance documented inline (the task is fixed across all cells, and a constant projection cannot satisfy `setTask`/`taskOf` lens laws â€” studies that hold a paradigm factor constant should *omit* the lens, not stub it with a constant). **Sub-cell predicates** (the strata): `ColorCells`/`ScalarCells`/`MaterialCells` as `Cell â†’ Prop` filters on `c.adjType`; `InferenceTriggeringCells := ColorCells âˆ¨ ScalarCells` (the union scoped by the interaction predicate). **`structure SatisfiesRonderosPattern (looks : LookProportion Cell R) : Prop`** with three fields: `color_contrast_reduces_competitor : ContrastReducesCompetitorLooksWhen ColorCells looks` (paper آ§3.1 cluster 240â€“600 ms; آ§3.2 خ² = 0.24, t = 2.41, p < 0.05 â€” documented in field docstring), `scalar_contrast_reduces_competitor : ContrastReducesCompetitorLooksWhen ScalarCells looks` (cluster 260â€“500 ms; خ² = 0.19, t = 2.02), `material_effect_smaller : ContrastEffectLargerFor .crossCategoryCompetitor InferenceTriggeringCells MaterialCells looks` (interaction cluster 280â€“600 ms). Material null encoded by *absence* of a positive clause, not by `آ¬ ContrastReducesCompetitorLooksWhen MaterialCells looks` â€” statistical power and direction-of-null are prose, not theorem; the interaction field is the stronger qualitative claim that survives without statistical machinery (material's effect strictly smaller, not exactly zero). **Non-vacuity witness**: `trivialLooks : LookProportion Cell â„ڑ` constructed so that competitor looks are 1 in every contrast cell and depend on adjective type in no-contrast cells (color 5, scalar 4, material 2 â€” yielding contrast effects 4/3/1 with strict inference-triggering > material ordering); `trivial_satisfies_pattern : SatisfiesRonderosPattern trivialLooks` proved by `obtain âں¨k, a, lâں© := c; simp only [SubCellPred] at hc; subst hc; cases k <;> simp [trivialLooks]` for the per-stratum fields, and the interaction field by destructuring both cells, casing on the `InferenceTriggeringCells` disjunction, and `cases kP <;> cases kQ <;> simp [contrastEffect, trivialLooks] <;> norm_num`. **آ§7 cross-study bridges** are *type-level* (no `rfl`-over-stats): `scalar_shares_sedivy_domain` (both studies place scalar effect on `.size`, which `requiresComparisonClass`); `color_does_not_require_comparison_class` and `material_does_not_require_comparison_class` (`PropertyDomain.requiresComparisonClass = false` for both â€” Sedivy/Bierwisch lexical-only mechanism predicts no color effect, contradicted by `color_contrast_reduces_competitor`); `effect_ordering_aligns_with_noise_discrimination` (delegates to existing `RSA.Noise.color_gt_size`/`size_gt_material`, no re-proof); `adjType_to_noise_discrimination` (records the structural identity that `Core.PropertyDomain.noiseDiscrimination âˆک AdjType.toDomain` resolves to the corresponding `RSA.Noise` constant). Build clean: 921 jobs through Ronderos2024, 2672 jobs through the entire Reference subtree (Sedivy + Ronderos + Cohn-Gordon all green); preexisting Phonology/`hasValue` errors in `Fragments/{Korean,Akan,English}/Phonology.lean` are from the in-progress f1f44769 phase-C refactor and unrelated to this change.

## [0.230.34] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Paradigms/VisualWorld.lean`** polymorphised over codomain. **`LookPrediction (Cell : Type) := ObjectRole â†’ Cell â†’ â„ڑ`** renamed and generalised to **`LookProportion (Cell R : Type) := ObjectRole â†’ Cell â†’ R`** â€” paradigm consumers may now choose `R := â„ڑ` (exact rationals, common for tableaux) or `R := â„‌` (default for RSA-derived posteriors that arise from `Real.exp`/`Real.log`-mediated speakers). Paradigm-level look-prediction predicates (`ContrastReducesRoleLooks`, `ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks`, `ContrastSpeedsTargetLooks`) re-stated with `[LT R]` predicates so the Cohn-Gordon RSA-derived bridge can apply them directly to â„‌-typed posteriors without first coercing to â„ڑ. RT predicates kept on â„ڑ (RT is conventionally rational in this paradigm). **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/SedivyEtAl1999.lean`** threaded the new type parameter through the local API: the study's `SatisfiesSedivyPattern` and the trivial non-vacuity witness now take `(R : Type)` with the same `[LT R]`/`[Sub R]` instances. Existing call-sites within the study unchanged (instances default to â„ڑ).
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/CohnGordonEtAl2019.lean`** rewired the آ§10 Sedivy bridge onto the polymorphised `Paradigms.VisualWorld`. **New `SedivyScene_NoContrast` namespace**: a fresh four-word `IncrementalSemantics` (`Word := tall | cup | pitcher | key` â€” `.short` deliberately omitted because the shortCup referent is absent from the no-contrast display, leaving the speaker with no scene-anchored use for the word) over the three remaining referents (`tallCup`/`tallPitcher`/`key`, sharing `SedivyScene.Referent` so a single Cell-typed projection can read off both scenes). Rationale documented inline: CG's `IncrementalSemantics` is a *scene-specific* production model (the `wordApplies` tier is a Boolean truth predicate over the scene's referent set), not a lexicon-wide model â€” keeping `.short` in `Word` with no extension would yield `0/0` in `incrementalSem`, which is mathematically `0` in mathlib but not reducible by `rsa_predict`'s RExpr evaluator. **`cgSedivyLooks : LookProportion SedivyEtAl1999.Cell â„‌`** is the Cell-typed projection that reads off both scenes' L1 posteriors after the prefix `[.tall]`. The definition carries a load-bearing docstring naming the *Bayesian posterior linking hypothesis* â€” the assumption that the proportion of fixations on referent `r` at a moment when L1's posterior over referents has shape `p` is itself proportional to `p(r)` â€” and acknowledges this hypothesis is editorial: Cohn-Gordon do not state it (they only run the comprehension model and report posteriors), so the linking layer is the formaliser's bridge, flagged honestly in prose. A weaker alternative (Luce-choice activation monotone in posterior) is mentioned as the conservative fallback. **Bridge theorem `cgSedivyLooks_satisfy_contrast_reduces_competitor`**: proves `ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks (R := â„‌) cgSedivyLooks` directly â€” for every cell `c`, `incRSA_sedivy_noContrast.L1 .tall .tallPitcher > incRSA_sedivy.L1 .tall .tallPitcher` (1/2 vs 2/5) â€” by destructuring `c` (lens preserves `c.task` and `c.typicality`, so the goal collapses to a single inequality), `show`-ing the goal in unfolded form, then closing via `rsa_predict` (single 76ms reflection). **Removed**: the old `sedivyBundle_noContrast` (which kept `.short`, broken by the 0/0 issue) and the آ§11.5 `sedivyLooksAfterTall : ObjectRole â†’ â„‌` paradigm-flavor projection plus `sedivyLooksAfterTall_target_gt_competitor` â€” both superseded by the typed `LookProportion`/`ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` bridge. Removed unverified Allopenna/Magnuson/Tanenhaus 1998 citation rather than fabricate metadata. آ§8 docstring updated to drop the prior "out of scope" caveat about Cell-typed `LookPrediction`. Build clean: 2670 jobs, 0 errors, 0 new sorries.

## [0.230.33] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Theories/Semantics/Modality/EventRelativity.lean` Boolâ†’Prop migration**: `possibility`, `necessity`, `orderedPossibility`, `orderedNecessity` now take `(p : W â†’ Prop) [DecidablePred p]` instead of `(p : W â†’ Bool)`. `duality`, `empty_ordering_reduces`, `doxastic_necessity_eq`, `doxastic_possibility_eq` re-stated and re-proved over `Prop` predicates (Bool boolean negation `!p w` becomes `آ¬ p w`; `p w' = true` collapses to `p w'`). In-file test theorems use `(آ· = .took)` etc. instead of `(آ· == .took)`. `Decidable` instances preserved via `[DecidablePred p]` guards on the boundary.
- **`Theories/Semantics/Modality/ModalIndefinites.lean` cascade**: `modalComponent`, `modalIndefiniteSat`, `upperBoundedSat` now take `P Q : Entity â†’ W â†’ Prop` with `[âˆ€ y, DecidablePred (P y)] [âˆ€ y, DecidablePred (Q y)]` decidability witnesses. Worked-example predicates `isBook`, `isAvailable`, `isCard`, `canGrab` migrated to `Prop` with explicit per-case `Decidable` instances (`instDecidableTrue`/`instDecidableFalse`). Removes the deferred TODO at line 151.
- **`Theories/Semantics/Modality/Assert.lean` cascade**: speech-act test theorems (`declarative_leave_possible`, `declarative_stay_possible`, `imperative_leave_permitted`, `imperative_stay_not_permitted`, `speech_act_modulates_domain`) updated `(آ· == .leave)` â†’ `(آ· = .leave)` predicates. Other 9 listed consumers of `EventRelativity` only used the unaffected anchor/binder/projection API and required no changes.

## [0.230.32] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/PhonologicalAlternation/Studies/Belth2026.lean` rewrite**: deleted the fabricated "Cser-style fine tier" apparatus (`fineTier`, `latinDissimFine`, `latinFineOCP`, `predictedFine`, every `_fine` theorem, `coarse_fine_disagree_on_lunaris`, `latinDissimFine_covers_all`) â€” @cite{belth-2026} learns ONE rule on the `[+cons]` tier (rule 54), and *lunaris* is one of the ~3% errors the Tolerance Principle tolerates, not a case the fine tier "saves". Renamed `latinDissimCoarse` â†’ `latinDissimRule` and `predictedCoarse` â†’ `predicted`. **Fixed `/v/` treatment**: `LatSeg.isCons .v => false` (Belth treats orthographic âں¨vâں© as `[w]`, `[âˆ’cons]`) â€” the choice that lets the new *pluvalis* example surface as `[r]` (tier-preceding consonant of `/pluv-/` is `/l/`, not `/v/`). Added *pluvalis* (`pluvalis_ur`, `pluvalis_predicts_r`, `pluvalisERC`, etc.) to complete @cite{belth-2026} ex. 5. Reframed `lunaris_predicts_l_INCORRECT` as the genuine empirical limit. **Wired Tolerance Principle**: `tolerates_six_one : tolerates 6 1` (proof via `Real.log_le_sub_one_of_pos` + `le_div_iffâ‚€`) and `latinDissimRule_tolerated_on_examples` connecting `latinData.length` and the filter-based exception count to the threshold. **Subregular bridge**: `consTier_apply_eq_tierProject` grounding `LatSeg.consTier` in the canonical `Core.Computability.Subregular.tierProject` from the TSL formalism (@cite{lambert-2022}, @cite{heinz-rawal-tabor-2011}). **آ§9 OT tableau bridge rebuilt on the actual `[+cons]` tier**: `latinOCP := mkOCPOnTier "OCP/[+cons]" LatSeg.consTier id`; per-example optimum theorems for *navalis*/*popularis*/*pluvalis*/*floralis*/*legalis*; honest `lunaris_optimal_is_l_INCORRECT` showing the OT analysis fails the same way the rule does; per-example ERCs (`popularisERC_is_simple`, `pluvalisERC_is_simple`, `lunarisERC_isContradictory`, three `_isTrivial`); `latinERCSet_consistent_without_lunaris` (consistency holds on the 5 non-lunaris contrasts); `latin_OCP_dominates_starR` derives the unique Hasse arrow.
- **`Core/Constraint/OT/ERC.lean`**: promoted three reusable lemmas. **`simpleERC_consistent : i â‰  j â†’ ERCSet.consistent [simpleERC i j]`** with explicit witness construction (identity ranking when `i.val < j.val`; `Equiv.swap i j` otherwise) â€” replaces ad-hoc `âں¨Ranking.id n, by decideâں©` patterns at study sites. **`@[simp] simpleERC_apply_W` / `simpleERC_apply_L`** characterising the W- and L-positions. **Decidable instance** for `ERC.isContradictory` (`inferInstanceAs (Decidable (_ âˆ§ _))`) â€” was missing despite `isTrivial` having one; enables `by decide` proofs of contradictoriness in study files.
- **`Core/Constraint/Evaluation.lean`**: cleaned up scaffolding `noncomputable example : LinearOrder (ViolationProfile 3) := inferInstance` and the two unnamed `example`s for pointwise `Add`/`Zero`. Replaced with named (non-simp) theorems `ViolationProfile.add_apply` and `ViolationProfile.zero_apply` â€” accessible to consumers, no behavioral change to simp set.

### Added
- **Bibliography entries**: `cser-2010` (Cser 2010, *The -alis/-aris allomorphy revisited*, John Benjamins, doi:10.1075/cilt.310.02cse â€” feature inventory and descriptive blocking generalisation cited by Belth); `yang-2016` (Yang 2016, *The Price of Linguistic Productivity*, MIT Press, doi:10.7551/mitpress/9780262035323.001.0001 â€” Tolerance Principle source); `ringen-heinamaki-1999` (Ringen & Heinأ¤mأ¤ki 1999, NLLT 17(2):303â€“337, doi:10.1023/A:1006158818498 â€” Finnish vowel harmony OT account cited for D2L's Finnish rule).

## [0.230.31] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Theories/Pragmatics/RSA/Incremental.lean`**: graduated the scene-specific incremental-RSA bundle pattern out of `Phenomena/Reference/Studies/CohnGordonEtAl2019.lean` into a reusable theory module. **`structure IncrementalSemantics (U W : Type) [DecidableEq U]`** bundles the three pieces that uniquely determine an @cite{cohn-gordon-goodman-potts-2019} scene: `wordApplies : U â†’ W â†’ Bool` (word-level Boolean truth), `completeUtterances : List (List U)` (closed set of full utterances), `worlds : List W` (referents to normalize over). Every other model ingredient is *derived*: `uttSem` (utterance-level conjunction), `trueExtCount`/`viableExtCount` (extension counts via `List.filter` + `List.isPrefixOf`), `noncomputable incrementalSem` (the آ§2.2 âں¦pfxâں§(r) ratio), `noncomputable l0Utt` (literal listener over complete utterances), and `noncomputable toRSAConfig : RSAConfig U W` (the No-Brevity sequential RSA model with `s1Score = L0`, خ± = 1, no cost, `transition := fun ctx w => ctx ++ [w]`, `initial := []`). **Promoted deep theorem `l0Utt_ge_inv_card`**: for *any* complete utterance `utt` true of `r âˆˆ worlds`, `sem.l0Utt utt r â‰¥ 1 / sem.worlds.length` â€” the آ§2.4 weakly-informative bound that certifies greedy chain-rule unrolling never produces an utterance arbitrarily worse than uniform. Proof is purely combinatorial (`List.length_pos_of_mem` + `List.length_filter_le` + `one_div_le_one_div_of_le`) and reusable across scenes â€” concrete studies need only prove their candidate utterance is in `completeUtterances` and is true of the target. **New bib entry**: `sedivy-2007` (Philosophy Compass 2(3): 475â€“496, doi:10.1111/j.1747-9991.2007.00082.x), the methodological review CG actually cite for the contrastive-inference paradigm.

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/CohnGordonEtAl2019.lean`**: rewired onto the graduated `IncrementalSemantics` bundle. Three triplicated definition blocks (Figure 1 scene + Sedivy contrast scene + Rubio-Fernأ،ndez STOP scene) collapsed into three one-line bundle instantiations (`figureOne`, `SedivyScene.sedivyBundle`, `RubioFernandezScene.rfBundle`) plus three `noncomputable def incRSA := bundle.toRSAConfig` derivations. **آ§2.4 case-analysis replaced**: `greedyUnroll_weakly_informative` now invokes the generic `figureOne.l0Utt_ge_inv_card` once per call site, with the bundle-side proof carrying the combinatorics â€” replaces the earlier 3-case `cases r` enumeration that re-derived the bound per referent. **آ§8 D&R bridge deleted**: removed the `incRSA_s1Score_is_L0 := rfl` thin theorem, the editorial Dale-Reiter table, and the `import Linglib.Phenomena.Reference.Studies.DaleReiter1995` â€” the No-Brevity classification is already true by construction once `toRSAConfig` is the canonical builder, and the editorial alignment table belongs in prose, not Lean. **آ§3.2 citation fix**: replaced hallucinated `@cite{sedivy-etal-1999}` provenance for the contrastive-inference scene with `@cite{sedivy-2007}` (the review CG actually cite); kept `sedivy-etal-1999` mention in the docstring as the editorial empirical bridge to the original 1999 study. All 7 Findings (`adj_first_for_target` through `incremental_prefers_bare_noun`) preserved and close via `rsa_predict` against the bundle-derived configs (struct projection + def unfolding reduces correctly through `toRSAConfig`). `Linglib.lean` updated to import the new theory module before `Quantities`.

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/CohnGordonEtAl2019.lean`**: completed the audit of @cite{cohn-gordon-goodman-potts-2019} formalisation against the paper, fixing two hallucinations and adding four sections for previously unmodelled paper content. **Hallucinations fixed**: dropped the false `@cite{dale-reiter-1995}` provenance from the module docstring header (Cohn-Gordon does not cite Dale-Reiter â€” the آ§8 conceptual comparison is editorial); replaced the content-free `incremental_rsa_is_no_brevity` (which was `rfl` over a stipulated table â€” the "encoding conclusions as definitions" anti-pattern called out in CLAUDE.md) with `incRSA_s1Score_is_L0`, a real theorem about the actual `incRSA` proving its `s1Score` is literally L0 with no cost re-weighting (the formal sense in which `incRSA` is in the No-Brevity regime). **آ§7b weakly-informative greedy unrolling (paper آ§2.4)**: added `l0Utt` (literal listener over complete utterances), `greedyUnroll : Referent â†’ List Word` (Figure 1 scene), and the bound theorem `greedyUnroll_weakly_informative : âˆ€ r, l0Utt (greedyUnroll r) r â‰¥ 1/3` proving the architectural sanity property the paper establishes â€” even without a global view of the utterance space, greedy chain-rule unrolling cannot do worse than uniform. Proof factors through a `incSem_val` private lemma and `decide` on the `trueExtCount`/`viableExtCount` natural-number computations. **آ§9 global RSA + divergence theorem (paper آ§2.4)**: added `inductive Utterance := dress | redDress | redObject` (the three complete utterances treated as atomic options), `globalRSA : RSAConfig Utterance Referent` (one-shot, خ±=1, no cost), and `global_prefers_red_dress : globalRSA.S1 () .redDress .redDress > globalRSA.S1 () .redDress .dress` proving the standard-RSA preference for the more-informative full utterance â€” the central empirical wedge between global and incremental architectures, opposite of Finding 7 (`incremental_prefers_bare_noun`). **آ§10 Sedivy آ§3.2 paradigm bridge**: added `namespace SedivyScene` containing the contrast-condition reference game from @cite{sedivy-etal-1999} (5 words: tall/short/cup/pitcher/key; 4 referents: tallCup/shortCup/tallPitcher/key; 6 complete utterances including bare nouns), `incRSA_sedivy : RSAConfig Word Referent` (same architecture as the main `incRSA`, only the lexicon differs), and the prediction theorem `sedivy_contrast_inference : incRSA_sedivy.L1 .tall .tallCup > incRSA_sedivy.L1 .tall .tallPitcher` (3/5 vs 2/5) â€” the anticipatory contrastive-inference effect. Bridge to `Paradigms.VisualWorld` via `sedivyLooksAfterTall : ObjectRole â†’ â„‌` mapping each role to the L1 posterior on the corresponding referent, plus `sedivyLooksAfterTall_target_gt_competitor` restating the prediction in paradigm vocabulary. Honest scope statement in docstring: only the contrast condition is modelled, so a full `SedivyEtAl1999.LookPrediction Cell` typed by the `HasContrastCondition` lens is out of scope (would require a no-contrast variant model that Cohn-Gordon does not separately construct). **آ§11 Rubio-Fernأ،ndez آ§3.1 STOP machinery (paper آ§3.1)**: added `namespace RubioFernandezScene` with `inductive Word := dress | hat | red | blue | stop`, `inductive Referent := redDress | blueHat`, `wordApplies` (with `stop` not applying to any referent), `completeUtterances` (all four terminating in `stop`), and structural invariants `completeUtterances_terminate_in_stop` and `stop_never_applies` (proved by `decide`). Cost-dependent over-modification theorem (`S1^UTT-IP(red dress, STOP | R1) > S1^UTT-IP(dress, STOP | R1)` for some cost regime) deferred â€” formalising it requires `Real.exp` over a cost schedule and a quantitative argument that doesn't reduce via `rsa_predict`; structural framework is in place for future work. **Citation cleanup**: rewrote module docstring header to position cross-study connections (Sedivy 1999, Rubio-Fernأ،ndez 2016) explicitly and acknowledge the آ§8 D&R comparison is editorial. New imports: `Linglib.Paradigms.VisualWorld`. Build clean: 5201 jobs, 0 new errors. New `rsa_predict` invocations: 2 (`global_prefers_red_dress`, `sedivy_contrast_inference`) â€” the latter is the largest model (4 referents أ— 5 words = 20 cells, sequential context = `List Word`).

## [0.230.29] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Theories/Semantics/Questions/Answerhood/ProbabilisticAnswerhood.lean`**: three `of_witness` constructor lemmas (`relevantS.of_witness`, `probAnswersS.of_witness`, `someResolutionStrengthenedS.of_witness`) absorb the `convert h` boilerplate that bridges the `Classical.propDecidable` instances baked into the `noncomputable` `open Classical in` predicate bodies with the user-supplied structural `[DecidablePred]` instances at consumer sites. Rationale: `relevantS`/`probAnswersS`/`someResolutionStrengthenedS` deliberately use Classical decidability so the spec body type-checks without per-alternative `[DecidablePred]` hypotheses (avoiding a `[âˆ€ a, DecidablePred (آ· âˆˆ a)]` quantifier that's too universal to synthesize); concrete consumers compute `condProbSet`/`probOfSet` values with their own structural instances, and the two instance choices agree because `Decidable` is a `Subsingleton`. Each helper takes the alternative `a` plus a structural-instance proof of the inequality and produces the existential witness with the Classical-instance form. The added module-level docstring section "Witness constructors â€” Classical/structural Decidable bridge" documents the call-site pattern (`refine probAnswersS.of_witness s q prior a hAltMem ?_; rw [...]; norm_num`) and notes that destructuring (`obtain` + `convert hStr`) on the negative direction in infelicity proofs remains the residual idiom (because the destructured hypothesis carries Classical instances and must be bridged at use sites). New infrastructure available for future consumers; existing Thomas2026 proofs unchanged. Build clean: 973 jobs through `ProbabilisticAnswerhood`, 1012 jobs through `Thomas2026`.

## [0.230.28] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Constraint/` consolidation**: unified scattered OT machinery under `Core/Constraint/` with mathlib-style sub-namespaces. **Moves**: `Core/Logic/ConstraintEvaluation.lean` â†’ `Core/Constraint/Evaluation.lean` (namespace `Core.Constraint.Evaluation`); `Core/Logic/OT.lean` â†’ `Core/Constraint/OT/Basic.lean` (namespace `Core.Constraint.OT`); `Core/OT/{Defs.lean, ERC.lean}` merged â†’ `Core/Constraint/OT/ERC.lean`; `Core/OT/{Antimatroid.lean, ERCAntimatroid.lean}` merged â†’ `Core/Constraint/OT/Antimatroid.lean`; old `Core/OT/` directory deleted. **Rationale**: prior layout scattered constraint/OT code across `Core/Logic/`, `Core/OT/`, and `Core/Constraint/`; the sub-namespace approach (mathlib pattern: flat top + named subpackages) consolidates without the `LexProfile` collision that a flat namespace would have caused (`Core.Constraint.LexProfile` is an abbrev for `Lex (Fin n â†’ خ²)` while the evaluation-side struct lives at `Core.Constraint.Evaluation.LexProfile`). **Consumer updates**: 47 files updated (33 `open Core.OT` â†’ `open Core.Constraint.OT`; ~16 `open Core.ConstraintEvaluation` â†’ `open Core.Constraint.Evaluation`; ~15 `import Linglib.Core.Logic.OT` â†’ `import Linglib.Core.Constraint.OT.Basic`; ~7 `import Linglib.Core.Logic.ConstraintEvaluation` â†’ `import Linglib.Core.Constraint.Evaluation`; qualified refs `Core.OT.NamedConstraint`, `Core.OT.ConstraintFamily` in Belth2026.lean; docstring references in Profile.lean/Weighted.lean/SiptarTorkenczy2000.lean/Krifka2007.lean/Wang2023.lean). Build clean: 5201 jobs, 0 errors.

## [0.230.27] - 2026-04-19

### Fixed
- **`Theories/Pragmatics/Particles/Additive.lean`** + **`Phenomena/Focus/AdditiveParticles/Studies/Thomas2026.lean`**: closed all 5 outstanding `sorry`s in the Thomas2026 study and fixed an over-strong quantification in `maximalityCondition`. **Spec fix**: the Set/Prop port of `maximalityCondition` quantified the witnesses-capture obligation over *every* alternative `a âˆˆ Issue.alt RQ`; the original Bool spec only iterated over the *strengthened* resolutions (those where `P(a | ANT âˆ© د€) > P(a | ANT)`). Re-stated the predicate as `âˆ€ a âˆˆ alt, strengthened a â†’ âˆ€ w, prior w > 0 â†’ w âˆˆ ANT â†’ w âˆˆ a â†’ w âˆˆ د€` so the obligation is vacuous on unstrengthened cells. **Proofs**: structural derivations over `Set (Fin 4)` for all 5 verification theorems â€” `hotel_too_felicitous` (felicitous, polar question, all four conditions), `ecstatic_too_infelicitous` (3-cell `ofList` partition, non-triviality fails because د€ entails the only strengthened cell), `pizza_spaghetti_too_felicitous` (4-cell `ofList` partition, all four conditions including maximality with three vacuous branches), `cello_too_infelicitous` (polar question, maximality fails because `wâ‚پ âˆˆ ANT âˆ© baileyPlays \ baileyPlaysCello`), `cross_product_too_infelicitous` (polar question, conjunction-strengthening half fails because both alternatives stay at conditional-probability extremes). Each scenario's proof rests on three layers of helpers: per-set `DecidablePred` instances enabling `Fin.sum_univ_four`-driven probability computations, named lemmas for each `probOfSet` / `condProbSet` value, and an `alt`-characterisation lemma (`Issue.alt_polar_of_nontrivial` for polar contexts, `Issue.alt_ofList_of_pairwise_disjoint_nonempty` for partition contexts). The `convert h` idiom bridges the `Classical.propDecidable` instances baked into `noncomputable` predicates with the user-supplied structural ones via `Subsingleton (Decidable P)`. Build clean: 1012 jobs in the Thomas2026 dependency cone, 0 sorries remaining in this file.

## [0.230.26] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Paradigms/VisualWorld.lean`** + **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/SedivyEtAl1999.lean`**: made the paradigm typeclasses *load-bearing* by giving `HasContrastCondition` and `HasTask` proper lens APIs and lifting the qualitative empirical patterns to the paradigm level. **Lensified typeclasses**: `HasContrastCondition` now carries `contrastOf : Cell â†’ ContrastCondition`, `setContrast : ContrastCondition â†’ Cell â†’ Cell`, and three lens laws as fields (`contrastOf_setContrast`, `setContrast_contrastOf`, `setContrast_setContrast`); same shape for `HasTask` (`taskOf`/`setTask` + 3 laws). The lens laws are what make the typeclasses load-bearing â€” they let paradigm-level predicates universally quantify over a base cell and apply the lens to swap the manipulated factor, so any study whose `Cell` instances the class inherits the predicates without re-deriving them per design. `HasDisplayKind` deliberately stays projection-only (display kind is between-study constant, not within-study manipulation; documented). **Paradigm-level predicates** (new): `ContrastSpeedsResponse (rt : RTPrediction Cell) := âˆ€ c, rt (setContrast .contrast c) < rt (setContrast .noContrast c)`, `ContrastReducesRoleLooks (role : ObjectRole) (looks : LookPrediction Cell) := âˆ€ c, looks role (setContrast .contrast c) < looks role (setContrast .noContrast c)`, abbrev `ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` specialising `role := .crossCategoryCompetitor`, and `ContrastSpeedsTargetLooks` (the dual, target-look acceleration from Sedivy Tables 7, 11). **`ObjectRole` expanded** from 4 to 7 constructors per Huettig et al. 2011 آ§2.1.2: added `cohortCompetitor`, `rhymeCompetitor`, `semanticCompetitor` to cover Allopenna et al. 1998 + Huettig & Altmann 2005 paradigms (extending the type rather than encoding role distinctions in study-local enums). **Sedivy migration**: `Cell` now derives `Fintype` (added `Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Sigma` import); `HasContrastCondition Cell` and `HasTask Cell` instances now provide structure-update lens implementations (`{ c with contrast := k }`) with `rfl` proofs of all three lens laws; new `HasDisplayKind Cell` instance pinning Sedivy's displays to `.objectArray`. **Predicate cleanup**: dropped local `PredictsContrastSpeedsTarget` and `PredictsContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` (superseded by paradigm-level versions); kept study-specific `ContrastAttenuatesTypicality` (typicality isn't a paradigm primitive â€” it's Sedivy's operationalisation of "exemplar goodness" via the norming study). `SatisfiesSedivyPattern` now bundles the paradigm-level `ContrastSpeedsResponse`/`ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` + study-specific `ContrastAttenuatesTypicality`. `trivial_satisfies_pattern` non-vacuity witness re-proved against the new lens-based predicates by destructuring the cell and casing on `typicality`. **Empirical discriminator surfaced**: the lens-based predicates *enforce* task invariance by quantifying over `c` while `setContrast` preserves `c.task` â€” so any theory satisfying the patterns is committed to the task-invariant mechanism that Sedivy Exp 3 uses to discriminate the lexical comparison-class account from the presupposition account. Build clean: 5203 jobs, 0 errors.

## [0.230.25] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Linglib/Paradigms/`** (new top-level directory) + **`Paradigms/VisualWorld.lean`** + **CLAUDE.md** "Paradigms/ - Experimental-Task Contract Layer" section: introduces a fifth top-level architectural layer sitting between `Theories/` and `Phenomena/Studies/`. **Architectural role**: paradigms are the *contract layer* â€” they specify the typed input an experiment provides (display kind, condition cells, object roles) and the typed shape of output a theory must produce (RT prediction, look prediction). Theories themselves remain paradigm-agnostic; bridge theorems in `Studies/` translate theory-native predictions into paradigm-typed predictions and prove the qualitative patterns documented in the study file. Dependency-wise `Paradigms/` depends on `Core/` only and is a sibling of `Theories/`/`Fragments/` rather than nested under `Phenomena/` (which would have buried it among ~76 phenomenon directories). **Anchoring discipline**: each `Paradigms/X.lean` file is anchored on a methodological review or meta-analysis of paradigm X, with the review's data-field ontology serving as the file's type structure â€” no invented categories. **`VisualWorld.lean`** anchored on @cite{huettig-rommers-meyer-2011} (Acta Psychologica 137(2): 151â€“171, doi:10.1016/j.actpsy.2010.11.003), with each type citing the section of the review it comes from: `DisplayKind` (semiRealisticScene/objectArray/printedWord, آ§2.1.1), `ObjectRole` (target/contrastingObject/crossCategoryCompetitor/distractor), `ContrastCondition` (contrast/noContrast), `Task` (directAction/lookAndListen/verification/description, آ§2.1.3), `RTPrediction (Cell : Type) := Cell â†’ â„ڑ`, `LookPrediction (Cell : Type) := ObjectRole â†’ Cell â†’ â„ڑ` (note the typed `ObjectRole` slot â€” predicates over look predictions can therefore make role-specific claims at the type level), plus `HasContrastCondition`/`HasTask`/`HasDisplayKind` typeclasses for studies to extend with their own condition cells. Per the CLAUDE.md "Processing scope" rule, deliberately omits stimulus-timing details (preview duration), participant population metadata, data-analysis pipeline (ROIs, time windows, ANOVA vs. growth curves), and eye-tracker apparatus enums â€” paradigms specify the *experimental contract*, not the *measurement apparatus* or *analysis pipeline*. New bib entry `huettig-rommers-meyer-2011` (subfield `semantics/methodology`, role `foundational`). Added `Linglib.Paradigms.VisualWorld` import to `Linglib.lean` between `Tactics/` and `Fragments/` blocks. Updated CLAUDE.md layered diagram, directory structure, and added "Paradigms/" subsection documenting the contract-layer role and review-anchoring discipline.

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/SedivyEtAl1999.lean`** migrated to consume `Paradigms.VisualWorld`. **Removals**: dropped local `ObjectRole` (was dead code â€” defined but never used in the file), local `ContrastCondition`, local `Task` (now imported with the canonical four-element enum from Huettig et al. 2011 آ§2.1.3), local `RTPrediction`/`LookPrediction` abbreviations. **Renamings**: `Trial` â†’ `Cell` (per the per-cell-not-per-trial naming critique â€” the structure represents a cell in the 2أ—2أ—2 design, not a single eye-tracking observation). **Type-safety improvement**: `LookPrediction` now has the paradigm-typed shape `ObjectRole â†’ Cell â†’ â„ڑ`; `PredictsContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` selects `looks .crossCategoryCompetitor` at the type level rather than relying on a docstring convention that the prediction function silently models competitor looks. **New typeclass instances**: `HasContrastCondition Cell` and `HasTask Cell`, hooking the study's condition cells into the paradigm's typeclass machinery. **New theorem `adjDomain_requires_comparison_class`**: the size domain requires comparison-class binding, derived by `rfl` from `Core.PropertyDomain.requiresComparisonClass` rather than asserted in prose â€” making the load-bearing connection between Sedivy's empirical paradigm and the comparison-class typology in `Core.PropertyDomain` machine-checkable. **New non-vacuity witness**: `trivialRT`/`trivialLooks` + `trivial_satisfies_pattern` proves `SatisfiesSedivyPattern` is satisfiable (without this the structure could in principle be uninhabited and the empirical contract could be vacuous). **Proof-style cleanups**: `unfold X; norm_num` â†’ `norm_num [X]` idiom for the two norming-verification theorems. Cross-study consumers (`EngelhardtEtAl2006.lean`, `RonderosEtAl2024.lean`) reference `SedivyEtAl1999.adjDomain` (kept) and don't reference any of the dropped types â€” both build clean unchanged. Build clean: 5203 jobs, 0 errors.

## [0.230.24] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Computability/Subregular/Tier.lean`** + **`Theories/Phonology/Subregular/TierProjection.lean`**: collapsed the two-tier-formalisms duplication onto a single source of truth. **Before**: `Subregular.tierProject T xs := xs.filter (decide âˆک T)` and `Core.Tier.apply (Core.Tier.byClass p) xs := List.filterMap (fun y => if p y then some y else none) xs` were definitionally distinct (`List.filter` vs `List.filterMap`) but operationally identical on class-membership tiers, with `0.230.20`'s `apply_byClass_eq_tierProject` discharging the equality via `Tier.apply_byClass`. **After**: `tierProject T xs` is defined directly as `Core.Tier.apply (Core.Tier.byClass (fun x => decide (T x))) xs` â€” a definitional alias that commits the codebase to `Core.Tier` (in `Core/StringHom.lean`) as the single primitive for tier projection. The bridge theorem `apply_byClass_eq_tierProject` now reduces to `rfl`. New helper lemma `tierProject_eq_filter` (`tierProject T xs = xs.filter (decide âˆک T)`) preserves Lambert's filter-form characterisation as a public theorem rather than the definition. The simp lemmas `tierProject_nil` (still `rfl`), `tierProject_cons` (re-proved via `tierProject_eq_filter`), and `tierProject_univ` (re-proved via `tierProject_eq_filter`) keep their existing call-sites working unchanged. `tierProject_ofByClass` in `TierProjection.lean` re-proved via `tierProject_eq_filter` + `Tier.apply_byClass`. Added `import Linglib.Core.StringHom` to `Subregular/Tier.lean`. Mathlib-style payoff: phonology code that already uses `Core.Tier` (autosegmental tonal/featural tiers, the harmony machinery, `mkOCPOnTier` via `Tier.apply`) and subregular code that uses `tierProject` (TSL grammars, `OCP.TSLGrammar.ocp`, `Hierarchy.lean`'s `IsStrictlyLocal.toIsTierStrictlyLocal`) now share a single underlying primitive â€” no parallel formalisms to keep in sync, and the autosegmentalâ†”subregular bridge is no longer a theorem to discharge but a definitional consequence. Build clean across all five affected files (`Tier.lean`, `Hierarchy.lean`, `TierProjection.lean`, `OCP.lean`, `OptimalityTheory/Constraints.lean`).

## [0.230.23] - 2026-04-19

### Removed
- **`Core/Inquisitive.lean`**: deleted the legacy Hamblin `Discourse.Issue` type (a `structure { alternatives : List (InfoState W) }` with `Issue.polar`/`Issue.ofAlternatives`/`Issue.toQUD`/`Issue.infoContent`/`Issue.highlighted`/`Issue.isPartition`). After the 0.229.993 â†’ 0.230.21 migration of all consumers (Roberts2012, Thomas2026, IppolitoKissWilliams2025, TheilerRoelofsenAloni2018, Additive, Support, ProbabilisticAnswerhood, Relevance, DecisionTheoretic.Core, DiscourseOnly, â€¦) onto the bundled mathlib-aligned `Core.Issue W` (`Set (Set W)` with `SetLike` + `CompleteDistribLattice` + `HasAltList` finiteness witness), the file had zero live consumers â€” only its own internal references remained. Removed the import from `Linglib.lean`; cleaned vestigial `open Discourse` statements from four migrated files (`Theories/Pragmatics/Particles/Additive.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Questions/Answerhood/{Support, ProbabilisticAnswerhood}.lean`, `Phenomena/Focus/AdditiveParticles/Studies/Thomas2026.lean`); rewrote two stale docstrings (`Core/FinitePMF.lean`, `Core/Issue/Relevance.lean`) that named the removed type. Net: 1 file deleted (~125 lines), single source of truth for inquisitive content. `Core/InfoState.lean` (the `Set W` propositional algebra alias `InfoState W := Set W` plus `absurdState`/`trivialState`/`supports`/`propEntails`) is independent of the deleted type and stays. Build clean: 5202 jobs (was 5203), 0 errors.

## [0.230.22] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Theories/Phonology/Subregular/OCP.lean`**: second new bridge under the phase-G `Subregular/` umbrella, connecting the Obligatory Contour Principle (the prototypical phonological dissimilation constraint, expressed in OT as a markedness constraint via `mkOCPOnTier` in `OptimalityTheory/Constraints.lean`) to the subregular complexity class TSL_2 (`Core.Computability.Subregular.TSLGrammar`). **`ocpForbidden خ± : Set (Augmented خ±)`**: the forbidden 2-factor set `{[some x, some x] | x : خ±}`, parameterised over the alphabet â€” boundary-adjacent factors `[none, some x]` and `[some x, none]` are deliberately excluded so the OCP penalises only interior adjacent identicals. **`TSLGrammar.ocp (p : خ± â†’ Bool) : TSLGrammar 2 خ±`**: the TSL_2 grammar with tier predicate `(p آ· = true)` and permitted set `(ocpForbidden خ±)ل¶œ`, capturing "no two adjacent identical symbols on the tier defined by `p`." **`adjacentIdentical_eq_zero_iff_isChain`**: the OT score's 0-violation condition reformulated as the mathlib-idiomatic relational chain `List.IsChain (آ· â‰  آ·)` â€” proved by structural induction with `List.isChain_cons_cons`, requiring `[BEq خ±] [LawfulBEq خ±]`. **`mkOCPOnTier_zero_iff_isChain`** (the relational-form bridge, fully proved): a candidate's OCP score is zero iff its raw string projects under `Tier.byClass p` to a list with no two adjacent identical elements; the proof composes the previous lemma with `Tier.apply_byClass`. **`mkOCPOnTier_zero_iff_in_ocp_lang`** (the full TSL_2 language-form bridge, stated with `sorry` + TODO): a candidate's OCP score is zero iff its raw string is in `(TSLGrammar.ocp p).lang`. The remaining proof step needs to characterise `kFactors 2 (boundary 2 ys)` as the consecutive-pair list and show that "no factor lies in `ocpForbidden`" iff `IsChain (آ· â‰  آ·) ys`. New file lives in `Theories/Phonology/Subregular/`, sitting next to `TierProjection.lean`. 1 new file (~99 lines), 1 new sorry (the language-form bridge â€” relational form is fully proved), builds clean against the full library.

### Fixed
- **`Core/Computability/Subregular/Hierarchy.lean`** + **`Core/Computability/Subregular/Tier.lean`**: hallucinated section/figure references to @cite{lambert-2022} corrected. `Hierarchy.lean` previously cited "Lambert (2022) Figure 3.1" for the piecewise-local subregular hierarchy â€” the correct location is **Figure 2.3** (Ch 2 on language-theoretic preliminaries). `Tier.lean` previously cited "Lambert آ§3.4" for the segmental-tier formulation of TSL â€” Lambert's Ch 3 has آ§3.1 (model-theoretic descriptions), آ§3.2 (language-theoretic, with subsections on SL/LT/LTT/Piecewise) and آ§3.3 (automata); آ§3.4 doesn't exist. Updated to cite **Ch 3 آ§3.2** and describe the formal content (erasing string homomorphism keeping tier symbols) rather than locating by section number alone.

## [0.230.21] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Theories/Semantics/Questions/Answerhood/Support.lean` + `Theories/Pragmatics/Particles/Additive.lean` + `Phenomena/Focus/AdditiveParticles/Studies/Thomas2026.lean`**: continued Boolâ†’Set/Prop migration, eliminating the legacy `Discourse.Issue` (List of `W â†’ Bool`) consumers in the additive-particles subtree. **Support.lean** rewritten around `Core.Issue W` + `Set W`: `probSupportsS` (wraps `isPositiveEvidenceS`) + `noncomputable fullSupportS` (IKW Def 13: `âˆƒ q âˆˆ Issue.alt sentDen, dox âٹ† q âˆ§ isPositiveEvidenceS q answer prior`) + `fullSupport_fails_unbelievedS` (info-seeking interrogatives block discourse *only* via doxastic failure, regardless of probabilistic component). 218â†’75 lines. **Additive.lean** rewritten as `AdditiveContext W` (resolvedQuestion : Core.Issue W, antecedent : Set W, prior : Prior W) with four `noncomputable` Prop felicity conditions: `antecedentCondition` (probAnswersS), `conjunctionCondition` (probAnswers âˆ§ someResolutionStrengthenedS), `nonTrivialityCondition`, `maximalityCondition` (universal closure over Issue.alt). `tooFelicitous = ant âˆ§ conj âˆ§ nonTrivial âˆ§ maxim`. New helper lemma `condProb_eq_one_of_entails`; `standard_use_reduction`/`cumulative_evidence_necessary`/`irrelevant_prejacent_infelicitous`/`argumentBuilding_requires_implicit_qud` proven structurally; `heim_subsumption` `sorry`'d with TODO (decidability instance divergence between `Set.decidableInter` and `Classical.dec` blocks the rewrite â€” same Subsingleton, not definitionally equal). 521â†’390 lines. **Thomas2026.lean** converted from 5 `native_decide` Bool theorems to Prop verification theorems over `Set (Fin 4)` worlds; issues built via `Core.Issue.polar`/`Core.Issue.ofList`; 5 `sorry`'d with TODO (was `native_decide` over Bool API; spec predicates are now `noncomputable` so structural proofs are required). Net: 489 lines deleted, 285 added across the three files. Full library builds clean (5203 jobs, 0 errors).

## [0.230.20] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Theories/Phonology/Subregular/TierProjection.lean`**: phase G of the mathlib-shaped Phonology regrouping â€” first new bridge connecting the autosegmental tier formalism (`Core.Tier`, the Kleisli morphism `خ± â†’ Option خ²` lifted via `List.filterMap`) to the subregular TSL formalism (`Core.Computability.Subregular.tierProject`, the predicate-filter `xs.filter (decide âˆک T)`). The two are operationally identical in their shared common case â€” a class-membership tier that keeps symbols of one type without relabeling â€” and the bridge makes the connection explicit so phonological tiers (autosegmental tonal tier, sibilant tier, nasal tier) can be reused as the segmental tier of a TSL_k grammar without restating the projection. **Main theorem `apply_byClass_eq_tierProject`**: for any `T : خ± â†’ Prop` `[DecidablePred T]` and `xs : List خ±`, `Tier.apply (Tier.byClass (decide âˆک T)) xs = tierProject T xs` â€” both reduce to `List.filter`. **Adapter `TSLGrammar.ofByClass`**: builds a `TSLGrammar k خ±` from a `Bool` predicate `p : خ± â†’ Bool` and a permitted-factor set; the resulting tier predicate is `(p آ· = true)`. **`tierProject_ofByClass` simp lemma**: `tierProject (TSLGrammar.ofByClass k p âˆ…).tier = Tier.apply (Tier.byClass p)`. New file lives in `Theories/Phonology/Subregular/`, opening the umbrella for follow-up bridges (planned: OCP-as-TSL_2 forbidding adjacent identical interior factors on the projected tier; long-distance vowel/consonant harmony as TSL_2 over a featural tier; word-final neutralization as D_2 from `Subregular/Definite.lean`). 1 new file (~95 lines), 0 sorrys, 0 warnings, builds clean against the full library.

## [0.230.19] - 2026-04-19

Two subdirectories and one standalone file moved from the flat `Theories/Phonology/` top level into a new `Process/` umbrella that clusters the derivational/process-oriented machinery: `RuleBased/` â†’ `Process/RuleBased/` (subdir with `Defs.lean`: SPE-style rewrite-rule infrastructure); `Alternation.lean` â†’ `Process/Alternation.lean` (input-output alternation patterns); `Harmony/` â†’ `Process/Harmony/` (subdir with `Defs.lean` + `OT.lean`: vowel/consonant harmony engines, both rule-based and OT-based). Path-only refactor: namespaces preserved to avoid the per-symbol cascade; consumer imports updated across all 16 affected files (`Phenomena/{VowelHarmony, PhonologicalAlternation, CompensatoryLengthening, Phonotactics, Phonology}/Studies/`, `Fragments/{Akan, Finnish (أ—2), English, Turkish, Hungarian, Korean}/`, `Theories/Phonology/Process/Harmony/{Defs, OT}.lean` self-imports); `Linglib.lean` reorganized so all four Process files cluster contiguously, immediately after the `Prosodic/` block. Build clean across the moved files and their consumers.

## [0.230.15] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Computability/Subregular/`**: deep mathlib-quality refactor of all 7 files (B3 + B4) per a self-review that flagged 22 idiom drift issues. **Basic.lean**: copyright header added; `kFactorsList k xs` rewritten as `(xs.tails.filter (k â‰¤ آ·.length)).map (آ·.take k)` and renamed `kFactors` so it composes with mathlib's existing `List.tails`/`List.filter`/`List.take` rather than recursing structurally; the redundant `lift : List خ± â†’ Augmented خ±` alias removed in favor of inline `w.map some`; `lift_nil`/`lift_cons` deleted (subsumed by `List.map_nil`/`map_cons`); `boundary_zero` deleted in favor of the more useful `boundary_one`; the private `isInfix_cons_right` helper deleted in favor of Init's `IsPrefix.isInfix`/`IsInfix.trans`; `length_of_mem_kFactors`/`isInfix_of_mem_kFactors` reproven structurally via `List.mem_map`/`List.mem_filter`/`List.mem_tails`/`List.take_prefix` (3-line proofs vs the previous 12-line `induction xs with`/`by_cases hk` traversals); `Type u`/`universe u` switched to `Type*`. **StrictlyLocal.lean**: `IsSLk` â†’ `IsStrictlyLocal`; `SLGrammar.isSLk_lang` â†’ `SLGrammar.isStrictlyLocal_lang`; `@[ext]` added to `SLGrammar`; `Inhabited` instance added; the namespace-shadowing `SLGrammar.univ`/`empty` and the awkward `mem_empty_lang_iff` (whose proof contorted around `Set.notMem_empty`) deleted (lattice-style top/bot deferred to a later commit when `OrderTop`/`OrderBot` instances are wired); `mem_ofForbidden_lang` proof tightened from `simp [ofForbidden]` (with linter-flagged `lang` arg) to `simp only [mem_lang, ofForbidden, Set.mem_compl_iff]`. **LocallyTestable.lean**: `IsLTk`/`IsLTTk` â†’ `IsLocallyTestable`/`IsLocallyThresholdTestable`; `mem_factorSet : f âˆˆ factorSet k w â†” f âˆˆ kFactors k (boundary k w)` added as a `@[simp]` characterization; banner-comment dividers replaced with markdown `## Main definitions`. **Tier.lean** (biggest change): the structurally-recursive `project T xs := match xs with | [] => [] | x :: xs => if T x then ...` rewritten as `tierProject T xs := xs.filter (decide âˆک T)` â€” `project_nil`/`_cons_pos`/`_cons_neg` collapse into the single `tierProject_cons` characterization (built from `List.filter_cons`); `decTier : DecidablePred tier` lifted to an instance field via `[decTier : DecidablePred tier]` + `attribute [instance] TSLGrammar.decTier`, eliminating the `have := G.decTier` boilerplate at every projection use site; `IsTSLk` (was nested under `namespace TSLGrammar`) â†’ top-level `IsTierStrictlyLocal`; `mem_lang` simp lemma added. **Hierarchy.lean**: `IsSLk.toIsLTk` â†’ `IsStrictlyLocal.toIsLocallyTestable`; `IsSLk.toIsTSLk` â†’ `IsStrictlyLocal.toIsTierStrictlyLocal`; `IsLTk.toIsLTTk` â†’ `IsLocallyTestable.toIsLocallyThresholdTestable`; the SLâ†’TSL proof simplified via `ext` + `simp only [TSLGrammar.mem_lang, SLGrammar.mem_lang, tierProject_univ]` instead of `funext` + `show ... = ...` + multi-arg `simp only`; the `key : âˆ€ w, w âˆˆ G.lang â†” âˆ€ f âˆˆ factorSet k w, f âˆˆ G.permitted` lemma kept inline (the proof is `Iff.rfl` so extracting a top-level lemma adds no clarity). **Piecewise.lean**: copyright header; `IsSPk` â†’ `IsStrictlyPiecewise`; `Sublist` notation `<+` made available via `open List`; `@[ext]` + `Inhabited` instance added. **Definite.lean** (parameterization refactor): `DefiniteGrammar`/`ReverseDefiniteGrammar` unified under a single `EdgeDefiniteGrammar k e خ±` parameterized over a new `Edge` enum (`.left | .right`) with `Edge.takeAt e k xs` dispatching to `xs.take k` vs `xs.drop (xs.length - k)`; `DefiniteGrammar k خ± := EdgeDefiniteGrammar k .right خ±` and `ReverseDefiniteGrammar k خ± := EdgeDefiniteGrammar k .left خ±` are now `abbrev`s; `IsDefiniteK`/`IsReverseDefiniteK` â†’ `IsDefinite`/`IsReverseDefinite`; the `DefiniteGrammar.lastK` helper folded into `Edge.takeAt`; the duplicated `lang`/`mem_lang`/`univ`/`lang_univ`/`isDefiniteK_lang` for `ReverseDefiniteGrammar` eliminated. Net effect: 7 files, ~30% LOC reduction overall, all proofs unchanged or shorter, kFactors and tierProject directly compose with mathlib's `List.filter`/`List.tails`/`List.take` infrastructure, no downstream consumers (these files were brand-new at 0.230.11/0.230.14 with no callers yet). Build clean, 0 sorrys, 0 warnings on the 7 refactored files.

## [0.230.14] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Core/Computability/Subregular/{Piecewise, Definite}.lean`**: precedence-based and edge-based subregular classes (B4 of the mathlib-shaped Subregular family begun in 0.230.11). `Piecewise.lean` defines `SPGrammar k خ±` (set of permitted length-`k` *subsequences* â€” non-contiguous selections, no boundary augmentation since position-blind) with `SPGrammar.lang w := âˆ€ s, s.length = k â†’ List.Sublist s w â†’ s âˆˆ G.permitted`, the `IsSPk` property, and a trivial `SPGrammar.univ` accepting all strings. SP_k captures long-distance precedence patterns (sibilant harmony as forbidden length-2 subsequence `[s, تƒ]`) where SL_k's contiguous factors cannot, per @cite{rogers-pullum-2011} @cite{lambert-2022}. `Definite.lean` defines `DefiniteGrammar k خ±` (suffix-based, with `lastK k xs := xs.drop (xs.length - k)` for the length-`k` suffix; truncating `Nat` subtraction handles short strings as their own suffix) and `ReverseDefiniteGrammar k خ±` (prefix-based via `List.take k`), each with `lang`/`mem_lang`/`univ`/`lang_univ`/`IsXxxK` and `_isXxxK_lang` constructor witnesses. D_k and RD_k are weaker than SL_k (look at one fixed edge position rather than every contiguous window) but foundational for word-final neutralization (D_1, D_2) and word-initial prosodic templates (RD_k). Following the SL convention, the classical definite vs strictly-definite distinction is collapsed to a single generative formulation. Both files wired into `Linglib.lean`. 2 new files, 0 sorrys, 0 warnings.

## [0.230.13] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/CausalBayesNet.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of the five validity/correlation predicates on `NoisyOR` and `WorldState`. `NoisyOR.isValid (n) : Prop := 0 â‰¤ n.background âˆ§ n.background â‰¤ 1 âˆ§ 0 â‰¤ n.background + n.power âˆ§ n.background + n.power â‰¤ 1` (was `Bool := decide (...) && decide (...) && ...`); `WorldState.isValid (w) : Prop` likewise inlined as a 7-way conjunction (was a `&&`-chain over `decide` wrappers); `WorldState.isIndependent (w) : Prop := w.pAC = w.pA * w.pC` (was `Bool := w.pAC == w.pA * w.pC`); `WorldState.isPositivelyCorrelated`/`isNegativelyCorrelated` likewise drop the `==` â†” `=` and `>`/`<` Bool wrappers in favor of `Prop`. All five carry `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (...))`. The duplicate `WorldState.IsValid` (capital-I `Prop` mirror that previously coexisted with the `Bool` version) is removed â€” `isValid` is now the single Prop-valued source of truth. Six internal theorems migrate (`valid_implies_pA_bounded`/`pC_bounded`/`pAC_bounded`/`pCGivenA_bounded`/`pCGivenNotA_bounded`/`law_of_total_probability`) â€” hypothesis types switch `w.IsValid` â†’ `w.isValid`, proofs unchanged. One external consumer (`Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Assertability.lean::assertability_score_bounded`/`conditional_probability_bounded`) updates `ws.IsValid` â†’ `ws.isValid`.
- **`Core/Relativization/Hierarchy.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of the AH-rank order predicates. `AHPosition.atLeastAsAccessible (p1 p2) : Prop := p1.rank â‰¥ p2.rank` (was `Bool := decide (p1.rank â‰¥ p2.rank)`); `AHPosition.moreAccessible (p1 p2) : Prop := p1.rank > p2.rank` (was `Bool := decide (...)`). Both carry `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (_ â‰¥ _))` / `(_ > _)`. `ah_reflexive`/`ah_transitive` drop `= true`. One external consumer (`Phenomena/FillerGap/Studies/KeenanComrie1977.lean::welsh_kc_covers_deeper_than_wals`) drops `= true` and switches `native_decide` â†’ `decide`. Skipped: the `contiguousOnAH`/`hasAHRank`/`RelClauseMarker.{isContinuous,covers}` family stays `Bool` â€” the `prc_from_hc2`/`contiguous_intermediate` proofs unfold the `contiguousOnAH` Bool body and rely on `simp only [â†“reduceIte]` reductions, and `RelClauseMarker.covers` is consumed as a Bool filter argument across `KeenanComrie1977.lean`. Migrating those would require restructuring the proofs around `Decidable.casesOn` instead of Boolean reductions; tracked but out of scope for this batch.

### Notes
- **Skip decisions** for transparency: `Core/SyntaxConstraints/NullSubject.lean` (the `subjectAssignment_overtPRO_iff` bridge would cascade into a `ProDropProfile` structure-field migration that touches many `Fragments/*.lean` Bool literal initializers â€” deferred); `Core/Semantics/InfoState.lean` (the file's own docstring already documents the Bool/`List W` design as an intentional dynamic-semantics computational substrate â€” migration is its own project).

## [0.230.12] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/VoiceSystem.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of `VoiceSystemProfile`'s three derived predicates. `promotesRole (p) (r) : Prop := âˆƒ v âˆˆ p.voices, v.promotes = r` (was `Bool := p.voices.any (آ·.promotes == r)`); `distinguishesObliques (p) : Prop := âˆƒ v âˆˆ p.voices, v.promotes = .locative âˆ¨ ... âˆ¨ v.promotes = .circumstantial` (was a `match`-style `.any`); `isActivePassive (p) : Prop := p.voiceCount = 2 âˆ§ p.promotesRole .agent âˆ§ p.promotesRole .patient` (was a `&&` chain). All three carry `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (âˆƒ _ âˆˆ _, _))` / `(_ âˆ§ _ âˆ§ _)`. Mathlib idiom: `.any (آ· == x)` â†’ `âˆƒ _ âˆˆ _, _ = _` (`List.Mem` + âˆƒ). Cascade through 11 consumer sites in 8 files: `Fragments/Mayan/Kaqchikel/AgentFocus.lean::kaq_not_active_passive`, `Fragments/Mayan/Mam/VoiceSystem.lean` (`mam_not_simple_active_passive`/`mam_no_oblique_pivots`), `Fragments/Mayan/Kiche/VoiceSystem.lean::kiche_not_simple_active_passive`, `Fragments/Chuj/VerbBuilding.lean` (`chuj_not_simple_active_passive`/`chuj_no_oblique_pivots`), `Fragments/TobaBatak/Basic.lean` (`tb_is_active_passive`/`tb_promotes_agent`), `Fragments/Indonesian/VoiceSystem.lean` (`promotes_agent`/`promotes_patient`), `Fragments/English/Predicates.lean::english_is_active_passive`, `Fragments/Finnish/Predicates.lean::finnish_is_active_passive`, `Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Studies/Creissels2025.lean::english_is_active_passive` â€” all `... = true := rfl`/`= true := by native_decide` â†’ `... := by decide`, `... = false := rfl`/`= false := by native_decide` â†’ `آ¬ ... := by decide` (also eliminates four `native_decide` invocations in favor of kernel-checked `decide`).
- **`Core/ScopeTypes.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of `AvailableScopes` query predicates. `hasReading (a) (r) : Prop := r âˆˆ a.readings` (was `Bool := a.readings.contains r`); `isAmbiguous (a) : Prop := a.readings.length > 1` (was `Bool := a.readings.length > 1`). Both carry `Decidable` instances. Mathlib idiom: `.contains` â†’ `List.Mem`. No external consumers.
- **`Core/Lexical/RootFeatures.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of the two derived `RootProfile` predicates. `RootProfile.constrainsPatient (rp) : Prop := rp.patientRob.isConstrained = true` (was `Bool := rp.patientRob.isConstrained`); `RootProfile.overlaps (rpâ‚پ rpâ‚‚) : Prop := rpâ‚پ.forceMag.overlaps rpâ‚‚.forceMag = true âˆ§ ... âˆ§ rpâ‚پ.agentControl.overlaps rpâ‚‚.agentControl = true` (was a six-way `&&` chain). Both carry `Decidable` instances. The inner `Range.overlaps`/`Range.isConstrained` predicates remain `Bool`-returning (they wrap `.any`/`.contains` over a polymorphic `خ±`-list â€” moving them too would force `[DecidableEq خ±]` constraints on every consumer); the `= true` reflection bridges the layers without forcing that cascade. No external consumers.

## [0.230.11] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Core/Computability/Subregular/{Basic, StrictlyLocal, LocallyTestable, Tier, Hierarchy}.lean`**: foundational subregular hierarchy primitives (B3 of the mathlib-shaped relocation begun in 0.230.6/0.230.8), grounded in @cite{lambert-2022} آ§3.1-3.4 and shaped to mirror `Mathlib.Computability.Language`. `Basic.lean` defines `Augmented خ± := List (Option خ±)` (mathlib idiom: single `none` boundary marker collapses Lambert's distinct â‹ٹ/â‹‰ since edges only appear at fixed positions by construction), `lift`/`boundary k w := replicate (k-1) none ++ lift w ++ replicate (k-1) none`/`kFactorsList k`/`IsKFactor` plus structural lemmas (`length_of_mem_kFactorsList`, `isInfix_of_mem_kFactorsList`, `length_boundary`, `boundary_zero`/`boundary_one`). `StrictlyLocal.lean` defines `SLGrammar k خ±` (positive `permitted : Set (Augmented خ±)`), `SLGrammar.lang : Language خ±` (membership iff every `k`-factor of `boundary k w` is permitted), `ofForbidden` (forbidden-set presentation as set complement), `IsSLk k L := âˆƒ G, G.lang = L`, and trivial `univ`/`empty` grammars. `LocallyTestable.lean` defines `factorSet`/`IsLTk` (presence-of-factor closure) plus `factorCount k t f w := min t (count)` saturation and `IsLTTk` (LTT_{k,t} threshold-testable closure), with `isSLk_imp_isLTk` proving SL_k âٹ† LT_k from the SL test depending only on the *set* of factors. `Tier.lean` defines `project T : List خ± â†’ List خ±` (decidable-tier projection with `project_cons_pos`/`project_cons_neg`/`project_univ` simp lemmas), `TSLGrammar k خ±` (tier + SL grammar), and `IsTSLk`. `Hierarchy.lean` proves `IsSLk.toIsLTk` (re-export), `IsSLk.toIsTSLk` (universal-tier construction reducing TSL to SL), and `IsLTk.toIsLTTk` (saturation at `t â‰¥ 1` preserves the count-zero distinction, hence factor-set equality follows from saturated-count equality via `lt_min_iff` + `List.count_pos_iff`). The cross-`k` inclusions (SL_k âٹ† SL_{k+1}) are deferred â€” proving them directly requires reconciling boundary-padding mismatch between `boundary k` and `boundary (k+1)`, which is cleanest via the equivalent finite-state characterization (deferred until DFA-side infrastructure lands). 5 new files, 0 sorrys, all wired into `Linglib.lean`.

## [0.230.10] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Temporal/Tense.lean` + `Theories/Semantics/Tense/Decomposition.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of the tense-pronoun mode predicates and the derived deicticity predicate. `TensePronoun.isIndexical (tp) : Prop := tp.mode = .indexical` and `TensePronoun.isBound (tp) : Prop := tp.mode = .bound` (were `Bool := tp.mode == .indexical`/`.bound`) plus `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (tp.mode = ...))`. The derived `KratzerDecomposition.canBeDeictic (d) : Prop := d.tensePronoun.isIndexical` (was `Bool := d.tensePronoun.isIndexical`) cascades the migration into the Kratzer decomposition layer with its own `Decidable` instance. Five consumer cascades: `Decomposition.lean`'s `english_deictic_german_anaphoric` (uses `nofun` for the universally-quantified anaphoric branch â€” `decide` can't reduce with free `n`) and `reflexive_simultaneous_parallel` (drops `= true`); `Phenomena/TenseAspect/ComparePartee.lean`'s `zero_tense_parallels_reflexive` (drops `= true`); `Phenomena/TenseAspect/Studies/HeimKratzer1998.lean` migrates three "full chain" theorems (`kratzer_english_chain`/`kratzer_german_preterit_chain`/`kratzer_german_perfekt_chain`) â€” the `... = true`/`... = false` deictic clauses become bare/negated propositions, and the dataâ€“fragment Bool-equality clauses `(... == .indexical) = canBeDeictic` become `â†”`-biconditionals proved by `decide`; three Fragment files (`Fragments/{English,Italian,German}/Tense.lean`) migrate `kratzerSimplePast_deictic`/`kratzerPassatoProssimo_deictic`/`preterit_not_deictic`/`perfekt_deictic`/`preterit_perfekt_contrast` from `... = true/false := rfl` to `... := by decide`/`آ¬ ... := by decide`.

## [0.230.9] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Modality/ModalIndefinite.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of `hasEpistemic`/`hasCircumstantial` using mathlib idiom. `hasEpistemic (e : ModalIndefiniteEntry) : Prop := .epistemic âˆˆ e.flavors` (was `Bool := e.flavors.any (آ· == .epistemic)`); same for `hasCircumstantial`. `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (_ âˆˆ _))`. Mathlib-quality move: `.any (آ· == x)` â†’ `x âˆˆ xs` (List.Mem) per `feedback_mathlib_quality_migration` â€” replaces handrolled `.any`+`==` with mathlib's `List.Mem`. Cascade through 8 consumers: 5 fragment files (`Chuj`/`German`/`Italian`/`French`/`Spanish` `ModalIndefinites.lean`) â€” `... = true := rfl` â†’ `... := by decide` (List.Mem not `rfl`-reducible), `... = false := rfl` â†’ `آ¬ ... := by decide`, conjunctive forms refold; `Phenomena/ModalIndefinites/Studies/AlonsoOvalleRoyer2024.lean` â€” four filter sites (`yalnhej_unique_profile`, `multi_flavor_items`, `epistemic_only_items`, `rc_only_items`) wrap `hasEpistemic`/`hasCircumstantial` in `decide ...` to bridge to `&&`/`!`'s Bool context, three propositional theorems (`volitional_blocks_epistemic`/`unrestricted_with_epistemic`/`unrestricted_without_epistemic`) drop `= true`/`= false`; `Phenomena/ModalIndefinites/Studies/AlonsoOvalleMenendezBenito2010.lean` `algأ؛n_entry_epistemic_only` switches to bare `âˆ§ آ¬` form proved by `decide`; `Phenomena/Reference/Studies/Bubnov2026.lean` `irgend_compatible_classifications` drops `= true`.

## [0.230.7] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Register.lean` + `Core/Modality/ModalTypes.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of register-variant predicates. `Core.Register.areVariants (a b : Level) : Prop := a â‰  b` (was `Bool := a != b`) plus a `Decidable` instance via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (a â‰  b))`. `ModalItem.areRegisterVariants` follows suit (`Prop` + `Decidable`). Two consumer theorems migrate: `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/RotterLiu2025Concord.lean`'s `register_opposition` drops `= true` and proves by `decide` (was `native_decide`); `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/LiuRotter2025.lean`'s `must_certainly_same_register` and `may_possibly_same_register` switch from `... = false := by native_decide` to `آ¬ ... := by decide`.
- **`Core/InformationStructure.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of the IS-clash predicate underlying both @cite{abeille-et-al-2020}'s FBC and @cite{erteschik-shir-1973}'s Dominance Condition. `extractionISClash (fillerStatus domainStatus : DiscourseStatus) : Prop := fillerStatus = .focused âˆ§ domainStatus = .given` (was `Bool := fillerStatus == .focused && domainStatus == .given`) plus a `Decidable` instance. The three in-file lemmas drop `= true`/`= false`: `extractionISClash_focused_given := âں¨rfl, rflâں©`; the two `_focused_new`/`_given_given` use `آ¬ ... := by decide`. Two consumer cascades: `Theories/Semantics/Focus/BackgroundedIslands.lean` migrates 5 theorems (`backgrounded_extraction_clashes`/`atissue_extraction_compatible`/`mos_extraction_clashes`/`bridge_no_extraction_clash`/`prosodic_focus_resolves_clash`) to `Prop` form (positive: `simp ...; exact âں¨rfl, rflâں©`; negative: `آ¬ ... := by ... ; decide`). `Phenomena/Islands/Studies/CartnerEtAl2026.lean` migrates `fbcPredictsIsland`/`fbcRevisedViolation`/`fbcRevisedPredictsIsland` to `Prop` with `Decidable` instances; downstream theorems (`fbc_predicts_whq_island`/`fbc_predicts_no_rc_island`/`fbc_predicts_no_top_island`/`both_fbcs_same_predictions`/`fbc_predicts_construction_dependence`/`fbc_falsified_for_rc`/`_top`/`fbc_revised_falsified_for_rc`/`_top`/`argument_chain`) drop Bool-equality patterns â€” `= true` becomes bare proposition, `= false` becomes `آ¬`, the Bool-equality clauses in `both_fbcs_same_predictions` and `argument_chain` become `â†”`-biconditionals proved by `decide`.
- **`Core/Lexical/MorphRule.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of the paradigm-cell predicates. `ParadigmCell.isGap (c : ParadigmCell F) : Prop := c.form = none` (was `Bool := c.form.isNone`) plus a `Decidable` instance. `ParadigmCell.isIrregular (c : ParadigmCell F) : Prop := c.regular = false âˆ§ c.form â‰  none` (was `Bool := !c.regular && c.form.isSome`) plus a `Decidable` instance. No external consumers â€” the shapes are picked up by future Fragment paradigms via the `Decidable` instances.

## [0.230.5] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Theories/Phonology/Featural/Bundle.lean`** (NEW): parametric feature-bundle primitive `FeatureBundle (F V : Type) := F â†’ Option V` with mathlib-aligned operations (`single`/`assimilate`/`delete`/`set` all built on `Function.update`). Underspecified (`none`) vs specified (`some v`); `V = Bool` recovers classical `[آ±F]`, `V = â„ڑ`/`â„‌` recovers GSC/probabilistic features (@cite{smolensky-goldrick-2016} @cite{pater-2009}), `V = Unit` recovers privative. Parametric over the feature index `F` per @cite{reiss-2017}'s Substance-Free Phonology. Also includes `Tier F V := List (FeatureBundle F V)` plus `merge` (OCP-style), `mergeAdjacent`, and `assimilateLeftward` building blocks for autosegmental rule infrastructure.

### Changed
- **`Theories/Phonology/Autosegmental/RegisterTier.lean`**: substantial rewrite to @cite{lionnet-2022} architecture. Renames `ToneFeature` â†’ `TRN` (Tonal Root Node); reformulates the tier as a bundle of *paradigmatic* `[آ±upper]`/`[آ±raised]` subtonal features (vs @cite{snider-1999}'s overloaded paradigmatic+syntagmatic `h`/`l`). Now grounded on `Featural/Bundle.lean` rather than ad-hoc inductive. Cascade renames `ToneFeature` â†’ `TRN` through `Autosegmental/{BasemapCorrespondence, DominantCophAgreement, GrammaticalTone}.lean` and `Theories/Phonology/Tier.lean` (`Phonology.Tier.tonal` retypes to `TRN`).
- **`Theories/Phonology/Correspondence.lean`**: dropped the `Bool`-valued `isContiguous` (only consumer was `IsContiguous`'s decidability instance); the two callers (`contigIViol`, `contigOViol`) switch to the `Prop` form via the existing `Decidable (IsContiguous l)` instance.
- **`Theories/Phonology/OptimalParadigms.lean`**: `majorityRules : Prop` (was `Bool`) + `Decidable` instance via `inferInstance`.
- **`Theories/Phonology/StratalOT.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of the cross-stratal ranking API. `isPromoted`/`isDemoted`/`immediatelyDominates`/`dominates` (âˆƒ-form, depth-3 transitive closure)/`isOutputFeeding` (per-stratum match) all become `Prop` with `Decidable` instances; the three `stem_feeds_word`/`word_feeds_phrase`/`stem_not_feeds_phrase` lemmas drop `= true`/`= false` (the negative one switches `rfl` â†’ `decide`).
- **`Theories/Phonology/Syllable/Foot.lean`**: `isDegenerate`/`isWellFormedFoot` migrate to `Prop` with `Decidable` instances; the six well-formedness/degeneracy lemmas switch to `by decide` proofs (the `iamb` case switches `&&` to `âˆ§`).

## [0.230.4] - 2026-04-19

- **`Core/Lexical/PolarityItem.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of the polarity-item predicate API. `isLicensedIn item ctx : Prop := ctx âˆˆ item.licensingContexts` (was `Bool := item.licensingContexts.contains ctx`); `isNPI`/`isFCI` switched from match-on-polarityType returning Bool to `âˆ¨`-disjunctions of the relevant `polarityType = ...` cases; `isPPI` and `canonicityConsistent` likewise migrated to `Prop` (the `||`-of-`==` form for `canonicityConsistent` becomes a three-way `âˆ¨` of equalities).
