Fragment/study consumer cascade â€” `Fragments/English/PolarityItems.lean`, `Fragments/English/TemporalAdverbials.lean`, `Phenomena/Polarity/Studies/Israel2001.lean`: ~12 `#guard X.isNPI`/`#guard X.canonicityConsistent` style assertions converted to mathlib-style `example : X.isNPI := by decide` (rather than wrapping `decide` inside `#guard` to satisfy its Bool requirement); two `#guard List.all (آ·.canonicityConsistent)` Bool-folds rewritten as `example : âˆ€ p âˆˆ ..., p.canonicityConsistent := by decide`. `Phenomena/Polarity/Studies/DenicEtAl2021.lean`: `tested_npis_are_npis` (was `... = true âˆ§ ... = true âˆ§ ... = true := âں¨rfl, rfl, rflâں©`) now `... âˆ§ ... âˆ§ ... := by decide` since the `âˆ¨`-disjunction proofs vary by which constructor matches; `tested_ppi_is_ppi` drops `= true`, proves by `rfl`. `Phenomena/Polarity/Studies/VonFintel1999.lean` and `Theories/Semantics/Entailment/PolarityBuilder.lean`: 5 paired `fragment_builder_agree_*` theorems each, `isLicensedIn ... = true` â†’ bare Prop / `isLicensedIn ... = false` â†’ `آ¬ isLicensedIn ...`; first conjunct proof switches from `rfl` to `by decide` (List membership), second conjunct (`Builder.licensesItem ... = true/false`) unchanged. `Phenomena/TenseAspect/Studies/Rouillard2026.lean`: `npi_status_from_fragment` migrated to `inGTIA.isNPI âˆ§ آ¬ inETIA.isNPI := by native_decide`.

## [0.230.3] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/SpecificityCondition.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of the Specificity Condition predicates. `isSpecific (d : Definiteness) : Prop := d = .definite` (was `Bool` via match) plus a `Decidable` instance via `DecidableEq Definiteness`. `blocked (_op : ExternalOperator) (d : Definiteness) : Prop := isSpecific d` plus a `Decidable` instance via `inferInstanceAs`. The five exposition theorems migrate accordingly: `demonstratives_are_specific`/`blocks_definite`/`wh_indefinite_blocked_in_specific_dp` drop the `= true`, prove by `rfl`; `allows_indefinite`/`wh_indefinite_allowed_in_nonspecific_dp` become `آ¬ blocked ...` proved by `Definiteness.noConfusion` (the `_op` underscore makes `decide` choke on the free variable, so the explicit term-mode proof is cleaner); `operator_insensitive` switches from a `Bool`-equality `rfl` to an `Iff.rfl` (`blocked opâ‚پ d â†” blocked opâ‚‚ d`). Two consumer cascades: `Phenomena/Possession/Studies/AissenPolian2025.lean`'s `specificity_convergence_on_stranding`/`specificity_divergence_on_piedpiping` drop `= true` from the right conjunct (proof `âں¨rfl, rflâں©` unchanged); `Phenomena/Islands/Studies/ShenHuang2026.lean`'s `specificity_predicts_exp3` becomes `blocked ... âˆ§ آ¬ blocked ...` proved by `âں¨rfl, by decideâں©`.

## [0.230.2] - 2026-04-19

### Removed
- **`Core/Semantics/CommonGround.lean`**: deleted the `BContextSet W := W â†’ Bool` namespace and its `HasContextSet` instance â€” a parallel Bool API to `ContextSet W := Set W` with zero external consumers. The `BContextSet.toProp`/`trivial`/`absurd`/`update`/`filterWorlds`/`entails` defs and the `trivial_toProp`/`update_toProp` bridge theorems are gone; callers requiring computation on context sets attach `[DecidablePred]` to a `Set W` directly per mathlib practice. File-level docstring updated to drop the "decidable variants" line; `PresuppositionContext.lean:133` docstring's `BContextSet W` reference replaced with `Commitment.CommitmentSlate W` / `LocalCtx W` (extant `HasContextSet` instances).
- **`Core/Inquisitive.lean`**: deleted the redundant `isPartition_iff` rewrite lemma â€” the new `Prop` form of `isPartition` IS the iff, so the bridge theorem is vacuous.

### Changed
- **`Core/Scales/Scale.lean` + `Core/Discourse/AtIssueness.lean`**: Boolâ†’Prop migration of the gradient at-issueness/projectivity threshold predicates. `Rat01.exceeds (d خ¸ : Rat01) : Prop := خ¸.val < d.val` (was `Bool := decide (خ¸.val < d.val)`) plus a `Decidable (exceeds d خ¸)` instance via `inferInstanceAs`. Cascade through `AtIssueness`: `isAtIssue` and `isProjective` now `Prop` with `Decidable` instances; the `if isAtIssue d خ¸ then .new else .given` consumer in `Core/InformationStructure.lean` continues to work via the `Decidable` instance.
- **`Core/Inquisitive.lean`**: `Discourse.Issue.isPartition (q : Issue W) (worlds : List W) : Prop := âˆ€ w âˆˆ worlds, (q.alternatives.filter (آ· w)).length = 1` (was `Bool := worlds.all (...)`) plus a `[DecidableEq W] â†’ Decidable (q.isPartition worlds)` instance. The legacy Bool/List Hamblin form is preserved for finite-world consumers (`ProbabilisticAnswerhood`, etc.); the canonical Set-based partition predicate lives in `Core/Issue/Partition.lean` (`Core.Issue.IsPartition`, backed by mathlib's `Setoid.IsPartition`).
- **`Theories/Semantics/Focus/BackgroundedIslands.lean`**: consumer cascade for the `isAtIssue` Boolâ†’Prop migration. `IslandPrediction.isIsland` field stays `Bool`; `predictIsland` wraps the Prop with `decide` at the boundary (`isIsland := !decide (isAtIssue d خ¸)`). Helper lemmas migrated to positive/negative `Prop` forms (`isAtIssue_lexical : آ¬ isAtIssue ...`, `isAtIssue_none : isAtIssue ...`, `isAtIssue_factive : isAtIssue ...`); the three `predict_*` theorems use `decide_eq_false`/`decide_eq_true` to bridge; the two `*_rank_le_*` theorems use `if_pos`/`if_neg` instead of plain `if_true`/`if_false`; `predictIsland_monotone` uses `by_cases` on the Decidable Prop instead of `cases` on the Bool.
- **`Phenomena/Focus/Studies/Roberts2012.lean`**: dropped the stale "Migration note (in progress)" docstring claiming 17 sorries pending â€” the file has had zero sorries since the `Core/Issue/Hamblin.lean` infrastructure (`mem_alt_ofList_of_disjoint_others`, `ofList_le_polar_of_classified`, `polar_inf_polar_le_ofList_of_corners`) and `Core/Issue/Partition.lean` (`Core.Issue.IsPartition`) landed in 0.229.999. Replaced with a brief "Representation" note pointing to the active infrastructure.

### Added
- **`Core/Semantics/CommonGround.lean`**: `@[simp] CG.contextSet_add : (cg.add p).contextSet = p âˆ© cg.contextSet` exposes the cons-step of the foldr-intersection definitionally (only the `âٹ†` form `add_restricts` was previously public); `Inhabited (CG W)` instance with `default := CG.empty`; `empty_contextSet` promoted to `@[simp]`.

## [0.230.1] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Fragments/Numee/Prosody.lean`** (NEW): Numأ¨أ¨ (Glottocode `numa1247`, Southern Oceanic, New Caledonia) prosodic fragment, drawn from the Goro dialect as analysed by @cite{lionnet-2025} آ§3.3â€“آ§3.4. **(1)** `Syllable` structure (`form : String`, `specs : List RegisterSpec`) makes mora structure explicit so that `morae`/`isLightCV`/`isRegisterless`/`isDownstepped` predicates address the syllable-level eligibility conditions for the boundary phenomenon â€” neither expressible in the flat `List RegisterSpec` representation Drubea uses. **(2)** `BoundaryEffect` inductive (`none`/`single`/`double`) models the three outcomes of the utterance-final boundary downstep `âپ؛%`, including the stacked double-downstep `âپ؛âپ؛` that preserves the registerless/downstepped contrast utterance-finally (@cite{lionnet-2025} ex. 24 vs 25). **(3)** `numeeBoundaryEffect : Utterance â†’ BoundaryEffect` reverses the syllable list and inspects the final + penult: light CV final + registerless penult enables the boundary, with `single` for registerless finals and `double` for already-downstepped finals; heavy finals or downstepped penults yield `.none`. **(4)** Nine lexical entries instantiated from آ§3.4 ex. 22â€“29: `jaa` 'juice', the minimal pair `niCoconut`/`niBreast` 'coconut'/'breast', `mii` 'low', `ku` 'yam', `beTii` '(three)', `kwe` 'sand', `paa` 'down' + abstract fillers `regCV`/`regCVV`. The fragment models آ§3.4's mora-weight and preceding-context conditions explicitly â€” `RegisterTier`'s tier-level operations apply to underlying specs and do not yet model the boundary process, which lives at the prosodic-word/utterance interface.
- **`Phenomena/Tone/Studies/Lionnet2025.lean` آ§13** (Numأ¨أ¨ Boundary Downstep): eight theorems exercising the fragment against آ§3.4 ex. 24â€“29: `numee_registerless_final_single` (`[jaa, niCoconut] â†¦ .single`, ex. 24), `numee_downstepped_final_double` (`[jaa, niBreast] â†¦ .double`, ex. 25 â€” the stacked double-downstep), `numee_minimal_pair_distinguished` (the `nؤ©`/`âپ؛nؤ©` minimal pair survives the boundary as `.single` vs `.double`), `numee_heavy_final_blocks` (CVV final `mii` blocks, ex. 26), `numee_after_downstepped_blocks`/`numee_after_downstepped_blocks'` (downstepped penult blocks even when final is light CV, ex. 28: `âپ؛tؤ©ؤ© ku`, ex. 29: `âپ؛paa kwة›جƒ`), `numee_singleton_no_boundary` (â‰¥2 syllables required by structural description), and `numee_lexical_culminative` (`niBreast`/`beTii`/`paa` are `IsCulminative` from `RegisterTier`, confirming the boundary is *postlexical* and does not feed underlying culminativity). All eight theorems close by `decide` over the explicit syllable lists. `Linglib.lean` adds the `Fragments.Numee.Prosody` import.

## [0.230.0] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Theories/Phonology/Autosegmental/RegisterTier.lean`**: mathlib-discipline overhaul of Snider's Register Tier Theory (@cite{snider-1999}, @cite{snider-2020}, @cite{lionnet-2025}). **(1)** Reinvented mathlib primitives replaced: `pointwiseLE` and `decPointwiseLE` deleted in favour of `List.Forallâ‚‚ (آ· â‰¤ آ·)` from `Mathlib.Data.List.Forall2`; the bespoke `regSpecLE`/`specsMoreLowering` Bool ordering replaced with `RegisterSpec.le sâ‚پ sâ‚‚ := sâ‚پ.pitchEffect â‰¤ sâ‚‚.pitchEffect` (Prop) plus a `DecidableRel` instance â€” derived from a single primitive `RegisterSpec.pitchEffect : RegisterSpec â†’ Int`, not stipulated separately. **(2)** Boolâ†’Prop migration for the propositional predicate: `isCulminative : List RegisterSpec â†’ Bool` â†’ `IsCulminative : List RegisterSpec â†’ Prop := specs.count (some .l) â‰¤ 1` (uses mathlib `List.count`) with `DecidablePred` instance. **(3)** `realizePitch` switched from `Nat â†’ List RegisterSpec â†’ List Nat` to `Int â†’ List RegisterSpec â†’ List Int` â€” the old `Nat`-truncating subtraction silently violated the cumulativity property (Leben 2018 (c)) at the floor and broke `realizePitch_baseline_mono` for low offsets. New `terracing_below_zero` regression test exercises the Int fix. **(4)** `pitchDeltas := realizePitch 0` introduced as the **theory-primary view**: cumulative register shifts from the start, no privileged zero. Docstring frames it as the syntagmatic content (only differences are linguistically meaningful â€” RTT is relational). `realizePitch n specs = (pitchDeltas specs).map (آ· + n)` proved as `realizePitch_eq_pitchDeltas_shift` via the helper `realizePitch_shift` (offset acts additively). **(5)** `@[simp] realizePitch_cons` lemma bridges the explicit-branch definition to a uniform `pitchEffect`-based form, collapsing `realizePitch_baseline_mono` and `realizePitch_mono` to ~3-line proofs each. **(6)** Code organization: top-level `section` blocks (`Features`/`Geometry`/`Realization`/`Constraints`/`Postlexical`/`Typology`/`Verification`/`Monotonicity`); `hEpenthesisSpread.mark` helper inlined into the top-level pattern. **(7)** Docstring/citation cleanup: removed the false claim that `hEpenthesis` "models the abrupt raising pattern (the most common case)" â€” per @cite{lionnet-2025}, "abrupt" refers to the spreading variant `hEpenthesisSpread` (now correctly identified); dropped unverified `آ§4` from the @cite{beckman-pierrehumbert-1986} reference (paper unread); `DownstepProperties` docstring now flags Bool fields as stipulative annotations rather than derivable properties. **(8)** Cascade through downstream consumers: `Phenomena/Intonation/Studies/BeckmanPierrehumbert1986.lean` (`pointwiseLE` â†’ `List.Forallâ‚‚ (آ· â‰¤ آ·)` in `more_catathesis_lower_pitch`/`catathesis_blocking_concrete`; `catathesis_blocking` reframed from `(n k : Nat)` with truncating sub to `{m n : Int} (h : m â‰¤ n)`; `catathesis_terracing`/`catathesis_lowers` switched to `Int`); `Phenomena/Tone/Studies/Lionnet2025.lean` (5أ— `pattern_culminative_N` migrated from `isCulminative ... = true` Bool form to `IsCulminative` Prop form, proved by `decide`; `all_stems_culminative` reformulated as `âˆ€ e âˆˆ allStems, IsCulminative e.specs`; new `four_downsteps_deltas : pitchDeltas [some .l, some .l, some .l, some .l] = [-1, -2, -3, -4]` test exercises the theory-primary view alongside the existing offset-anchored `four_downsteps_terrace`). All four touched files build clean; pre-existing failures elsewhere are from concurrent in-progress work on unrelated Core/Semantics files.

## [0.229.999] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Core/Issue/Partition.lean`**: bridge from `Core.Issue W` to mathlib's `Setoid.IsPartition` and to the legacy Bool-based `QUD W`. Closes the missing piece flagged in the Discourse.Issue â†’ Core.Issue migration memo: a Set-based way to recover partition-shaped inquisitive content and the Roberts-style cell-equivalence relation. **(1)** `IsPartition (P : Issue W) : Prop := Setoid.IsPartition (alt P)` â€” predicate that the alternative set is a partition of `W` in mathlib's sense (no empty cell, every world in a unique alternative). Forces non-informativity (`info_eq_univ`); inherits `pairwiseDisjoint`, `nonempty_of_mem_alt`, `sUnion_alt_eq_univ` from mathlib's `Setoid.IsPartition` API rather than reproving them. **(2)** `toSetoid h : Setoid W := Setoid.mkClasses (alt P) h.2` â€” equivalence relation derived from a partition issue: two worlds are equivalent iff they share an alternative cell. `@[simp] classes_toSetoid : (toSetoid h).classes = alt P` and `toSetoid_rel_iff : (toSetoid h) w v â†” âˆƒ p âˆˆ alt P, w âˆˆ p âˆ§ v âˆˆ p` make the cell semantics machine-checkable. **(3)** `noncomputable def toQUD h : QUD W` â€” bridge to the legacy Bool-based `QUD W` via classical decidability of the underlying equivalence; `toQUD_sameAnswer_iff` characterizes via shared cell, `toQUD_cell_eq_alt` shows `(toQUD h).cell w` recovers the alternative cell of `w` in `P`. **(4)** Round-trip with `fromSetoid` (from `Core/Mood/PartitionAsInquiry.lean`): `isPartition_fromSetoid [Nonempty W] (r : Setoid W) : (fromSetoid r).IsPartition` (the `Nonempty W` rules out the degenerate `âˆ…`-alternative when `W` is empty); `toSetoid_fromSetoid : toSetoid (isPartition_fromSetoid r) = r` proves the `Setoid â†’ Issue â†’ Setoid` direction is identity. Mention-some, intermediate-exhaustive, and conditional-question alternative sets are not partitions and so fail `IsPartition` â€” the predicate carves out exactly the partition slice of inquisitive content. Zero sorries; `Linglib.lean` adds the import between `Issue.Hamblin` and `Issue.Singleton`.

## [0.229.998] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/ArealTypology/Studies/Haspelmath2001.lean`**: Standard Average European as a `LinguisticArea SAELanguage SAEFeature`, the first consumer of `Theories.Diachronic.Areal`. New top-level `Phenomena/ArealTypology/` directory (cross-cutting category like Negation/Coordination), housing the first areal-typology study. **(1)** `SAELanguage`: 41-constructor inductive (deriving `DecidableEq`, `Repr`, `Fintype`) covering Romance, Germanic, Slavic, Balkan, Baltic, marginal Finno-Ugric (area), Celtic + Basque + Turkic + Maltese (adjacent), Persian/Hindi-Urdu/Armenian (cofamilial â€” other IE branches), and a worldwide reference sample (Mongolian, Indonesian, Yoruba, Japanese, Mandarin, Swahili, Quechua, Lezgian, Georgian). **(2)** `SAEFeature`: 12-constructor inductive enumerating @cite{haspelmath-2001} آ§2.1â€“آ§2.12 â€” the dozen major Europeanisms (`definiteIndefiniteArticles`, `relativeClausesWithRelPro`, `havePerfect`, `nominativeExperiencers`, `participialPassive`, `anticausativeProminence`, `dativeExternalPossessor`, `negativePronounsNoVerbalNeg`, `particleComparative`, `relativeBasedEquative`, `strictAgreement`, `intensifierReflexiveDifferentiation`). **(3)** Per-feature `Finset SAELanguage` isoglosses (`articleLgs`, `havePerfectLgs`, `particleCompLgs`, etc.) encoding @cite{haspelmath-2001} Maps 107.1â€“107.12; `isoglossFinset : SAEFeature â†’ Finset SAELanguage` dispatches and `isoglossFor : SAEFeature â†’ Isogloss SAELanguage` lifts to the schema's `Set`-valued isogloss via Finset coercion. **(4)** `europeanReference : ArealReference SAELanguage` partitions the language sample into the four schema fields (area = 26 core + marginal SAE; cofamilial = 3 eastern IE; adjacent = 6 non-IE neighbors; world = 18 incl. cofamilial+adjacent for the worldwide-frequency criterion). **(5)** `sae : LinguisticArea SAELanguage SAEFeature` instance with `features := Finset.univ`, `isogloss := isoglossFor`; the `areal` proof obligation is currently `sorry` (TODO: closes by `decide` once schema density takes `[DecidablePred (آ· âˆˆ I)]` instead of `Classical`; this is the v1 architectural punch list, documented in آ§5 docstring). **(6)** `clusterCount : SAELanguage â†’ â„•` is a local computable mirror of `LinguisticArea.clusterScore` so that concrete predictions discharge by `decide`. Six verification theorems exercise the cluster-map gradience of @cite{haspelmath-2001} آ§4: `french_has_all_features` and `german_has_all_features` (= 12 â€” the Charlemagne nucleus), `english_in_outer_band` (â‰¥ 7 â€” the آ§4 outer band of English/Greek/Romanian), `turkish_low_score` and `persian_low_score` (â‰¤ 1 each â€” adjacent and cofamilial respectively, criteria (ii) and (iii)), `mongolian_zero_score` (= 0 â€” criterion (iv), worldwide reference). All six pass `by decide` against the encoded Map data, confirming internal consistency: French and German really do score 12/12 on the 12-feature inventory as encoded, English really does pass the 7-feature threshold, and Mongolian/Persian/Turkish really are below the boundary as the paper claims. The architectural payoff: future areal studies (Balkan, Mesoamerican, Circum-Baltic) instantiate the same `LinguisticArea` schema; cross-areal comparison (does any feature qualify as both an SAE and a Balkan Europeanism?) becomes a structural query rather than informal prose. The architectural debt: per-feature isoglosses are hard-coded from the paper's Maps rather than derived from existing per-phenomenon `Typology.lean` profiles (Comparison, Negation, Anaphora, Possession, Causation) â€” the next refactor pass should replace each `*Lgs` Finset with a derivation from the relevant `Typology` profile, making the connection between SAE and the per-phenomenon analyses true by construction rather than by editorial coincidence.

## [0.229.997] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Theories/Diachronic/Areal.lean`**: framework-neutral schema for typological linguistic areas (Sprachbأ¼nde) following @cite{haspelmath-2001}'s discrete-criteria model. `Isogloss L := Set L` (`abbrev` so `âˆ©`/`âˆھ`/`âٹ†`/`âˆˆ` inherit from mathlib); `Isogloss.density I S : â„ڑ` is the proportion of a `Finset` reference sample inside an isogloss (classical, since `Set L` membership isn't decidable). `ArealReference L` bundles the four reference samples (`area`, `cofamilial`, `adjacent`, `world`) needed for criteria (i)â€“(iv). `IsAreal R I : Prop` is a `structure` (not a `Prop` conjunction) with one named field per criterion (`area_majority`, `cofamilial_lacks`, `adjacent_lacks`, `not_worldwide`), thresholds hard-coded at `1/2` for v1. `LinguisticArea L F` bundles a reference, a `Finset F` of features, an `isogloss : F â†’ Isogloss L`, and a proof that every listed feature satisfies `IsAreal`. Derived gradient API: `clusterScore` (count of features a language shares), `isopleth k` (â‰¥ `k` features), `nucleus` (all but at most one feature) â€” recovers Haspelmath آ§4's cluster-map methodology from discrete data without requiring gradient features themselves. Feature type `F` is intentionally abstract: each areal study (SAE, Balkan, Mesoamerican) instantiates against its own inductive feature type. Docstring acknowledges the discrete framing as a first approximation per Stolz/Grambank/AUTOTYP-era critiques and notes that a future `GradientLinguisticArea` sibling can swap `Set L` for `L â†’ â„ڑ` strength functions without disturbing this schema. The schema is the foundation for an upcoming `Phenomena/ArealTypology/Studies/Haspelmath2001.lean` SAE study that composes existing per-phenomenon Typology profiles (Comparison, Negation, Anaphora, Possession, Causation) into a single `LinguisticArea SAELanguage SAEFeature` instance.

## [0.229.996] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Napoli & Nespor 1976 formalization + shared `Pragmatics.Bias` abstraction.** First sustained formalization of bias-conditioned negation as a *cross-construction* licensing predicate, treating the Italian *nonâ‚‚* paradigm of @cite{napoli-nespor-1976} as one instantiation among several (biased polar questions, VERUM focus, use-conditional rejection, commitment-update rejection moves). **(1)** `Theories/Pragmatics/Bias.lean` (NEW, ~215 lines): the framework-neutral `BiasLicensingProfile` structure bundles N&N آ§2's four conditions as named `Bool` fields (`contradictsPriorBelief`, `isAssertive`, `matrixUnnegated`, `imprecise`) with `DecidableEq`, `Repr`, and a `Fintype` instance via `Boolâپ´` equivalence. `BiasLicensingProfile.licenses` is the conjunctive predicate; five canonical profiles (`licensedProfile` plus four one-axis blocking profiles `questionedProfile`/`matrixNegatedProfile`/`preciseProfile`/`noContradictionProfile`) cover N&N آ§2.1â€“آ§2.3's distributional witnesses; `licenses_iff_all_true`, `unique_licensing_profile` (`(univ.filter ...).card = 1` by `decide` over the 16-element `Fintype`), `blocking_axes_independent`, and the five `*_blocks` `rfl` theorems make the architecture mathlib-style. The bridge `BiasLicensingProfile.toPolarityLicensing : BiasLicensingProfile â†’ Core.PolarityLicensing` connects N&N آ§3.11's *pur* data to @cite{greco-2020}'s weak-EN classification (licensed bias profile â†¦ `weakENProfile`, blocked profile â†¦ no licensing). **(2)** `Phenomena/Negation/Studies/NapoliNespor1976.lean` (NEW, ~300 lines): consumes `Pragmatics.Bias` end-to-end. آ§ 1 encodes Dario/Paolo dialogue contexts (آ§1 ex. 6â€“9). آ§ 2 instantiates the five canonical profiles per blocking environment (`questionedComparative`, `matrixNegatedComparative`, `equalityComparative`, `precisionComparative`) and proves each blocks via `rfl`. آ§ 3 the six morphosyntactic diagnostics from آ§3.1â€“آ§3.2 (`predictsSubjunctive`, `predictsPurNPI`, `predictsNegativeSpecificityRestriction`, `predictsNeancheConjunction`, `predictsCheComplementizer`, `predictsLoClitic`) all derive thinly from `p.licenses` per N&N's claim that *nonâ‚‚* triggers the same morphosyntactic profile in all licensing environments. آ§ 4 `WeakNPIClass` distinguishes `purClass` (licensed in N&N آ§3.11 ex. 46â€“48) from `affattoClass` (precision-requiring, blocked by آ§3.22's imprecise condition); `affatto_blocked_in_non2` derives the exclusion from `licenses`'s conjunctive structure (precision + imprecise are mutually exclusive â€” *not* a stipulation). آ§ 5 the `chissaSeNon` indirect-question profile uses the same `licensedProfile` as comparatives (N&N آ§4 ex. 88â€“91), making the cross-construction generalization machine-checked. آ§ 6 explicit bridge theorem `napoli_nespor_pur_is_weakEN` connecting *pur* licensing to @cite{greco-2020}'s weak-EN profile. **(3)** `Phenomena/Negation/Studies/Rett2026.lean` patched: `italianComparative.licensesWeakNPIs` flipped from `false` to `true` per N&N آ§3.11 ex. 46â€“48 (the *pur* paradigm). Added load-bearing docstring referencing N&N â€” previously `@cite{napoli-nespor-1976}` appeared decoratively in headers but no theorem actually depended on N&N's empirical content; now the data field is N&N-grounded with a comment distinguishing *pur* (weak NPI, licensed by N&N آ§3.11) from *affatto* (precision-requiring, blocked by N&N آ§3.22 fn 6 for orthogonal precision reasons). Verified `high_en_blocks_npis` still holds (italianComparative is `.low`). **(4)** `Linglib.lean` adds two new imports: `Linglib.Theories.Pragmatics.Bias` and `Linglib.Phenomena.Negation.Studies.NapoliNespor1976`. The architectural payoff: future formalizations of @cite{romero-han-2004} (biased polar questions), @cite{repp-2013} (VERUM focus), @cite{gutzmann-2015} (use-conditional rejection), and @cite{farkas-bruce-2010} (commitment-update rejection moves) plug into the same `BiasLicensingProfile`, making the cross-construction unification true by construction rather than by ad-hoc bridge theorems. The historical Generative-Semantics deep-structure-with-deletion-rule machinery (1968â€“1976) is omitted as artifact; the licensing predicate itself is foundational.

## [0.229.995] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Boolâ†’Prop migration: `rootLicensesTemplate` (ArgDerivation).** The rootâ€“template licensing predicate is propositional ("can this root fill this template?"), so it migrates to `Prop` + `[Decidable]`. **(1)** `Theories/Semantics/Verb/ArgDerivation.lean`: `rootLicensesTemplate : RootEntailments â†’ Template â†’ Bool` â†’ `RootLicensesTemplate : RootEntailments â†’ Template â†’ Prop` (PascalCase, mathlib `*Licenses*` style); per-Template-case decidability instance via `cases t <;> unfold ... <;> infer_instance`. The `licensedTemplates : RootEntailments â†’ List Template` filter bridges to Bool with `decide` at the call site (`List.filter` requires Bool). **(2)** Six verification theorems migrated from `= true âˆ§ = true âˆ§ ...` to direct Prop conjunctions proved by `decide`: `pc_licenses`, `pureManner_licenses` (uses `آ¬` for the false cells, replacing `= false`), `pureResult_licenses`, `causativeResult_licenses`, `fullSpec_licenses`, `pc_result_stative_difference`. **(3)** `primary_licensed_canonical` rewritten: was `(primaryTemplate X).any (rootLicensesTemplate X) = true âˆ§ ...` (a five-fold `Option.any` of Bool predicates), now five direct `RootLicensesTemplate X T` instances naming the actual primary template per case (`.propertyConcept .state`, `.pureResult .achievement`, etc.) â€” sharper since it spells out *which* template each root primaries. **(4)** Cascaded `Phenomena/Causation/Studies/Krejci2012.lean`: `eat_licenses_accomplishment` migrated to `by decide`; آ§ 1 docstring pointer updated. ArgDerivation + Krejci2012 build clean.

## [0.229.994] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Boolâ†’Prop migration: Verb subtheory predicates (Affectedness, LevinTheory, AgentivityLattice).** Three rounds bringing the verb subtheory in line with mathlib discipline (`Prop` + `[Decidable]` over `Bool`-returning functions; bridge to Bool via `decide` only at use sites that demand a Bool). **(1)** `Affectedness.lean`: dropped the redundant `AffectednessDegree.ge` Bool wrapper (the existing `LE` instance already provides `â‰¤`/`<`); `Beavers2010.lean`'s `MapHolds : AlternationContrast â†’ Prop := خ» c => c.obliqueDegree â‰¤ c.directDegree` (was `Bool` using `ge`), `MAP_holds_all_alternations : âˆ€ c âˆˆ allContrasts, MapHolds c` (was `allContrasts.all mapHolds = true`); `BeaversUdayana2022.lean`'s `LicensesDispositionalMiddle : Prop`. **(2)** `LevinTheory.lean`: dissolved three Bool helper defs (`spec_implies_bkg_manner`, `causativeResult_always_cos`, `root_cause_accounted_for`) â€” they were the encode-conclusions-as-Bool anti-pattern (universal `âˆ€ c, fooImplication c = true` over a Bool function whose body was `!p || q`). Replaced with direct `Prop`-implication theorems (e.g. `c.meaningComponents.mannerSpec = true âˆ¨ c.meaningComponents.instrumentSpec = true â†’ c.rootEntailments.manner = true`), proved by `cases c <;> decide`. **(3)** `AgentivityLattice.lean`: migrated six validity/region predicates to `Prop` with `[Decidable]` instances â€” `AgentivityNode.Valid`, `GrimmNode.AgentivityValid`/`CrossValid`/`Valid`/`InTransitiveRegion`, `DomPredictedByLattice`, `SubjectInAgentRegion` (PascalCase per mathlib `Is*`/`*Valid` convention). The 17 verification theorems and the dom-monotonicity pair migrated alongside. Cascaded through `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Grimm2011.lean` (~10 sites): `russian_matches_lattice`/`spanish_subset_of_lattice`/`animacy_dom_within_lattice` use `decide (DomPredictedByLattice ...)` inside Bool comparisons against `DOMProfile.marks`; `latticeDOM`'s `marks` field bridges via `decide`; `transitivity_membership`/`verb_class_dom_behavior`/`creation_dom_inapplicable`/`lattice_matches_aissen_type2` migrated to direct Prop statements; all `native_decide` proofs replaced with `decide`. The DOMProfile.marks field stays Bool (genuine bitfield data â€” typology table values), but every predicate that *means* something propositional is now a Prop. AgentivityLattice + Grimm2011 + downstream Beavers2010/BeaversUdayana2022/LevinTheory all build clean.

## [0.229.993] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Discourse.Issue â†’ Core.Issue migration: Strategy.lean, QUDStack.lean, Roberts2012.lean (pilot).** Migrates the rose-tree strategy, QUD stack, and the canonical Roberts Dâ‚€ worked example off the legacy Bool/List `Discourse.Issue` onto the Set-based `Core.Issue` with Prop+Decidable Roberts QUD predicates. **(1)** `Core/Discourse/QUDStack.lean`: imports `Core/Issue/Basic.lean` instead of `Core/Inquisitive.lean`; `open Core` so the structural `List (Issue W)` field resolves to `Core.Issue`. Pure structural migration â€” no operations on Issue contents. **(2)** `Core/Discourse/Strategy.lean`: `isComplete` now `Prop`-valued (was `Bool`); uses lattice meet `âٹ“` and top `âٹ¤` (was `Issue.inter ... worlds` and `Issue.ofAlternatives [trivialState]`); calls `Core.Issue.questionEntails` (Prop) instead of the legacy 3-arg Bool predicate; `moveRelevantToStrategy` likewise restructured around `Issue.alt` and `Issue.partiallyAnswers`. **(3)** `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/Roberts2012.lean`: Dâ‚€ atoms (`hannahBeans`/`hannahTofu`/`rogerBeans`/`rogerTofu`) migrated from `D0World â†’ Bool` to `Set D0World`; per-set `DecidablePred` instances + nontriviality lemmas (أ—8); polar questions via `Issue.polar`; wh-questions `q_a`/`q_b` via `Issue.ofList` of partition cells; Big Question `q_1 := q_a âٹ“ q_b`; D0World gets a `Fintype` instance via the `Boolأ—Boolأ—Boolأ—Bool` equivalence (`card_D0World = 16` by `decide`). **Polar-polar theorems decide cleanly** via the `*_polar_iff` rewrite lemmas (positive/negative partial answers to `q_ai`, `qai_not_entails_qbi`, etc.). **Sorries with TODO markers** for theorems that depend on missing infrastructure: (a) `HasAltList` instance for `Issue.ofList L` when `L` is pairwise-disjoint nonempty (would yield `alt (ofList L) = L`), needed for `qa_entails_qai` and the wh-question entailment chain; (b) `HasAltList` for `P âٹ“ Q` from witnesses on each operand, needed for `q1_entails_qa`/`q1_entails_qb` and Strategy completeness. The `at-issueness` and `isPartition`/`toQUD round-trip` theorems were dropped pending Set-based Core.Issue â†’ QUD bridge infrastructure. 17 sorries land in this file (was: 0 in pre-migration version; the legacy version proved everything via `native_decide` over Bool/List alternatives). **(4)** Removed the `Compare.lean`-style `bridge_*` theorems testing `refinesOn â†” questionEntails` agreement (the LHS uses partition refinement on `q.toQUD`, which doesn't exist on Core.Issue yet). Full `lake build` clean (only the expected sorry warnings).

## [0.229.992] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Zheng2025 آ§6: empirical closure of آ§1â†”آ§5 on the dripping-raincoat scenario.** Closes the data-theory loop that 0.229.988 left half-open: آ§5 introduced `nandaoFullFelicity` as the conjunction of Issue-singleton (آ§4) and Kernel-bias (آ§2) layers, but no آ§1 datum was wired to a concrete instance. **(1)** Promoted `wearingRaincoat`, `expectDry`, `isRaining`, their `DecidablePred` instances, `raincoatK`, `dryU` from `private` to public in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kernel.lean` (paper-replica scenario data, intended to be reusable for the Phenomena bridge). **(2)** New آ§6 in `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Zheng2025.lean`: `biasedUse_integrated_felicity : nandaoFullFelicity (Core.Issue.declarative isRaining) raincoatK dryU isRaining` proven by `nandaoFullFelicity_declarative_iff` + the existing `raincoat_nandao_felicitous` (Layer 2 carries the cardinality work, Layer 1 reduces via `alt_declarative`). **(3)** Bridge theorem `biasedUse_witnesses_integrated_felicity` packages the آ§1 datum's empirical fields (`evidentialBias = true`, `epistemicBias = true`, `felicitous = true`) with the آ§5 theoretical prediction â€” the data â†” theory correspondence on @cite{zheng-2025} ex. 2 is now explicit and machine-checked. **(4)** `kernel_requires_evidence` signature migrated `World â†’ Bool` â†’ `World â†’ Prop` for consistency with the post-migration Kernel. **(5)** Header docstring `## Predictions verified` block updated to list the new theorems.

### Fixed
- **Two upstream Kernel.lean errors from the parallel-session Boolâ†’Prop migration.** (a) Line 305 in `foldl_refine_uniform_aux`: `rcases List.mem_singleton.mp hq'_p with rfl` substituted `p` away (rather than `q'` â€” both were free locals, `subst` kept the more recently introduced `q'`). The call to `refine_cell_uniform_p partition p hcell' hw' hv'` then failed with "Unknown identifier `p`". Fix: use `q'` instead of `p` (the surviving name post-substitution), with a one-line comment explaining why. (b) Line 1000 in `raincoat_nandao_felicitous`: `decide` couldn't see through the opaque `def evidenceRaises` to its underlying `Nat` `>` to derive `Decidable (evidenceRaises wearingRaincoat isRaining)`. Fix: `unfold evidenceRaises; decide` to expose the decidable comparison.

## [0.229.991] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Stage J of Numerals API cleanup: derive Class A/B and Bound direction structurally from `NumeralExpr`; collapse آ§5 to universal theorems.** Stage H/I left `ModifierClass`/`BoundDirection` from آ§1 as a pure conceptual taxonomy with zero connection to `NumeralExpr` â€” the آ§5 theorems were named `classA_gt_excludes_bare`, `classB_ge_includes_bare`, etc., but the classification didn't drive any proof. Per CLAUDE.md "do not encode conclusions as definitions, derive them from deeper principles", the classification is now structural and the inclusion/exclusion theorems are universally quantified, so adding a new modifier requires extending the classifier *and* the case analysis. **(1)** New `NumeralExpr.argument : NumeralExpr â†’ Nat` (extracts `m` from any constructor), `NumeralExpr.modifierClass : NumeralExpr â†’ Option ModifierClass` (`bare â†¦ none`, `moreThan/fewerThan â†¦ some classA`, `atLeast/atMost â†¦ some classB`), `NumeralExpr.boundDirection : NumeralExpr â†’ Option BoundDirection` (`bare â†¦ none`, `atLeast/moreThan â†¦ some lower`, `atMost/fewerThan â†¦ some upper`). **(2)** Replaced four per-modifier آ§5 theorems (`classA_gt_excludes_bare`, `classA_lt_excludes_bare`, `classB_ge_includes_bare`, `classB_le_includes_bare`) with two universal theorems: `classA_excludes_bare_world (bare) (e) (h : e.modifierClass = some .classA) : آ¬ e.meaning bare e.argument` and `classB_includes_bare_world (bare) (e) (h : e.modifierClass = some .classB) : e.meaning bare e.argument`. Both proved by `cases e <;> simp_all [...]` over the classifier â€” the case discipline forces every constructor to either be classified or fail compilation. The bare-numeral-semantics parameter is universally quantified, so the result is independent of Kennedy/Horn theory choice. **(3)** Deleted `classA_not_entailed_by_bare` (subsumed by `classA_excludes_bare_world` plus `bareMeaning m m = rfl`). **(4)** Generalized the four `decide`-based spot-check theorems: `bare_not_monotonic` â†’ `bare_not_upward_monotone` + `bare_not_downward_monotone` (both stated as `آ¬ âˆ€ m m' n, ...` and refuted with explicit witnesses); `bare_not_horn_scale` deleted (now follows trivially from the upward+downward monotonicity refutations); `atLeast_strictly_weaker_than_bare` and `moreThan_disjoint_from_bare` rewritten as universal theorems quantified over `m` (and over `n` for the disjointness one) instead of fixed-`m=3` spot-checks. **(5)** Added آ§4 alternative-set classification theorems: `lowerAlternatives_classification (n) : (lowerAlternatives n).map (آ·.argument, آ·.modifierClass, آ·.boundDirection) = [(n, none, none), (n, some .classA, some .lower), (n, some .classB, some .lower)]` and the upper analogue. Both `rfl` â€” they catch reordering, omission, or wrong-direction bugs in the alternative-set definitions. **(6)** Updated docstring reference in `Theories/Semantics/Alternatives/Lexical.lean` (renamed theorem). Numerals.Basic builds clean.

## [0.229.990] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Condoravdi 2002 mathlib-PR-style cleanup pass** (`Phenomena/Modality/Studies/Condoravdi2002.lean`). Audit the file from a mathlib-reviewer perspective and apply the entire punch list. (1) **Drop `C` author-tag suffix on operators**: `presC`/`perfC`/`mayC`/`wollC` â†’ `pres`/`perf`/`may`/`woll`. The namespace `Condoravdi2002` already disambiguates; tagging identifiers with the paper author is non-mathlib style. (2) **Delete the four `_eq` simp rfl lemmas** (`presC_eq`/`perfC_eq`/`mayC_eq`/`wollC_eq`): they're definitional unfoldings, and `simp [pres]` (etc.) suffices on demand â€” `@[simp]` rfl wrappers can destabilise simp normal forms. (3) **Collapse `Perspective أ— Orientation` (4 cells, 1 unattested) into 3-constructor `inductive ModalReading | present | epistemic | counterfactual`**: the unattested past-perspective + past-orientation cell is now impossible by construction. `ModalReading.perspective` and `ModalReading.orientation` are derived projections. Deletes `modalsForPresent`/`modalsForPastEpistemic`/`modalsForPastCF`, `attestedReadings`, and the `no_past_past`/`readings_distinct` decide-theorems (subsumed by exhaustion). (4) **Demote single-operator example glosses**: `mightBeHere` and `mightRun` deleted (pure aliases for `may MB .stative/.dynamic ... now`); the two scope-relevant compositions remain as `mayEpistemic` and `mightCounterfactual` (load-bearing for the epistemic-vs-counterfactual scope distinction). (5) **Variable hygiene**: file-scope `variable {W Time}` split into per-section `Operators`/`KleinBridge`/`ScopeCorrelation`/`Adverbs` blocks; the `Adverbs` section uses only `Time`, eliminating the auto-included-unused-W lint. (6) **Drop equation-number citations** (`[20]`/`[21]`/`[22]`/`[28]`/`[33]`/`[44]`/`[45]`/`[1]`/`[2]`) per CLAUDE.md "most-hallucinated category" rule â€” replaced with content descriptions ("the perfect operator", "the diversity condition"). (7) **Fix stale architecture pointer**: header referenced `Theories/Semantics/Tense/BranchingTime.lean` (does not exist) â€” corrected to `Core/Modality/HistoricalAlternatives.lean` (the actual import). (8) **Replace خ»-calculus snippets in docstrings with prose** (mathlib-style informal-English documentation). (9) **`compatible` now takes `ModalReading` directly** (was `Orientation`) and projects via `reading.orientation`; theorem renames `yesterday_past_compat` â†’ `yesterday_epistemic_compat`, `tomorrow_past_incompat` â†’ `tomorrow_epistemic_incompat`. (10) **`compatible` docstring honesty**: explicit note that the eventive/stative distinction is *not* recorded â€” the AT relation permits `now = e.start` for `atEventForward`, so the paper's "??He may get sick now" marking is documented as a pragmatic event-duration fact, not a temporal-logic incompatibility. (11) **Fragment-binding theorem** drops `condoravdi_` prefix (redundant with namespace): `condoravdi_might_have_distinct_aux_types` â†’ `might_have_distinct_aux_types`, moved to its own `Fragment binding` section header. (12) **Operator argument-naming consistency**: `pres` now takes `(t : Time)` matching `perf`/`may`/`woll`. Downstream (`CarianiSantorio2018.lean` docstring reference to `wollC`) updated to `woll`. Hacquard2006 (consumes `Perspective` â€” kept as a separate enum for downstream stability) builds clean. Net: file is ~30 lines shorter, eliminates 1 enum + 1 structure + 2 example defs + 4 simp wrappers + 2 decide theorems, with no loss of mathematical content.

## [0.229.989] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Hungarian focus fragment + Kiss 1998 study + lifted MSMH predicate.** Built the contrasting language to Hausa called for by 0.229.983's mathlib-PR-review pass on `HartmannZimmermann2007.lean`. **(1)** `Theories/Semantics/Focus/MeaningStructureMapping.lean` (NEW, ~50 lines): the polymorphic `MeaningStructureMapping {U S I} (licensed : U â†’ Prop) (struct : U â†’ S) (interp : U â†’ I) : Prop` predicate plus `refute_by_witness` lemma. The MSMH is a typology-level claim, not a per-language definition; lifting it to the Theory layer makes the Hausa/Hungarian contrast a difference of verdict on a *single shared hypothesis* rather than two parallel-but-unrelated statements. **(2)** `Fragments/Hungarian/Focus.lean` (NEW, ~210 lines): `Position` (preverbal/postverbal), `FocusType` (identificational/information) with `IsExhaustive : FocusType â†’ Prop`, `ConstituentClass` (regular/universal/onlyPhrase/someIndef) with the آ§3 `compatibleWith` distributional table, `FocusConfig` structure with `Licensed` predicate combining positionâ€“type pairing and class compatibility, smart constructors `mkIdentificational`/`mkInformation` taking compatibility proofs, and three universal theorems: `licensed_position_determines_type`, `onlyPhrase_forces_identificational` (csak X must move to Spec,FP â€” paper آ§3 last آ¶), `someIndef_never_licensed` (valami/valaki barred from both â€” paper eq. 17e). **(3)** `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/Kiss1998.lean` (NEW, ~190 lines): six paper cells (`preverbal_identificational`/eq. 5a, `postverbal_information`/5b, `preverbal_hat`/8a, `postverbal_hat`/8b, `starred_universal_identificational`/17b, `universal_information`/19b); `HungarianMSMH` instantiates the lifted predicate with `Licensed`/`position`/`focusType`; `hungarian_satisfies_MSMH` proves Hungarian validates the hypothesis (the same theorem statement that fails for Hausa); `preverbal_iff_exhaustive` composes positionâ€“type with typeâ€“exhaustivity to derive the semantic payoff; four distributional-restriction cell-level theorems. **(4)** `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/HartmannZimmermann2007.lean` refactored: dropped local `MeaningStructureMappingGeneric`/`MeaningStructureMapping`, replaced with `HausaMSMH` instantiating the lifted predicate (filter = `IsHausaLicensed`, struct = `cfg.strategy`, interp = `pragType`); `hausa_falsifies_MSMH` now uses `MSMH.refute_by_witness`; `hausa_falsifies_MSMH_inSitu` adds explicit `IsHausaLicensed` witness conjuncts to match the lifted predicate's signature. **(5)** `kiss-1998` bib entry added with verified Language 74(2):245-273 metadata and JSTOR stable URL (DOI omitted per CLAUDE.md hallucination-prevention discipline â€” the paper PDF has no DOI on the front matter). All four files (theory, fragment, study, refactored Hausa) build clean. The deeper integration is **typological symmetry by construction**: same predicate, opposite verdicts; future languages plug in by instantiating the `MSMH.MeaningStructureMapping` type at their own utterance/struct/interp choices.

## [0.229.988] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Zheng2025 آ§5: integrated felicity bridge between آ§2 (Kernel-bias) and آ§4 (Issue-singleton).** Closes the interconnection-density gap surfaced by 0.229.986's alias cleanup: nandao had two unrelated felicity stories living in the same study file â€” `nandaoFelicitous (k : Kernel) (u : Background) (د† : World â†’ Prop)` (the discourse-context bias check, آ§2) and `IsSingleton (declarative p)` (the Issue-level singleton presupposition on the sister, آ§4) â€” with no theorem connecting them. New آ§5 introduces `nandaoFullFelicity (Q : Core.Issue World) (k : Kernel) (u : Background) (p : Set World) [DecidablePred p] : Prop := alt Q = {p} âˆ§ nandaoFelicitous k u p` as the conjunction: the sister `Q` denotes a one-cell issue with witness `p` (Layer 1), AND the Kernel-bias check holds for `p` (Layer 2). The witness is supplied externally so decidability is concrete. **Bridges**: `nandaoFullFelicity_isSingleton` and `nandaoFullFelicity_kernel` project to the two component felicities (آ§4 and آ§2 respectively); `nandao_polar_no_witness` proves the universal Layer-1 obstruction (a non-trivial two-cell `polar pâ‚€` admits no integrated-felicity witness for any `(k, u)`, because the آ§4 type-level barrier blocks the `alt Q = {p}` requirement); `nandaoFullFelicity_declarative_iff` proves that on the canonical felicitous case `declarative p`, integrated felicity collapses to the آ§2 Kernel-bias check on `p` (Layer 1 holds trivially via `alt_declarative`). The bridge makes the layered architecture of nandao felicity explicit: presupposition at the semantics-syntax interface, bias at the discourse-context interface, with the witness serving as the unique connecting referent. Verified via scratch (`scratch/test_section5.lean` with stub Kernel/Background/nandaoFelicitous matching the post-migration `World â†’ Prop` signatures); the file itself currently fails to build because of an unrelated in-progress `Bool â†’ Prop` migration in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kernel.lean` from a parallel session â€” once that lands, both آ§2 (which also has a stale `World â†’ Bool` signature in `kernel_requires_evidence`) and آ§5 will typecheck against the migrated Kernel.

### Changed
- **Stage I of Numerals API cleanup: lift `typeLower` to `Core/Scales/Scale.lean` as a scale-general operation.** Stage H left `typeLower` (existential lowering of an exact-style degree property: `âˆƒ d' â‰¥ d, exact d' w`) inside `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic.lean` even though the operation has no dependence on `Nat` or numerals â€” it lives over any reflexive linear order. Per CLAUDE.md "import don't restipulate", any operation polymorphic over `Core.Scale` primitives belongs in `Core`. **(1)** New آ§6c "Existential Lowering (Type-Shifting)" added to `Core/Scales/Scale.lean` (within the `[LinearOrder خ±]` variable scope) containing: `def typeLower {W} (exact : خ± â†’ W â†’ Prop) (d : خ±) (w : W) : Prop := âˆƒ d', d' â‰¥ d âˆ§ exact d' w`; the collapse theorem `typeLower_eqDeg_iff : typeLower (eqDeg خ¼) d w â†” atLeastDeg خ¼ d w` (proved by `خ¼ w` witness in one direction, equation rewriting in the other); the generic `Decidable (typeLower (eqDeg خ¼) d w)` instance via `decidable_of_iff` against `atLeastDeg`; `atLeastDeg.decidable` (lifted from being implicit in numeral context); and the universal-closure failure lemma `distinct_no_universal_witness {خ±} (kâ‚پ kâ‚‚ : خ±) (hne : kâ‚پ â‰  kâ‚‚) : آ¬ âˆƒ x, âˆ€ k, k = kâ‚پ âˆ¨ k = kâ‚‚ â†’ x = k` (no `LinearOrder` needed â€” pure equality reasoning). **(2)** `Numerals/Basic.lean` آ§6 reduced from 14 lines to a thin re-export: `theorem typeLower_bareMeaning_iff (m n : Nat) : Core.Scale.typeLower bareMeaning m n â†” atLeastMeaning m n := Core.Scale.typeLower_eqDeg_iff id m n`. The `bareMeaning â‰، Core.Scale.eqDeg id` and `atLeastMeaning â‰، Core.Scale.atLeastDeg id` `abbrev` definitions make the instantiation definitional. Local `def typeLower`, `theorem typeLower_uniqueness`, the explicit Numeral-specific Decidable instance, and `theorem universal_closure_fails` all deleted. **(3)** Deleted آ§7 duplicates `typeLower_iff_atLeast` and `exh_typeLower_roundtrip` â€” both were literal restatements of `typeLower_bareMeaning_iff` masquerading as separate theorems for the duality table. The آ§7 docstring narrative now points at `exhNumeral_iff_bare` and `typeLower_bareMeaning_iff` directly as the two halves of the duality, no synonym layer. **(4)** `Phenomena/Quantification/Studies/ScontrasPearl2021.lean` `typeshift_resolves_tension` re-pointed: `Semantics.Numerals.typeLower` â†’ `Core.Scale.typeLower` (one call site). **Net** the type-shifting operation is now usable on any scale (gradable adjectives, time intervals, mass nouns, modal degrees), not just numerals; `Core.Scale` gains a foundational existential-lowering primitive that `Numerals/Basic.lean` now derives by passing `id`. All touched files (Core.Scale, Numerals.Basic, ScontrasPearl2021, Spector2013, KaoEtAl2014/Hyperbole) build clean.

## [0.229.986] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mathlib-PR cleanup of the kya:/nandao singleton-presupposition stack: collapse the alias triple into one foundational predicate + one subtype.** The 0.229.982 build had three layered aliases â€” `IsSingleton` (Core), `singletonPresup`/`singletonInterp` (Theories felicity wrapper), `kyaPresup`/`kyaInterp` + `nandaoSingletonPresup`/`nandaoSingletonInterp` (per-particle) â€” plus `highlightedPolar p := declarative p` as a fourth alias. Subjected to mathlib-PR-review discipline: any layer that's `def f := g` (zero added structure) gets dropped; partial functions become subtypes; the polar-failure proof's inline `alt`-membership computation gets extracted into a reusable lemma. **(1)** `Core/Issue/Hamblin.lean`: added `alt_polar_of_nontrivial : alt (polar p) = {p, pل¶œ}` (when `p â‰  âˆ…, univ`), the maximality characterization that drives the polar Q vs. highlighted Q contrast. Replaces the ~30-line inline `alt`-membership computation in `not_isSingleton_polar_of_nontrivial`. **(2)** `Core/Issue/Singleton.lean`: simplified `not_isSingleton_polar_of_nontrivial` from 30 lines of inline `alt` proofs to 8 lines invoking `alt_polar_of_nontrivial`; added `SingletonIssue W := {Q : Issue W // IsSingleton Q}` subtype with `issue`/`witness` extraction and `ofDeclarative` constructor â€” the mathlib-style replacement for the `Option`-valued partial-function `singletonInterp`. **(3)** Deleted `Theories/Semantics/Questions/Highlighted/Basic.lean` entirely: `highlightedPolar p := declarative p` was a pure alias with no semantic content. The "highlighted polar" terminology of @cite{roelofsen-farkas-2015}/@cite{bolinger-1978} now lives as a comment in `Singleton.lean`'s docstring; consumers use `declarative p` directly. **(4)** Deleted `Theories/Semantics/Questions/Presupposition/Singleton.lean` entirely: `singletonPresup Q := IsSingleton Q` (alias) and `singletonInterp Q : Option (Issue W)` (Option-valued partial fn) were a redundant middle layer. The Option pattern is replaced by the `SingletonIssue` subtype. **(5)** `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/BhattDayal2020.lean` آ§4 rewritten: dropped `kyaPresup`/`kyaInterp` aliases; theorems now state directly in terms of `IsSingleton (declarative p)` and `SingletonIssue.ofDeclarative` (`kya_felicitous_declarative`, `kya_interp_declarative`, `kya_infelicitous_two_cell_polar`, `kya_polarQuestion_signals_singletonPresup`). **(6)** `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Zheng2025.lean` آ§4 rewritten: dropped `nandaoSingletonPresup`/`nandaoSingletonInterp` aliases; the cross-particle agreement theorem `nandao_kya_share_felicity` now states the proof terms are *definitionally equal* (both reduce to `isSingleton_declarative`) rather than wrapping an `Iff.rfl` over alias-unfolding. **(7)** `Linglib.lean` drops the two deleted module imports. **Net** four files instead of five, three layers of aliasing collapsed to one (the foundational `IsSingleton` predicate) plus one subtype, and the convergence noted in @cite{bhatt-dayal-2020} fn. 11 holds true by construction â€” both particles' felicity theorems reduce to the same `isSingleton_declarative`. All touched files build clean (Hamblin, Singleton, BhattDayal2020 verified directly; Zheng2025 آ§4 verified via scratch â€” the file currently fails to build because of an unrelated in-progress Boolâ†’Prop migration in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kernel.lean` from a parallel session).

## [0.229.985] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Condoravdi 2002 adverb-compatibility section refactored to derive from AT primitives** (`Phenomena/Modality/Studies/Condoravdi2002.lean`). The previous section encoded the conclusions as definitions (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern): `ReferenceDirection.reaches : ReferenceDirection â†’ TemporalRegion â†’ Bool` was a 3أ—3 lookup table, `compatible` was a Bool composition over it, and the eight (in)compat theorems were `by decide` over the table â€” so the "theorems" only unpacked what was stipulated. Replaced with a structural derivation: (1) **Three inductives collapse to one structure**: `TemporalRegion`/`FrameAdverb`/`ReferenceDirection` deleted; `FrameAdverb (Time : Type*)` carries `name : String` + `period : Time â†’ Set Time` per @cite{condoravdi-2002} [28]'s intersective-predicate-modifier schema (`YESTERDAY â‰، خ»Pخ»wخ»t[AT(t âˆ© yesterday, w, P)]`). Concrete adverbs `yesterday`/`now_`/`tomorrow` define `period now := {t | t < now}` / `{now}` / `{now < t}`. (2) **`projectedRegion : Orientation â†’ Time â†’ Set Time`** reads off the AT relation directly: future maps to `{t | now â‰¤ t}` (from `atEventForward`/`atStateForward`'s `now â‰¤ e.start`/`now â‰¤ e.finish`); past maps to `{t | t < now}` (from `perfC`'s `t' < now`). (3) **`compatible : FrameAdverb Time â†’ Orientation â†’ Time â†’ Prop`** is now non-empty intersection of adverb period with projected region â€” Boolâ†’Prop migration per project-wide policy. (4) **All 8 theorems reproved structurally**: `tomorrow_present_compat [NoMaxOrder Time]` via `exists_gt`+`le_of_lt`; `yesterday_present_incompat` via `lt_of_lt_of_le`+`lt_irrefl`; `now_present_compat` via `le_refl`; `yesterday_past_compat [NoMinOrder Time]` via `exists_lt`; `tomorrow_past_incompat` via `lt_trans`+`lt_irrefl`. The eventive-vs-stative distinction at "now" is dropped from the formal predictions (AT permits `now = e.start` for `atEventForward`); the docstring of `now_present_compat` flags Condoravdi's "??He may get sick now" marking as a pragmatic event-duration fact, not a temporal-logic incompatibility â€” surfacing the previously-hidden mismatch between the lookup table and the AT semantics. Hacquard2006 (only consumer, uses `Perspective`) builds clean.

Two consumer docstrings updated (no code consumers). (2) **Split off `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Degree.lean`**: the `HasDegree`-typed predicates (`numeralExact`) and `CardinalityDegree : HasDegree Nat â„ڑ` instance moved to a new sibling file; deleted `numeralAtLeast`/`numeralApprox`/`numeralWithPrecision` (zero consumers). `KaoEtAl2014.lean` (Hyperbole) re-pointed to the new file. `Linglib.lean` updated. (4) **`exhNumeral` lost its unused `_maxN` parameter**: signature is now `exhNumeral (m n : Nat)`. Spector2013.lean's eight call sites updated (`exhNumeral 3 N n` â†’ `exhNumeral N n`). (5) **`typeLower` made unbounded**: now `âˆƒ k, k â‰¥ m âˆ§ exact k n` (no `maxN` / `List.range` bound); Decidable instance provided for the `bareMeaning` case via `decidable_of_iff` against `atLeastMeaning`. ScontrasPearl2021.lean's `typeLower bareMeaning 4 2 2` â†’ `typeLower bareMeaning 2 2`. (6) **`universal_closure_fails` generalized** from the m=2/maxN=3 spot check to: `âˆ€ kâ‚پ kâ‚‚, kâ‚پ â‰  kâ‚‚ â†’ آ¬âˆƒ n, âˆ€ k, k = kâ‚پ âˆ¨ k = kâ‚‚ â†’ n = k`. (7) **Class A/B proofs rewritten in direct term mode**: `classA_gt_excludes_bare := Nat.lt_irrefl m`, `classB_ge_includes_bare := Nat.le_refl m`, `classB_entailed_by_bare m n h := h â–¸ Nat.le_refl m`, `classA_not_entailed_by_bare m := âں¨rfl, Nat.lt_irrefl mâں©` â€” replaced 5-lemma `simp only` chains. (8) **Renamed `numerals_not_horn_scalar` â†’ `bare_not_horn_scale`** for parse clarity (only docstring consumers updated). (9) **Consolidated `open` blocks**: GQT bridges (آ§8) wrapped in `section GQTBridge / open Classical Core.IntensionalLogic Semantics.Quantification` instead of three repeated `open ... in`. (10) **Deleted آ§10 (single `Iff.rfl` `moreThan_iff_gt`), آ§11 (12 verification examples), آ§6 (`standardNumWords`/`standardWorlds` â€” used only by deleted spot-checks).** (11) **Header citations expanded**: added `@cite{chierchia-fox-spector-2012}`, `@cite{partee-1987}`, `@cite{fox-2007}`. Section list now 8 sections (was 14). All touched files (Numerals.Basic, Numerals.Degree, Spector2013, ScontrasPearl2021, KaoEtAl2014, FoxHackl2006, Alternatives.Lexical) build clean.

## [0.229.983] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Focus/Studies/HartmannZimmermann2007.lean` mathlib-PR-review pass: deepen the load-bearing claims, remove the window dressing.** Re-audited the file from a mathlib-maintainer perspective and either deepened or deleted every flagged item. **Deepenings** (1) `selkirk_BFR_fails_for_hausa` was a one-line `âˆƒ u, u.IsHausaLicensed âˆ§ u.cfg.strategy = .inSitu` that held by destructuring the registry and proved nothing about any actual universalist claim. Replaced with a structural triad: `FocusUtterance.HasMorphosyntacticReflex u := u.cfg.strategy = .exSitu âˆ¨ u.cfg.hasStab = true` (the property the BFR demands), `UniversalBFR : Prop := âˆ€ u, u.IsHausaLicensed â†’ u.HasMorphosyntacticReflex` (the universalist claim, restricted to morphosyntactic reflexes â€” already strong enough to fall on Hausa), and `hausa_falsifies_UniversalBFR : آ¬ UniversalBFR` proved structurally via `inSitu_newInfo` (eq. 23, *Naa tahoo dأ gأ  Bأ­إ™nin Kwأ nni* â€” in-situ, no stabilizer). The refutation now engages an *actual* universal-quantifier statement, not an existence proof in disguise. **Removals** (2) `multi_focus_attested` was a vacuous `And.intro` on two registry lookups â€” it didn't model two-focus *clauses*, just claimed two utterances are independently licensed; deleted. (3) `exSituMatrix`, `inSituMatrix`, `subjectFocusMatrix` were defined and never consumed; deleted (the flat `hzMatrix` is the only registry the universal theorems iterate). (4) `PragType.exhaustive` and `kiss_1998_falsified_for_hausa` removed: the Kiss-1998 refutation as written was tautological (`PragType.newInfo â‰  .exhaustive` holds by `decide` regardless of constructor semantics). The آ§3.2.5 docstring header now flags exhaustivity as out-of-scope pending an alternatives-semantics exhaustivity operator that can carry actual semantic content. **Renamings** (5) `FocusUtt` â†’ `FocusUtterance` (mathlib doesn't truncate). (6) `FocusUtt.HausaLicensed` â†’ `FocusUtterance.IsHausaLicensed`; downstream theorems renamed in lockstep (`all_hzMatrix_HausaLicensed` â†’ `all_hzMatrix_IsHausaLicensed`, `starred_inSitu_subject_not_HausaLicensed`, `licensed_exSitu_subject_HausaLicensed`, `inSitu_subject_subjunctive_HausaLicensed`). (7) `MSMH` / `MSMHGeneric` â†’ `MeaningStructureMapping` / `MeaningStructureMappingGeneric` (acronym in identifier flagged); `hausa_falsifies_MSMH` â†’ `hausa_falsifies_MeaningStructureMapping`. (8) Cell helpers `es`/`is_` â†’ `mkExSituUtt`/`mkInSituUtt`; cell aliases `es_*`/`is_*` â†’ `exSitu_*`/`inSitu_*` (eight cells أ— two name-form changes). **Polish** (9) Smart constructor `mkExSituUtt` no longer carries a tactic-default proof argument (`:= by intro _; rfl`) â€” explicit `(fun _ => rfl)` at every call site; mathlib avoids tactic defaults in core constructor APIs. (10) Header docstring stripped of unsupported claims: dropped the false promise to bridge `Semantics.FocusInterpretation.qaCongruent` for آ§4.3 (the integration was never delivered) and the prose claim that `Focused` should derive from a Fragment-side syntactic-position predicate (placeholder TODO that was distracting from the actual content). The `Focused` enum stays as a paper-specific tag for the آ§2.2.2 generalization; future syntactic-position derivation can be added when there's a concrete consumer demanding it. **Net** the file shrinks ~25 lines, gains one genuinely deeper theorem (`hausa_falsifies_UniversalBFR`), loses three vacuous theorems and three unused registries, and reads as Lean rather than as project shorthand. `lake build Linglib.Phenomena.Focus.Studies.HartmannZimmermann2007` clean (615 jobs).

## [0.229.982] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Singleton-alternative presupposition foundation for kya: + nandao (mathlib-style layered design).** Three new files plus particle bindings in two existing study files factor the @cite{bhatt-dayal-2020} eq. 23 analysis of Hindi-Urdu *kya:* (and the parallel Mandarin *nandao* analysis cited in fn. 11) into a foundational predicate, a felicity wrapper, and per-particle aliases â€” enforcing the CLAUDE.md "derive don't stipulate" discipline. (1) `Core/Issue/Singleton.lean`: foundational predicate `IsSingleton P := âˆƒ p, alt P = {p}` over `Core.Issue W`, plus characterization lemmas `isSingleton_iff_subsingleton_and_nonempty`, `IsSingleton.of_isDeclarative`, `not_isSingleton_of_two_alternatives`, the canonical-success case `isSingleton_declarative`, the `âٹ¤`/`âٹ¥` simp lemmas, and the structural failure theorem `not_isSingleton_polar_of_nontrivial` proving the standard two-cell @cite{ciardelli-groenendijk-roelofsen-2018} polar `polar p` (= `declarative p âٹ” declarative pل¶œ`) is **not** singleton when `p â‰  âˆ…, univ`. The latter captures algebraically why kya: cannot license a Hamblin polar â€” it requires the one-cell highlighted denotation. (2) `Theories/Semantics/Questions/Highlighted/Basic.lean`: the @cite{roelofsen-farkas-2015}/@cite{bolinger-1978} highlighted polar Q, defined as `highlightedPolar p := declarative p`. The dedicated alias signals consumer intent (singleton-presupposition reasoning, not informativity); `highlightedPolar p` is singleton by construction (`isSingleton_highlightedPolar` inherits from `isSingleton_declarative`); the bridge `polar_ne_highlightedPolar_of_nontrivial` makes the two-cell vs one-cell distinction crisp. `info_highlightedPolar : (highlightedPolar p).info = p` contrasts with `info_polar p = univ` â€” highlighted polar is informative about `p`, two-cell polar is not. (3) `Theories/Semantics/Questions/Presupposition/Singleton.lean`: particle-agnostic felicity wrapper. `singletonPresup Q := IsSingleton Q` is the predicate; `singletonInterp Q : Option (Core.Issue W)` is the partial-function interpretation pattern (mathlib's standard predicate + Op + bridge correspondence) returning `some Q` on felicitous inputs, `none` otherwise â€” faithful to eq. 23's "defined only when..., returns Q unchanged" semantics. Bridge `singletonInterp_isSome_iff` and the canonical-input theorems `singletonPresup_highlightedPolar`/`not_singletonPresup_polar_of_nontrivial`/`singletonPresup_declarative`. (4) `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/BhattDayal2020.lean` آ§4: `kyaPresup` and `kyaInterp` are particle-level aliases over the algebraic core, plus four empirical-prediction theorems (`kya_felicitous_highlightedPolar`, `kya_interp_highlightedPolar`, `kya_infelicitous_two_cell_polar`, `kya_polarQuestion_signals_singletonPresup`) and a fragment-bridge that connects kya's `polarQuestion = true` field to the singleton-presupposition analysis. (5) `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Zheng2025.lean` آ§4: `nandaoSingletonPresup` and `nandaoSingletonInterp` parallel aliases, the cross-particle agreement theorems `nandao_kya_share_singleton_presup` and `nandao_kya_share_interp` proving the @cite{bhatt-dayal-2020} fn. 11 cross-linguistic generalization holds **definitionally** (both unfold to the same `singletonPresup`) â€” true by construction rather than via bridge theorem, the desired CLAUDE.md "interconnection density" pattern. `Linglib.lean` adds the three new module imports. The mathlib-discipline win: every empirical claim about kya:/nandao felicity factors through the same algebraic predicate over `Issue`, so future particles encoding the same presupposition (e.g. additional Q-particles in other languages) can plug into the same foundation by aliasing.

## [0.229.981] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Stage G of Numerals API cleanup: ground the 5 meaning functions in `Core.Scale.{...}Deg id`.** Per the CLAUDE.md "import don't restipulate" principle, the five named numeral meanings in `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic.lean` آ§2 are now `abbrev`s over the polymorphic degree-property primitives in `Core/Scales/Scale.lean`: `bareMeaning := Core.Scale.eqDeg id`, `moreThanMeaning := Core.Scale.moreThanDeg id`, `fewerThanMeaning := Core.Scale.lessThanDeg id`, `atLeastMeaning := Core.Scale.atLeastDeg id`, `atMostMeaning := Core.Scale.atMostDeg id`. Connection to the maximal-informativity scale infrastructure now holds by construction, not by bridge theorem â€” آ§12 (Core.Scale Bridge Theorems) deleted entirely (was 4 trivial `Iff.rfl`s). `Decidable` instances re-derived via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (n â‰¥ m))` (etc.) since `abbrev` over a `def` doesn't auto-synthesize. Class A/B theorems (آ§5) and GQT bridges (آ§9, `gqt_atLeast_agrees`/`gqt_atMost_agrees`/`gqt_exactly_agrees`) had their `simp only` lists extended with `Core.Scale.{...}Deg, id_eq` to unfold the abbrev's right-hand side. Section list renumbered in the docstring (12 â†’ 14 sections, no Core.Scale Bridge section). `FoxHackl2006.lean` updated: deleted آ§1 (`atLeast_3_is_atLeastDeg`/`moreThan_3_is_moreThanDeg`) â€” vacuous after grounding; renumbered remaining sections; reworded module docstring to acknowledge that the bridge holds by construction. `Numerals.Basic` and `FoxHackl2006` build clean.

Dropped the redundant lines from `Theories/Semantics/Degree/Granularity.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Focus/BackgroundedIslands.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Exhaustivity.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Plurality/Distributivity.lean`, `Phenomena/Reference/Studies/Elbourne2013.lean`, `Phenomena/Quantification/Studies/RitchieSchiller2024.lean`, `Phenomena/Plurals/Studies/Kriz2016.lean`, `Phenomena/Dialogue/Studies/Ginzburg2012.lean`, `Phenomena/ScalarImplicatures/Studies/KatzirSingh2015.lean`, `Phenomena/ScalarImplicatures/Studies/CremersWilcoxSpector2023.lean`, `Core/Discourse/AtIssueness.lean`, `Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean`. Surfaces the true `Discourse.Issue` consumer set (now down to ~7 files). (D.3) Deleted `Discourse.refinesOn` def + `refinesOn_iff` theorem from `Core/QUD/Relevance.lean` and the four `bridge_q1_qa`/`bridge_q1_qb`/`bridge_qa_qai`/`bridge_qa_q1` theorems from `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/Roberts2012.lean`. The bridge_* theorems were `native_decide` spot-checks of the now-deleted general bridge `refinesOn_iff_questionEntails_of_partition` (removed in [0.229.978]'s Phase B); without the general theorem they were isolated Bool-equality assertions. The semantic content â€” partition refinement = question entailment â€” is preserved by `Core.Issue.fromSetoid_le_iff` (Set-based, no `worlds : List W` quantifier, no `isPartition` precondition).

## [0.229.979] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Focus/Studies/HartmannZimmermann2007.lean` audit + mathlib-style refactor + 6 new theorems; `Fragments/Hausa/TAM.lean` PAC inventory widening.** Re-verified the H&Z 2007 paper end-to-end and corrected several memory-driven inaccuracies and structural concerns. (1) **PAC widening (`TAM.lean`)**: added five paper-grounded PACs to enable per-example PAC accuracy in the H&Z matrix â€” `cmp_3sf_R` "ta" (eq. 24), `cont_3sf_R` "takأ¨e" (eq. 22), `cmp_1sg_G` "naa" (eq. 23), `cont_1sg_R` "nakأ¨e" (eq. 29), `fut_1sg` "zأ¢n" (eqs. 25, 30). `representativePACs` registry expanded from 5 to 10 entries; `all_representative_PACs_wellFormed` proof updated to 10 `rfl`s. The Hausa "4sg" impersonal *أ a/akأ¨e* (eqs. 26-27) deferred â€” `Core.Person.Category` does not yet expose an impersonal/4sg cell. (2) **PAC remap in H&Z matrix**: every `es_*`/`is_*` cell now uses the empirically correct PAC instead of the previous `cmp_3sm_R`/`cmp_3sm_G` placeholders â€” `es_newInfo` â†’ `cont_3sf_R`, `es_corrective` â†’ `cmp_3sf_R`, `es_selective` â†’ `cont_1sg_R`, `is_newInfo` â†’ `cmp_1sg_G`, `is_corrective`/`is_selective` â†’ `fut_1sg`. The two impersonal-subject cells (`es_contrastive`/`is_contrastive`) keep `cmp_3sm_R`/`cmp_3sm_G` with explicit docstring caveats noting the approximation. (3) **`HausaLicensed` correctness fix**: the previous unconditional subject-focus rule contradicted paper p. 4 ("subject foci are syntactically and morphologically unmarked in the future, habitual and subjunctive aspects"). New version conditions on `u.cfg.pac.tam.HasRelativeForm`: `u.cfg.Licensed âˆ§ ((u.focused = .subject âˆ§ u.cfg.pac.tam.HasRelativeForm) â†’ u.cfg.strategy = .exSitu)`. New theorem `inSitu_subject_subjunctive_HausaLicensed` witnesses the qualification (3sg.M subjunctive subject in-situ is licensed because subjunctive lacks a relative form). (4) **`starred_inSitu_subject` PAC fix**: paper آ§2.2.2 example *Daudأ a ya-nأ a kirأ -ntأ * uses *yanؤپ* (continuous), not the previous incorrect *yؤپ* (completive). Repointed to `cont_3sm_G`. (5) **MSMH polymorphism**: new `MSMHGeneric {خ±} (interp : FocusUtt â†’ خ±) : Prop` polymorphic over the interpretation function; the original `MSMH` is now defined as `MSMHGeneric FocusUtt.pragType`. Lets future interpretation functions (exhaustivity status, discourse-relation tag) reuse the same refutation skeleton. (6) **`PragType.exhaustive` + Kiss 1998 refutation**: added the fifth pragmatic case from آ§3.2.5 (where H&Z argue exhaustive is a *pragmatic* â€” not syntactic â€” use of focus, against @cite{kiss-1998}'s identification of ex-situ with structural exhaustivity). New `kiss_1998_falsified_for_hausa : âˆƒ u, u.cfg.strategy = .exSitu âˆ§ u.pragType â‰  .exhaustive` witnesses the refutation via `es_newInfo`. (7) **Selkirk BFR refutation (آ§6.2)**: `selkirk_BFR_fails_for_hausa : âˆƒ u, u.HausaLicensed âˆ§ u.cfg.strategy = .inSitu` â€” registers the categorical claim that an in-situ Hausa-licensed utterance with no morphosyntactic reflex of focus suffices to refute the universalist Basic Focus Rule. The آ§5 prosodic-pilot quantitative tendencies stay in docstring prose per CLAUDE.md (not Lean theorems). (8) **Multi-focus co-occurrence (آ§2.3)**: new `multi_focus_attested` proves any two H&Z matrix cells are jointly licensed (the آ§2.3 closure observation). (9) **Matrix splitting**: `hzMatrix` decomposed into `exSituMatrix` / `inSituMatrix` / `subjectFocusMatrix` for finer-grained registries. (10) **Vacuous theorem deleted**: `stabilizer_tone_independent_of_allomorph` was a one-line `rfl` because `Stabilizer.toneAfter` ignores its first argument by definition; kept the load-bearing `polar_tone_from_polarOf` cross-fragment bridge. (11) **Smart constructors `es`/`is_` generalised**: now take an explicit `PAC` argument (and gender/number for ex-situ) rather than hard-coding `cmp_3sm_R`/`cmp_3sm_G`, threading the relative-form proof obligation through `mkExSitu`. (12) **Subject-focus theorem updated**: `subject_focus_only_exSitu` now takes an `hRel : u.cfg.pac.tam.HasRelativeForm` premise, matching the new conditional licensing rule. The previous CHANGELOG entry [0.229.976] mentioned the example *Mأ؛usaa nل¸•e ya nأ©emأ a* â€” that was hallucinated; the actual paper آ§2.2.2 minimal pair is *Daudأ a (nee) ya-kأ¨e kirأ -ntأ * / *\*Daudأ a ya-nأ a kirأ -ntأ * and the file uses the correct examples. Builds clean for both touched files.

## [0.229.978] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`BhattDayal2020.lean` rewritten as fragment-driven typology bridge + Japanese fragment cleanup + new `Fragments/English/QuestionParticles.lean`.** Completing the QP fragment cleanup pattern (theory-neutral lexical primitives in `Fragments/`; per-paper layer assignments in `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/`): (1) `Fragments/Japanese/Particles.lean` strips its local `Layer` inductive (cp/perspP/sap) and the `layer : Layer` field from `ParticleEntry` â€” matching the same anti-pattern just removed from the 10 other QP fragments. The five Japanese particles (*ka*, *no*, *koto*, *kke*, *daroo*) now carry only distributional fields (inMatrix/inSubordinated/inQuasiSub). Verified `Fragments/Japanese/OutlookMarkers` (Part 2 of the same file) and `Phenomena/Modality/OutlookMarkers.lean` only consume the OutlookMarkers namespace, not the stripped `Layer`. (2) New `Fragments/English/QuestionParticles.lean` houses the *quick/quickly* MQP-like adverb entry (matrix-only urgency adverb), giving English a proper QP fragment file alongside Mandarin/German/Swedish/Slavic. (3) `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/BhattDayal2020.lean` rewritten: imports `Fragments/HindiUrdu/Particles`, `Fragments/Japanese/Particles`, `Fragments/English/QuestionParticles`; defines per-particle layer overlays `hindi_urdu_kya_layer`/`japanese_ka_layer`/`japanese_kke_layer`/`english_quick_layer` (each takes the fragment entry as `_` arg, signaling theoretical overlay rather than computed property); the four `QParticleDatum` rows derive `form`/`inSubordinated`/`inQuasiSub` from fragment fields rather than re-stipulating literals. The three typology theorems (`cp_particles_in_subordination`, `perspP_particles_not_in_subordination`, `sap_particles_not_in_quasi_sub`) re-prove cleanly against the fragment-driven structure. (4) `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Dayal2025.lean::particle_layer_predicts_embedding` simplified to delegate to the three BhattDayal2020 theorems via `refine âں¨?_, ?_, ?_âں©` â€” no longer redundantly enumerates the four particles. (5) Stale `Phenomena.Questions.Studies.QPLayerTypology` references in 7 Slavic QP fragment docstrings (Russian, Bulgarian, Ukrainian, Polish, Slovenian, Serbian, Macedonian) repointed to `Phenomena.Questions.Studies.Simik2024` (the actual home of Slavic layer assignments after the prior session's typology aggregator deletion). (6) `Linglib.lean` adds `import Linglib.Fragments.English.QuestionParticles`. Touched files build clean.

## [0.229.977] - 2026-04-18

### Removed
- **Stage E of Numerals API cleanup: strip `NumeralTheory` + `LowerBound`/`Exact` instances + derived/comparison properties from `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic.lean`.** Per the mathlib-discipline question that motivated the whole cleanup ("is `NumeralTheory` good or are we overengineering?"), the answer was: `(Nat â†’ Nat â†’ Prop) أ— Decidable-witness` bundled as a structure is overengineered when both implementations (`LowerBound.meaning = atLeastMeaning âˆک toNat`, `Exact.meaning = bareMeaning âˆک toNat`) are pointwise reducible to the underlying meaning functions. Sole external consumer of `LowerBound`/`Exact`/`NumeralTheory.*` was Basic.lean's own آ§8/آ§9 derived-property and comparison-theorem tables (`compatibleWorlds`, `compatibleCount`, `hasAmbiguity`, `isMonotonic`, `isStrongerThan`, `theoriesAgreeAt`, `theoriesAgreeOn`, `theoriesEquivalent`, `divergingWorlds`, `hasMoreAmbiguity` + ~25 `decide`-proof lemmas wrapping them). The آ§10 grounding theorems (`bare_eq_exact_*`, `atLeast_eq_lowerBound_*`) collapsed to `rfl` precisely *because* the instances were thin reducibility wrappers â€” they were trivially-true bridges between two names for the same function, the inverse anti-pattern of the "encode conclusions as definitions" warning in CLAUDE.md. Kept: the five core Prop meaning functions (`bareMeaning`, `moreThanMeaning`, `fewerThanMeaning`, `atLeastMeaning`, `atMostMeaning`), `BareNumeral`, `NumeralExpr.meaning` polymorphic over the bare-numeral semantics, alternative sets, Class A/B theorems, type-shifting (Kennedy آ§3.1), EXH-typeshift duality (Spector آ§6.2), GQT bridges (`gqt_atLeast_agrees`/`gqt_atMost_agrees`/`gqt_exactly_agrees`), Core.Scale degree bridges (آ§12), HasDegree-based numeral expression semantics, precision-mode numerals. `lowerBound_from_exact_typeshift` now reads `atLeastMeaning w.toNat n â†” typeLower bareMeaning 3 w.toNat n` directly (no `LowerBound.meaning` indirection). Renumbered remaining sections sequentially (1-15) and updated the docstring section list. Updated `Theories/Semantics/Alternatives/Lexical.lean` line 220 docstring (replaced stale `NumeralTheory.isStrongerThan` reference with `numerals_not_horn_scalar`/`atLeast_strictly_weaker_than_bare`). Affected files (`Numerals.Basic`, `Alternatives.Lexical`, `Spector2013`, `Kennedy2015`, `BylininaNouwen2020`) build clean; pre-existing failures in `Modality.Studies.Rubinstein2014`/`Ferreira2023` are concurrent-session work, untouched here.

## [0.229.976] - 2026-04-18

### Removed
- **Stage D of Numerals API cleanup: dissolve `Theories/Pragmatics/Implicature/NegationScope.lean` + `Theories/Pragmatics/Implicature/ScalarImplicatures/Operations.lean`.** Both were paper-replication files mashed into `Theories/` in violation of the CLAUDE.md Theoriesâ†”Phenomena split. `NegationScope.lean` (275 lines) replicated @cite{horn-1972}'s internal/external negation scope asymmetry as a `NegationScope` enum + `negatedMeaning T w scope k` parameterized over `NumeralTheory` + 8 `decide` lemmas; sole external consumer was `Phenomena/Quantification/Studies/ScontrasPearl2021.lean`, which used exactly two of the lemmas (`exact_collapses_negation_scope`/`lowerBound_preserves_negation_scope`) â€” both are now inlined as direct `bareMeaning`/`atLeastMeaning` calls in `ScontrasPearl2021.lean` with no scope enum at all (the asymmetry between exact and lower-bound semantics is now visible in two adjacent `decide` proofs rather than mediated by a `NegationScope` parameter). `Operations.lean` (325 lines) replicated Horn's `assert/contradict/suspend/cancel` implicature operations as an `ImplicatureOperation` enum + `operationFelicitous`/`hasImplicatureTarget` predicates â€” zero external consumers, deleted outright. Both can be revived in proper @cite{horn-1972}-grounded study files (e.g. `Phenomena/Polarity/Studies/Horn1972.lean`, `Phenomena/ScalarImplicatures/Studies/Horn1972.lean`) when those papers receive full formalizations. Removed corresponding imports from `Linglib.lean`.

## [0.229.975] - 2026-04-18

- **Stage C of Numerals API cleanup: dissolve `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Embedding.lean`.** The 585-line file mashed together 14 paper-replication sections (negation/modal/exactly/conditional/EXH/almost/only/QUD-convexity/Heim-Kennedy/imperative divergences across @cite{horn-1972}, @cite{kennedy-2015}, @cite{bylinina-nouwen-2020}, @cite{spector-2013}, @cite{penka-2006}, @cite{nouwen-2006}, @cite{coppock-beaver-2014}, @cite{solt-waldon-2019}, @cite{heim-2000}, @cite{musolino-2004}) parameterized over `NumeralTheory`, violating the CLAUDE.md Theoriesâ†”Phenomena split (paper replications belong in `Phenomena/<X>/Studies/`, not `Theories/`). Verified `Phenomena/Numerals/Studies/Spector2013.lean` is the *sole* external consumer; ported the six combinators it actually uses (`negatedMeaning`, `possibilityMeaning`, `necessityMeaning`, `exh`, `exhUnderPossibility`, `exhOverPossibility`) as local helpers (`notLB`/`notEx`/`possLB`/`possEx`/`necLB`/`necEx`/`exhLB`/`exhUnderPossLB`/`exhOverPossLB`) directly inlined to `atLeastMeaning`/`bareMeaning` â€” no `NumeralTheory` parameter, ready for Stage E (`NumeralTheory` deletion). Removed `import Linglib.Theories.Semantics.Numerals.Embedding` from `Linglib.lean`. The آ§3-آ§14 paper-citation theorems (e.g. `negation_divergence_above`, `almost_diverges_above`, `heimKennedy_nominal_blocked`) had zero consumers and were exposition-only `decide` proofs; they can be revived in proper `Penka2006`/`CoppockBeaver2014`/`Heim2000`/`SoltWaldon2019` study files when those papers receive full formalizations. Affected files (`Spector2013`, `Linglib`, deleted `Embedding.lean`) build clean; pre-existing failures in `Dynamic.Epistemic.Basic`/`Modality.Studies.*` are concurrent-session work, unrelated to this change.

## [0.229.974] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Hausa fragments audit cleanup: verb-example dehallucination + cross-fragment polarity bridge.** (1) `Fragments/Hausa/VerbGrades.lean`: dropped `@cite{parsons-1960}` from the header (no bib entry; replaced with prose attribution to Newman's codification), and replaced three hallucinated/wrong verb examples against @cite{newman-2000} ch. 74 â€” `aikee` (wrong tone/vowel for gr5) â†’ `sayar` 'sell' (genuine gr5 efferential of `saya` 'buy', minimal pair); `zoo` 'come' (irregular v*, not gr6) â†’ `koomoo` 'return here' (genuine gr6 ventive); `biyu` (numeral, not a verb at all) â†’ `taaru` 'meet' (genuine gr7 sustentative).
