(3) **`Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean` stripped** to just the `ArticleInventory` structure, `licensesKind`, `toMarkingParams`, `toMarkingStrategy`, `toArticleType`, the universal `licensesKind_bare` simp lemma, and the universal `toArticleType_eq_strategyToArticleType`. The five language defs and five `_marking` theorems are gone. (4) **Cross-language collapse theorems migrated to `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` آ§14** (`english_mandarin_articleType_collapse`, `english_mandarin_inventory_distinct`) â€” these are observations *about* a typological pattern, not facts about Core, so they belong at the Phenomena layer. (5) **`Moroney2021.lean` آ§14 rewritten** to import the four new fragments (plus the existing Shan one), introduce `englishInv`/`germanInv`/`mandarinInv`/`thaiInv`/`shanInv` abbrevs anchored to `Fragments.X.Definiteness.articleInventory`, and use those in all the existing inventory-bridge theorems (`inventory_agrees_with_params`, `inventory_derives_all_languages`, `shan_anaphoric_not_licensed_via_article`, etc.). New `mandarin_in_markedAnaphoric` makes the @cite{jenks-2018} backdrop visible. (6) **`Linglib.lean`** adds the four new fragment imports near each language's existing block. No semantic change; pure file relocation that brings the redesigned API into compliance with the strict `Theories â†’ Fragments â†’ Phenomena` dependency hierarchy. Build clean (988 jobs).

## [0.229.905] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Stage 7 of the Definiteness API redesign â€” Moroney2021 wired to `Core.Nominal`.** Verifies that the new `ArticleInventory` / `NominalKind` / `interpret` machinery reproduces and refines Moroney's typological analysis end-to-end, treating the existing study file as an integration test for the redesigned API. (1) **Thai inventory added.** `ArticleInventory.thai` (no indefinite, no unique, anaphoric-via-demonstrative, demonstrative paradigm, possessive paradigm) joins the language inventory; `thai_marking : ArticleInventory.thai.toMarkingStrategy = .markedAnaphoric` matches the آ§7 derivation. Same `markedAnaphoric` cell as Mandarin under Moroney's typology. (2) **`Moroney2021.lean` آ§14 added â€” "Integration with the Core.Nominal API".** Imports `Core.Nominal.{ArticleInventory,Description,Interpret}` and re-derives the four-language Table 4.4 typology from the inventory layer rather than the params layer: `inventory_agrees_with_params` proves `ArticleInventory.X.toMarkingParams = XParams` for English/German/Thai/Shan by `rfl`, witnessing that the inventory is the upstream object from which آ§7's `englishParams`/`germanParams`/`thaiParams`/`shanParams` fall out (no information loss for the params projection â€” the inventory carries strictly more data). `inventory_derives_all_languages` mirrors آ§7's `derive_all_languages` at the inventory level. (3) **Licensing predictions sharpen Moroney's central finding.** `shan_anaphoric_not_licensed_via_article` â€” Shan's `ArticleInventory.licensesKind` returns `false` for `.anaphoric` `NominalKind`s (no anaphoric article, no syncretic unique article either) â€” yet `shan_bare_licensed` and `shan_demonstrative_licensed` both return `true`, exposing the morphological substrate for Moroney's analysis: Shan expresses anaphoric definiteness through bare nouns + optional demonstratives, never through a dedicated article. The contrast with English (`english_anaphoric_licensed_via_syncretism` â€” *the* covers anaphoric via syncretism) and German (`german_anaphoric_licensed_via_strong_article` â€” independent strong article) makes the three modes of anaphoric licensing explicit at the inventory level. (4) **Interpretation agreement theorems for Shan.** `shan_bare_unique_agreement` is the Shan-named consequence of the universal `Core.Nominal.interpret_bare_eq_unique` â€” bare and uniqueness definites over the same restrictor select the same referent, the type-theoretic correlate of Moroney's claim that Shan bare nouns express weak/uniqueness definiteness via unblocked خ¹. `shan_demonstrative_anaphoric_agreement` is the Shan-named consequence of `interpret_demonstrative_eq_anaphoric` â€” *nأ¢j*/*nأ¢n* and the bare anaphoric reading pick the same entity at a shared discourse index, formalizing Moroney's آ§2.1.3 finding that the demonstrative's deictic feature is a presupposition filter, not a referent selector. (5) **`Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean` opens fixed.** `open Core.IntensionalLogic` and `open Core.IntensionalLogic.Variables` added at namespace top so the `Frame` and `DenotGS` references in `licensesKind` and `licensesKind_bare` resolve under `autoImplicit := false`. No new sorrys; no consumer-side changes outside the study file.

## [0.229.904] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core.Assignment` moved back to `Core` namespace (file-location revert of 0.229.899; wrapper-deletion preserved).** Stage 11 had pushed the polymorphic `Assignment خ± := â„• â†’ خ±` abbrev into `Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` to break a name clash with the now-deleted `Variables.Assignment F` wrapper. The clash is gone, and the IL-namespaced location was conceptually wrong: `Assignment` is **pre-intensional substrate** â€” pure Tarski variable mapping, used by extensional frameworks (plain DPL, classical model theory) just as much as by intensional ones â€” and didn't belong inside the intensional namespace. The intensional substrate (`Frame`, `SitAssignment F := Assignment F.Index`, `DenotGS`) builds on top of `Core.Assignment`, not the other way around. (1) **File:** `Linglib/Core/IntensionalLogic/Assignment.lean` deleted; the abbrev + `update` + `update_at` / `update_ne` / `update_overwrite` / `update_comm` / `update_self` lemmas are now at the top of `Linglib/Core/Assignment.lean` under `namespace Core` (alongside the `PartialAssign`, `PluralAssign`, `VarAssignment` abbrevs already there). The merged file is one logical module â€” variable assignments, total and partial. (2) **Consumers updated:** `Linglib/Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean` (import `Linglib.Core.Assignment`), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra.lean` (import + `open Core (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/VarAssignment.lean` (drops the redundant IL import; `open Core (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/DynamicSemantics.lean` (`open Core (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/Core/CCP.lean` (import + `open _root_.Core (Assignment)` + re-export switched back: `export _root_.Core (Assignment)` and `export _root_.Core.Assignment (update update_at update_ne update_overwrite update_comm)`), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/DynamicGQ/UpdateTheoretic.lean` (`Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` â†’ `Core.Assignment`, 9 occurrences). (3) **`Linglib.lean`:** drops the `import Linglib.Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` line. No semantic change; pure namespace move that aligns the file location with the conceptual layer (extensional substrate at top of `Core`, intensional substrate under `Core.IntensionalLogic`).

## [0.229.903] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core/NullSubject.lean` mathlib-style hardening.** Five-part follow-up to 0.229.902. (1) **Prop + Decidable.** `satisfiesImplicational : ProDropProfile â†’ Bool` is now `ProDropProfile.Satisfies : ProDropProfile â†’ Prop` (`p.hasOvertPRO = true â†’ p.allowsProDrop = false`) with a derived `Decidable` instance, so downstream theorems are closed by `decide` rather than `rfl` over the Boolean encoding. The Prop form composes with other logical predicates and matches the mathlib convention for universals. (2) **Contrapositive.** New `prodrop_excludes_overt_pro : p.Satisfies â†’ p.allowsProDrop = true â†’ p.hasOvertPRO = false` â€” the practically useful direction of the universal (given a *pro*-drop language, predict the absence of overt PRO). `Allotey2021.lean` exhibits the contrapositive concretely as `ga_prodrop_would_exclude_overt_pro`. (3) **Typology enum.** New `Core.NullSubject.Typology` enumerates the four cells (`nullPRO_noProDrop`, `nullPRO_proDrop`, `overtPRO_noProDrop`, `overtPRO_proDrop`); `Typology.isAttested` marks the fourth as forbidden; `ProDropProfile.classify` projects a profile to a cell; `satisfies_iff_attested` proves `p.Satisfies â†” p.classify.isAttested = true`. The cells are now first-class objects rather than implicit Boolean tuples â€” makes "the forbidden cell" a named referent. `Ostrove2026.lean` adds `smpm_classified`, `english_classified`, `buli_classified` cell-by-cell theorems. (4) **`hasOvertPRO` widened to non-nullness.** `MinPronInventory.hasOvertPRO` now tests `controlForm != .null` rather than `== .pronoun`. The typological criterion @cite{ostrove-2026} states is overt-vs-null, not pronoun-vs-everything-else: an inventory whose controlled-subject form was `.reflexive` would equally count as overt PRO. All current inventories yield either `.null` or `.pronoun` so verdicts are unchanged; the widened definition is faithful to the stated criterion. (5) **Old API removed.** `satisfiesImplicational : Bool`, `overt_pro_non_prodrop_satisfies`, `null_pro_satisfies` are gone â€” replaced by `Satisfies` + `null_pro_satisfies` + `overt_no_prodrop_satisfies` (Prop versions). The two consumer sites (`Allotey2021.lean`, `Ostrove2026.lean`) are updated to `by decide` proofs and the new lemma names; no other call sites existed.

## [0.229.902] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`ProDropProfile` lifted from `Phenomena/Control/` to `Core/NullSubject.lean`.** Pro-drop / overt-PRO is a typological parameter, not a control phenomenon: any syntactic framework must engage with it, and it is consumed by control studies but not specific to them. New `Core/NullSubject.lean` holds the framework-agnostic parameter (`ProDropProfile`) and @cite{ostrove-2026}'s implicational universal (`satisfiesImplicational`, `overt_pro_non_prodrop_satisfies`, `null_pro_satisfies`) â€” zero theory dependencies, modeled on the existing `Core/SubjectProperties.lean`. The Minimalism-side bridge function (was `Phenomena.Control.hasOvertPRO : MinPronInventory PronForm â†’ Bool`) is now `MinPronInventory.hasOvertPRO` in `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/MinimalPronoun.lean` آ§ 3 (where `MinPronInventory` and `PronForm` already live), so the layer that depends on Minimalism stays inside Minimalism. `language : String` field dropped from `ProDropProfile` â€” Core types should not carry per-language metadata; the calling site (`gaProfile`, `smpmProfile`, etc.) is the natural anchor for the language label. `Studies/Allotey2021.lean` and `Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` updated to `open Core.NullSubject` and use `inv.hasOvertPRO` dot-notation; `buliProfile` extracted as a top-level def in `Ostrove2026.lean` (was an inline structure literal). Old `Phenomena/Control/ProDropProfile.lean` deleted; `Linglib.lean` import line moved from the Phenomena block to the Core block.

## [0.229.901] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean` â€” Stage 6 of the Definiteness API redesign.** Records a language's surface inventory of nominal forms as the upstream object from which the existing typology classifications fall out. (1) **`ArticleInventory` structure** with `hasIndefinite`, `hasUniqueArticle`, `hasAnaphoricArticle`, `uniqueAnaphoricSyncretism` (English *the* covers both vs German distinct weak/strong), `hasDemonstrative`, `hasPossessive`. Bare nominals are not recorded â€” every language allows them at some level; the question is what readings they admit, which is a matter of the type-shift hierarchy, not the article inventory. (2) **`licensesKind {F : Frame} (inv) : NominalKind F â†’ Bool`** â€” pattern-match on the constructor: `.bare â†¦ true` (universal); `.indefinite â†¦ hasIndefinite`; `.unique â†¦ hasUniqueArticle`; `.anaphoric â†¦ hasAnaphoricArticle âˆ¨ (hasUniqueArticle âˆ§ syncretism)` (English *the* counts via syncretism); `.demonstrative â†¦ hasDemonstrative`; `.possessive â†¦ hasPossessive`. The morphological-availability claim, not a grammaticality claim about every NP. `licensesKind_bare` is the universal bare-licensing simp lemma. (3) **Derived classifications.** `toMarkingParams : ArticleInventory â†’ Core.Definiteness.DefMarkingParams` projects to the Moroney 3-boolean shape (the inventory carries strictly more information â€” demonstrative and possessive paradigms are discarded). `toMarkingStrategy := deriveStrategy âˆک toMarkingParams` and `toArticleType := strategyToArticleType âˆک toMarkingStrategy` re-use the existing derivation chain in `Core/Definiteness.lean` rather than restipulating it. (4) **Language instances** for English (syncretic *the*, full paradigm), German (distinct weak/strong, full paradigm), Mandarin (anaphoric-only, demonstrative + possessive), Shan (no articles, demonstrative + possessive). (5) **Consistency theorems.** `english_marking`, `german_marking`, `mandarin_marking`, `shan_marking` derive the four Moroney cells (`.generallyMarked`, `.bipartite`, `.markedAnaphoric`, `.unmarked`) from the four inventories by `rfl`. `english_mandarin_articleType_collapse` witnesses the lossiness of `ArticleType` relative to `DefMarkingStrategy`: distinct inventories with distinct strategies (`.generallyMarked` vs `.markedAnaphoric`) both collapse to `.weakOnly`; `english_mandarin_inventory_distinct` shows the inventories themselves are not collapsed, so the structure has finer resolution than either downstream typology. `Linglib.lean` import added. No existing call sites touched.

## [0.229.900] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Allotey 2021 cleanup pass.** Six-part rework of the 0.229.896 initial drop after a re-read of @cite{allotey-2021} surfaced inaccuracies and a build failure. (1) **ASCII identifier policy.** Lean 4 rejects IPA `ة›` (U+025B) and `إ‹` (U+014B) as identifier characters; the original `Fragments/Ga/Basic.lean` and `Predicates.lean` did not compile. All constructor and definition names are now ASCII (`ake`, `keji`, `nye`, `kpleno`, `kpang`, `kee`, `finiteAke`, `finiteKeji`, `hiekpano`); IPA orthography lives in the `.form` strings only. Module docstring records the policy. (2) **Verb-list correction.** Replaced `kai` 'remember' (not in the paper) with `hiekpano` 'forget' (Allotey ex 37â€“38, the actual attested negative-implicative subject-control verb); dropped `susu` 'think' (not attested as Allotey CTP); kept `kee` 'say' as the standard `akة›`-clause exemplar with a docstring noting it is the only entry not in Allotey's CTP list (kept so the OC-vs-non-OC contrast can be exhibited end-to-end). (3) **`ProDropProfile` factored out.** Moved `ProDropProfile`, `satisfiesImplicational`, `hasOvertPRO`, `overt_pro_non_prodrop_satisfies`, `null_pro_satisfies` from `Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` (where they were stranded) into a new top-level data file `Phenomena/Control/ProDropProfile.lean`. Both `Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` and `Studies/Allotey2021.lean` import from there; the local re-statement in `Allotey2021.lean` is gone. (4) **Derive-don't-stipulate for OC.** `gaOCSignature` is now `if (clauseProperties c).noncoreferentialSubject then ocNone else ocFull` â€” derived from the noncoreferential-subject flag rather than stipulated per clause. `gaAgr` is now `(clauseProperties c).finiteComplementizer`, with bridge theorem `gaAgr_eq_complementizer_isFinite` connecting the Agr flag to the complementizer's finiteness via `complementizer_isFinite_eq_finiteFlag` (also new in `Basic.lean`). New universal `oc_iff_no_noncoreferential` makes the structural derivation visible. The previous `irrealisNi_is_OC : ... := rfl` is no longer a tautology over a stipulated mapping but follows from the derived definition. (5) **Irrealis-vs-subjunctive diagnostic.** New section adds `isSubjunctiveLike (c : EmbeddedClauseType) : Bool := (clauseProperties c).noncoreferentialSubject` (a clause type passes the subjunctive test iff it allows a noncoreferential embedded subject â€” the Romance obviation diagnostic). Three theorems: `irrealisNi_not_subjunctive`, `finiteAke_subjunctive_test_passes`, `finiteKeji_subjunctive_test_passes`. Formalizes Allotey's argument against the @cite{dakubu-2004} / @cite{campbell-2017} subjunctive analysis. The `gaToNoonan` mapping still sends `irrealisNi â†’ .subjunctive` (Noonan's typological label, separate from the generative-grammar subjunctive); docstring records the distinction. (6) **Long Distance Agree analysis.** New `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/LongDistanceAgree.lean` formalizes @cite{szabolcsi-2009}'s three-condition LDA configuration (probe has valued د†, goal has unvalued د†, intervening C is non-defective) with `LDAConfig.licenses` and three structural impossibility theorems (`blocking_c_defeats_LDA`, `valued_goal_has_no_LDA_target`, `unvalued_probe_cannot_LDA`). `Allotey2021.lean` now wires `gaLDAConfig`, `ga_satisfies_LDA`, and `ga_LDA_implies_OC` to make Allotey's actual analytical contribution (LDA-driven cross-clausal valuation of the embedded minimal pronoun) visible â€” previously the formalization plugged Allotey only into the @cite{ostrove-2026} minimal-pronoun typology. **Bib:** added stubs for `szabolcsi-2009`, `satik-2019`, `dakubu-2004`, `campbell-2017` (all with `note` fields flagging fields that were not verifiable from training data). `Linglib.lean` adds `Phenomena.Control.ProDropProfile` and `Theories.Syntax.Minimalism.LongDistanceAgree` imports.

## [0.229.899] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core.Assignment` consolidated into `Core.IntensionalLogic`.** The polymorphic `Assignment E := â„• â†’ E` abbrev (and its `update` / `update_at` / `update_ne` / `update_overwrite` / `update_comm` / `update_self` lemmas) move from `Linglib/Core/Assignment.lean` to a new `Linglib/Core/IntensionalLogic/Assignment.lean` and live under the `Core.IntensionalLogic` namespace. The redundant `abbrev Assignment (F : Frame) := Core.Assignment F.Entity` wrapper in `Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean` is dropped; entity-pronoun signatures now read `Assignment F.Entity` directly, situation-pronoun signatures keep `SitAssignment F`. Motivation: the wrapper had the same name as the polymorphic abbrev but a different arity (`Frame` vs `Type*`), which silently lost name resolution against `open Core.IntensionalLogic` and forced fully-qualified `Variables.Assignment F` everywhere in `Core/Nominal/Interpret.lean` (Stage 5 of the Definiteness redesign). With both names unified to `Assignment` over `Type*`, `Core/Nominal/Interpret.lean` drops the qualifier. Updated callers: `Linglib/Core/Assignment.lean` (now hosts only `PartialAssign` / `PluralAssign` / `VarAssignment`, imports the new IL/Assignment), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra.lean` and `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/VarAssignment.lean` and `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/DynamicSemantics.lean` (`open Core (Assignment)` â†’ `open Core.IntensionalLogic (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/Core/CCP.lean` (re-export switched to the IL namespace), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/DynamicGQ/UpdateTheoretic.lean` (`Core.Assignment` â†’ `Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` qualifier, 5 occurrences), `Linglib.lean` import added. No semantic change; pure namespace move. Stops the clash that bit Stage 5 and unblocks Stage 6.

## [0.229.898] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR J (interrogative completes the 3أ—3 Portner table).** Three coordinated additions wire interrogative force into the Mood substrate so that the @cite{portner-2018} unification table gains its third column. (1) **`Core/Mood/POSWQ.lean`** â€” `structure POSWQ extends POSW` adds an `inquiry : Setoid W` (the QUD-induced answer partition; `âٹ¤` = no question). The third sentence-mood update `POSWQ.inquire c q := { c with inquiry := c.inquiry âٹ“ q }` is the partition-side analogue of `POSW.plus` (refining `cs`) and `POSW.star` (refining `lt`); `inquire_targets_disjoint_components` proves the partition operation leaves both `cs` and `lt` untouched (the third leg of @cite{portner-2018}'s thesis that mood updates target disjoint substrate components). The third necessity modal `POSWQ.boxAns c p := âˆ€ w v, c.cs w â†’ c.cs v â†’ c.inquiry.r w v â†’ (p w â†” p v)` says `p` has constant truth value within each inquiry cell â€” informational answerhood, the modal counterpart of `POSW.boxCs`/`boxLt`. The refinement preorder lifts componentwise (`Preorder (POSWQ W)`); `inquire_le_self`, `inquire_mono`, and `boxAns_anti` mirror the POSW preorder layer. `boxAns` is *not* upward-monotone in `p` (a strengthening can break per-cell constancy), but it *is* closed under negation (`boxAns_not`) and conjunction (`boxAns_and`) â€” answers compose like ordinary propositions. Concrete two-cell separation witness `sepPOSWQ` proves `boxAns_not_reducible_to_boxCs`: there exists a POSWQ where `boxAns p` holds but `boxCs p` fails, so the inquiry component is doing genuine work. (2) **`Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean` POSWQ bridge.** `polarSetoid q : Setoid W` (two worlds equivalent iff they agree on `q`) is the smallest piece of partition structure a single yes/no question contributes; `qudInquiry K : Setoid W` folds the QUD stack as iterated meet of polar Setoids (with `âٹ¤` as identity); `toPOSWQ K : POSWQ W := { K.toPOSW with inquiry := K.qudInquiry }` projects the scoreboard into the POSWQ substrate. Bridge theorem `toPOSWQ_interrogation_eq_inquire` proves `interrogationUpdate` is `POSWQ.inquire (polarSetoid q)` on the inquiry side â€” definitional, by the foldr convention chosen so the cons reduces to right-meet. Corollary `boxAns_polar_after_interrogation`: after posing `q`, the polar partition of `q` itself is settled by the resulting POSWQ. The three discourse-update bridges now form a complete table: `assertionUpdate` â†” `plus` â†” `boxCs_after_assertion`; `directionUpdate` â†” `star` â†” `direction_demotes_violators`; `interrogationUpdate` â†” `inquire` â†” `boxAns_polar_after_interrogation`. (3) **`Theories/Semantics/Mood/VerbalMood.lean` extended.** `VerbalMoodOp` gains a third constructor `.interrogative`; `interp` lifts to `POSWQ W â†’ (W â†’ Prop) â†’ Prop` with cases `.indicative â†¦ c.toPOSW.boxCs`, `.subjunctive â†¦ c.toPOSW.boxLt`, `.interrogative â†¦ c.boxAns`. The previous uniform `interp_mono` is replaced by `interp_indicative_mono` and `interp_subjunctive_mono` (each delegating to the corresponding `POSW.box*_mono`); `boxAns` has no upward monotonicity in `p`, and the asymmetry is itself part of the unification's content (different operators, different ordering behaviors, different lattice substrates). Separation theorems: `indicative_ne_subjunctive` (lifted from PR I via `POSWQ.ofPOSW sepPOSW`); `interrogative_ne_indicative` (delegates to `POSWQ.boxAns_not_reducible_to_boxCs`). `MoodSelector.toVerbalMood` is left unchanged (declarative-complement classification doesn't include question-embedders); `toVerbalMood_ne_interrogative` makes the closure explicit â€” the `.interrogative` case is reachable by direct construction, not by `MoodSelector` projection. The completed Portner unification:

| layer            | declarative                  | imperative                    | interrogative              |
|------------------|------------------------------|-------------------------------|----------------------------|
| sentence mood    | assertion (`+`-update on cs) | direction (`â‹†`-update on lt)  | interrogation (`?` on inq) |
| modal necessity  | `boxCs` (informational)      | `boxLt` (preferential)        | `boxAns` (answerhood)      |
| verbal mood      | `interp .indicative`         | `interp .subjunctive`         | `interp .interrogative`    |

Three components (`cs`, `lt`, `inquiry`), three update operations, three necessity modals, three verbal-mood operators â€” all type-driven on a single substrate. Closes the 7-PR Mood API unification arc. **Refactor:** the standalone `Core/Mood/Refinement.lean` from the prep PR is folded into `Core/Mood/POSW.lean` آ§4 (refinement preorder is intrinsic to POSW; mathlib pattern: small natural orders live with the structure). `Mathlib.Order.Basic` is now imported by `POSW.lean`. `Core/Mood.lean` re-exports `POSWQ`. Build: 0 new sorrys; existing pre-PR sorry warnings unaffected.

## [0.229.897] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/Interpret.lean`.** The interpretation function `interpret : NominalKind F â†’ Variables.Assignment F â†’ Variables.SitAssignment F â†’ Option F.Entity`, mapping the `NominalKind` sum to a referent (or `none` for presupposition failure / inapplicability) by composing the maximality/uniqueness operators in `Core/Nominal/Maximality.lean`. Per-constructor reductions: `bare R` and `unique R _` â†¦ `russellIota (R g gs آ·)` (default Core reading is weak/uniqueness; mereologically-aware fragments can opt into `sharvyMax` separately); `indefinite _` â†¦ `none` (existential closure isn't an entity at this layer); `anaphoric R d` â†¦ `some (g d)` if `R g gs (g d)` else `none`; `demonstrative R _ _ d` â†¦ same as anaphoric (deictic feature is a presupposition filter, not a selector); `possessive R poss rel` â†¦ `russellIota` over the restrictor conjoined with the possession relation. Six per-constructor `@[simp]` reductions plus three cross-constructor agreement theorems: `interpret_bare_eq_unique` (Core defaults bare to weak/uniqueness), `interpret_demonstrative_eq_anaphoric` (deictic doesn't change referent), `interpret_unique_index_irrelevant` (the situation-pronoun index records *which* pronoun is bound, not what the operator computes â€” the actual situation comes via the `SitAssignment`). Three witness theorems: `interpret_unique_witness_satisfies` (whatever the operator returns satisfies the restrictor), `interpret_anaphoric_witness_is_indexed` (anaphoric never picks anything other than `g d`), `interpret_anaphoric_isSome_iff` (anaphoric succeeds iff the restrictor accepts the indexed entity). All proofs use `Classical.dec` locally via `letI`. `Linglib.lean` import added.

## [0.229.896] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Allotey 2021 (Gأ£ obligatory control) formalized.** Three new files: (1) `Linglib/Fragments/Ga/Basic.lean` â€” Gأ£ (Kwa, Niger-Congo) fragment: pronoun proclitic paradigm (mi/o/e/wة”/nyة›/amة› أ— sg/pl), TAM (`baa-` future, `mii-` progressive, `أ©-` perfect, `أ،` irrealis tone), three complementizers (`akة›`, `kة›ji`, `ni`) with `Complementizer.isFinite` (`ni` = irrealis), three-way `EmbeddedClauseType` (`finiteAkة› | finiteKة›ji | irrealisNi`) with `ClauseProperties` (unrestrictedTAM, noncoreferentialSubject, finiteComplementizer). (2) `Linglib/Fragments/Ga/Predicates.lean` â€” Gأ£ CTPs with Landau predicate class: subject control `tao` 'want', `kplة›nة”` 'agree', `kpaإ‹` 'plan' (desiderative), `kai` 'remember', `nyة›` 'manage' (implicative); object control `kenya` 'urge', `laka` 'persuade' (desiderative), `dai` 'force' (implicative), `wa` 'help' (unclassified); finite complement `kة›ة›` 'say', `susu` 'think' (propositional). `CTP.controlTier` derives Landau tier per verb. (3) `Linglib/Phenomena/Control/Studies/Allotey2021.lean` â€” `gaOCSignature : EmbeddedClauseType â†’ OCSignature` (irrealisNi â†’ ocFull, finite* â†’ ocNone) with per-clause-type `isOC` theorems by `rfl`; `landau_predicts_control` (Landau cSubjunctive/finite + Agr â†’ Gأ£ OC pattern) by `cases <;> rfl`; per-verb OC theorems for all CTPs in the inventory; `oc_determined_by_clause_type` universal (any CTP whose complement is irrealis shows OC) by `cases <;> rfl`; `gaInventory : MinPronInventory PronForm` with `ga_overt_pro` and `ga_has_reflexive`; pro-drop / overt-PRO universal application (`gaProfile`, `ga_satisfies_universal`); Noonan complement-type bridge `gaToNoonan`. The Pollock 1989 V-movement parallelism in Allotey is deliberately not formalized â€” Pollock's diagnostic requires a free Neg head (Spec-NegP) and Gأ£ `-ee/-ko` is suffixal, so the inference doesn't license without independent morphological argumentation outside the paper. Removed stranded `gaInventory`/`ga_overt_pro`/`ga_satisfies_universal` from `Ostrove2026.lean` (relocated to their proper home in the Gأ£-focused study). Bib entry `allotey-2021` upgraded role: cited â†’ formalized.

## [0.229.895] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/Maximality.lean` â€” Sharvy/Kriإ¾ + Coppockâ€“Beaver operators** â€” Stage 4 of the Definiteness API redesign. Predicate operators over `F.Denot .et` for Stage 5 to compose. (1) **Coppockâ€“Beaver factorization** (@cite{coppock-beaver-2015}): `Existence P := âˆƒx, P x` and `Uniqueness P := âˆ€x y, P x â†’ P y â†’ x = y` as independent predicates; `existsUnique := Existence âˆ§ Uniqueness` is the classical Russellian condition; `existsUnique_iff_existence_and_uniqueness` is `Iff.rfl` (by construction); `uniqueness_does_not_imply_existence` proves the empty predicate satisfies vacuous Uniqueness without Existence â€” the data point that motivates @cite{coppock-beaver-2015}'s split. (2) **Russellian iota** (order-free): `russellIota P : Option F.Entity` returns the unique witness when `existsUnique P`; three theorems wire it together (`russellIota_isSome_iff_existsUnique`, `russellIota_witness_satisfies`, `russellIota_witness_unique`). (3) **Sharvy maximality** (@cite{sharvy-1980}): `IsMaximal P e := P e âˆ§ âˆ€ x, P x â†’ x â‰¤ e` and the selector `sharvyMax P : Option F.Entity` over `[PartialOrder F.Entity]` â€” collapses to Russellian iota on flat orders, returns the @cite{link-1983} sum on join semilattices for plurals; `isMaximal_unique` shows antisymmetry collapses two maxima into one. (4) **Kriإ¾ homogeneity** (@cite{kriz-2015}): `Homogeneous P S := (âˆ€ x, P x â†’ S x) âˆ¨ (âˆ€ x, P x â†’ آ¬ S x)` â€” the predicate that licenses uniform-truth-or-uniform-falsehood for plural definites; `uniqueness_implies_homogeneous_classical` proves Russellian uniqueness vacuously implies homogeneity (the singular-definite limiting case). All proofs use `Classical.dec` locally via `letI` â€” no `native_decide` and no `noncomputable` leakage outside the maximality module. The interpretation function in Stage 5 will compose these operators with the `NominalKind` sum from Stage 3 and the `DenotGS` substrate from Stage 1. No existing call sites touched.

## [0.229.894] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Faust 2026: universal Faust+Kramer integration theorem.** New `intrusion_wellformed_iff_no_intruder_or_n_with_gen` in آ§12 of `Phenomena/TemplaticMorphology/Studies/Faust2026.lean`: for any `RootTemplateMatch m` and any `CatHead ch`, `m.intrusionLicensed ch.licensesIntrusion = true â†” m.hasIntruder = false âˆ¨ (ch.cat = .n âˆ§ ch.phi.gender.isSome = true)`. Universally-quantified statement of @cite{faust-2026} (11), derived by composing `intrusionLicensed_iff` (Templates.lean) with `licensesIntrusion_iff_n_and_gen` (Categorizer.lean) â€” no new computational content; the previously per-instance integration of @cite{faust-2026}'s licensing predicate with @cite{kramer-2020}'s `CatHead` taxonomy is now structural. Two corollaries make the verbal/nominal split explicit: `v_plain_licenses_iff_no_intruder` (under v_plain, licensing reduces to intruder-freeness) and `n_uFem_licenses_universally` (under n_uFem, every match licensed regardless of intruder status). Subsumes the 12 per-derivation `decide` theorems (kept as a per-datum verification roll-call) and the 6-conjunct `verbal_nominal_asymmetry_from_kramer` (kept as the empirical-claim digest); the new theorem is the single structural fact behind both.

## [0.229.893] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/Description.lean` â€” `NominalKind F` sum type** â€” Stage 3 of the Definiteness API redesign. A single sum type, parameterized by a `Core.IntensionalLogic.Frame`, covers the principal flavors of nominal description that the syntaxâ€“semantics interface needs to distinguish: `bare` (number-neutral; covert type-shifts decide the reading), `indefinite` (âˆƒ; introduces a new discourse referent), `unique` (Coppockâ€“Beaver weak/uniqueness; Sharvy/Kriإ¾ maximal â€” carries a `situationIdx` for the Hanink-bound resource situation), `anaphoric` (Schwarz strong-article / Hanink-indexed familiarity â€” carries a `discourseIdx`), `demonstrative` (Patel-Groszâ€“Grosz/Davis: a separate object, not "strong the" â€” carries a `Core.Deixis.Feature` deictic, a situation index, and a discourse index), and `possessive` (definite via a possession relation; carries restrictor + possessor + relation). Every restrictor has type `DenotGS F .et`, the bi-assignment-relative type added in Stage 1 â€” both entity assignments and situation assignments are first-class arguments, the @cite{hanink-2021}/@cite{bondarenko-2023} position. Four classification predicates supplied (`isDefinite`, `isAnaphoric`, `usesSituationPronoun`, `expectedPresupType`); two structural theorems wire them together: `isDefinite_iff_expectedPresup_some` and `isAnaphoric_implies_familiarity`. No semantics yet â€” Stage 4 supplies maximality + Coppockâ€“Beaver uniqueness operators, Stage 5 supplies the interpretation `NominalKind F â†’ DenotGS F .e`. No existing call sites touched. Reuses Stage 2's `Core.Deixis.Feature` for demonstratives.

## [0.229.892] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR I (verbal mood as POSW component selection).** New `Theories/Semantics/Mood/VerbalMood.lean` formalizes @cite{portner-2018} (Ch. 2)'s reduction of the indicative/subjunctive contrast to *which POSW component the embedded clause is evaluated against*. `VerbalMoodOp := indicative | subjunctive`, with `interp : VerbalMoodOp â†’ POSW W â†’ (W â†’ Prop) â†’ Prop` defined as `.indicative â†¦ POSW.boxCs` and `.subjunctive â†¦ POSW.boxLt` â€” type-driven, no auxiliary state. The Truth intuition (Farkas 1985, Portner 1997 tradition) gets `boxCs` (universal quantification over the modal base / informational component); the Comparison intuition (Giorgi & Pianesi 1997 tradition) gets `boxLt` (quantification over the best-ranked subset / preferential component). Theorems: `interp_indicative` / `interp_subjunctive` (definitional simp lemmas), `interp_mono` (uniform upward monotonicity across the Truth/Comparison split), `indicative_ne_subjunctive` (concrete two-world `Bool` POSW where `boxLt sepProp` holds but `boxCs sepProp` fails â€” the type-level reflex of Portner's observation that comparative-meaning predicates entail truth only at the best-ranked subset, not the entire modal base). The `MoodSelector` enum (Mood/Basic.lean) projects to `VerbalMoodOp` via `MoodSelector.toVerbalMood`: `.indicativeSelecting â†¦ some .indicative`, `.subjunctiveSelecting â†¦ some .subjunctive`, variable/neutral â†¦ `none` (their selection pattern is not committed to a single POSW component by lexical class alone). `prefersSubjunctive_eq_subjunctive` proves the existing `prefersSubjunctive` boolean and `toVerbalMood` agree by construction â€” two surface representations of the same lexical-class commitment.

The full Portner unification is now visible at the type level across the three mood layers:

| layer            | indicative-flavored          | subjunctive-flavored          |
|------------------|------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| sentence mood    | assertion (`+`-update on cs) | direction (`â‹†`-update on lt)  |
| modal necessity  | `boxCs` (informational)      | `boxLt` (preferential)        |
| verbal mood      | `interp .indicative`         | `interp .subjunctive`         |

The shared substrate is `POSW`. The previous PRs (E.5â€“H) wired the bottom two rows; PR I closes the top of the table by reading the modal row as a complementizer-domain selector triggered by the matrix predicate's lexical class.

## [0.229.891] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Promote `SpatialRelation` to `Core/Deixis/Feature.lean`** â€” Stage 2 of the Definiteness API redesign. The two-constructor `SpatialRelation` enum (`proximal | distal`) that previously lived locally inside `Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` is now a framework-agnostic `Core.Deixis.Feature` enum with four constructors (`proximal | medial | distal | unspecified`), covering the WALS Ch. 41 distance-system typology: two-way systems (~54% of attested languages: English, Mandarin, French, Shan, Magahi, most IE languages); three-way distance-oriented systems (Latin, Spanish, Hunzib) via `medial`; distance-neutral demonstratives (Modern German *dieser*, Hawaiian no-contrast forms) via `unspecified`. The file documents that adding finer granularity (person-orientation for Japanese ko/so/a, Korean i/ku/ce; visibility for Quechua, Kwakwaka'wakw, ASL; elevation for Dyirbal, Nepali) should follow the *concrete-then-abstract* discipline â€” add a constructor when a fragment actually needs it, not in anticipation. Auxiliary `Feature.encodesDistance : Feature â†’ Bool` (returns `true` iff non-`unspecified`) with a one-line iff characterization. `Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` migrated: `ShanDemonstrative.spatial : Core.Deixis.Feature` (was `SpatialRelation`); `naj`/`nan` constructors unchanged (constructor names `.proximal`/`.distal` preserved); `demDenotation`, `dem_is_anaphoric`, `dem_refines_bare` signatures updated to take `Core.Deixis.Feature â†’ E â†’ Bool` spatial predicates. `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` follows: `open Core.Deixis (Feature)`; one theorem signature retypes to `Feature â†’ E â†’ Bool`. `Linglib.lean` adds `import Linglib.Core.Deixis.Feature`. The unrelated `SpatialRelation` enum inside `Phenomena/Evidentiality/Studies/Koev2017.lean` (`samePlace | differentPlace`, an event-locality predicate for evidentials, not a deictic feature) is a namespacing-distinct local type and is unaffected.

## [0.229.890] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Faust 2026 mathlib-PR-review pass.** Six fixes to `Phenomena/TemplaticMorphology/Studies/Faust2026.lean` and its bridges. (1) Fixed docstring hallucination on `hebrew_kala_unfilled` â€” replaced reference to nonexistent `hebrew_kala_unfilled_but_misalign_safe` with the actual joint statement `qataT_qata_three_way_contrast`. (2/3) Replaced 5 bare-`simp` invocations (lines 711, 753, 763, 788, 1070) with `decide` or `simp only [...]` per CLAUDE.md preference. (4) Promoted `Root.satisfiesOCP` from study-local def to `Core/Morphology/ConsonantalRoot.lean` (alongside a new auxiliary `Root.adjDupCount` â€” segment-level OCP infrastructure that other templatic-morphology studies will want; theory-neutral and not coupled to the tier-projection-level `Phonology.Constraints.adjacentIdentical`). (5) Moved `CatHead.licensesIntrusion` from `_root_`-qualified study-local def to its natural home in `Theories/Morphology/DM/Categorizer.lean` (alongside `satisfiesLicense` and the Kramer taxonomy it ranges over), with the 6 per-canonical-head verification theorems (`n_uFem_licenses_intrusion`, `v_plain_blocks_intrusion`, etc.) and the iff characterization `licensesIntrusion_iff_n_and_gen` migrating with it â€” these test the predicate's behavior against canonical `CatHead`s, not Faust-specific data. (6) Collapsed آ§10 stub (a content-free pointer to آ§13's analysis) into the آ§10 (formerly آ§11) lead-in; renumbered subsequent sections (آ§11â†’آ§10, آ§12â†’آ§11, آ§13â†’آ§12). The Faust2026 file is now ~50 lines shorter and the only Faust-specific content in آ§12 is the per-template `CatHead` tagging (`hebrewTaQTiL_locus := CatHead.n_uFem` etc.) and per-derivation licensing verdicts.

## [0.229.889] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Situation-pronoun substrate in `Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean`** â€” Stage 1 of the Definiteness API redesign. Adds `SitAssignment F := Core.Assignment F.Index` (the type-level dual of the entity `Assignment F := Core.Assignment F.Entity`), `interpSitPronoun n gs := gs n` (parallel to `interpPronoun`), `DenotGS F ty := Assignment F â†’ SitAssignment F â†’ F.Denot ty` (bi-assignment-relative denotations), and lift constructors `DenotGS.ofDenotG` / `DenotGS.const`. Reuses `Core.Assignment` at type `F.Index` so all update lemmas (`update_at`, `update_ne`, `update_overwrite`, `update_comm`, `update_self`) come for free at the new instantiation. The trivial `DenotGS.ofDenotG_const` agreement theorem documents that the two lift paths from a frame-level constant coincide. No existing call sites touched; no theorems weakened. Sets up Hanink (2018, 2021) / Bondarenko (2022, 2023) situation-pronoun binding to be used by the upcoming `NominalKind` sum (Stage 3) and `interpret` function (Stage 5) of the Definiteness redesign.

## [0.229.888] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR H (`directionUpdate â†” POSW.star`).** Completes the 7-PR arc by wiring the directive side of the Portner 2018 unification thesis. Three additions to `Linglib/Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean`: (1) lifted the inline `let rec go` inside `directionUpdate` to a top-level `addGoalAt : List (GoalSet W) â†’ Nat â†’ Nat â†’ Goal W â†’ List (GoalSet W)` helper (out-of-range targets silently leave the list unchanged â€” that's a stipulated property of the discourse update, not of POSW). (2) `Scoreboard.goalContents := K.goals.flatMap GoalSet.toPropertyList` flattens per-agent goal sets into a single property list â€” the substrate of the goal-induced preference ordering. (3) `Scoreboard.toPOSW` no longer carries a trivial `lt`; it now derives it from `goalContents`: `lt w v := âˆ€ p âˆˆ K.goalContents, p v â†’ p w` (`w` is at least as good as `v` iff `w` satisfies every goal `v` satisfies). Reflexivity and transitivity follow trivially from chaining implications. The bridge theorem `toPOSW_direction_eq_star` proves `(K.directionUpdate p s t pr).toPOSW.lt w v â†” (K.toPOSW.star p).lt w v` under the precondition `t < K.goals.length`, mirroring the assertion-side bridge from PR G â€” the proof goes through `mem_addGoalAt_flatMap` (the new directive's content is simply added to the goal-content list, with everything else preserved) and `mem_directionUpdate_goalContents`. Corollary `direction_demotes_violators` proves that after directing `p`, no `p`-violator can dominate a `p`-satisfier in the goal-induced ordering â€” the `lt`-side analogue of `boxCs_after_assertion`. The `lt` refactor required updating the simp lemma `toPOSW_lt` (the trivial `â†” True` is replaced by the goal-quantifier expansion); `toPOSW_cs` and `toPOSW_assertion_eq_plus` are unchanged because the assertion update doesn't touch `K.goals` (`assertion_preserves_goalContents := rfl`).

The two scoreboard updates that target the POSW substrate now correspond, by construction, to the two POSW updates of @cite{portner-2018}:

| scoreboard      | POSW    | informational consequence                  |
|-----------------|---------|--------------------------------------------|
| assertionUpdate | `plus`  | `boxCs_plus_self` (Stalnakerian principle) |
| directionUpdate | `star`  | `lt`-refinement: p-violators demoted       |

Out of scope for this PR (genuinely beyond Portner's two-update substrate): an unconditional `boxLt p` analogue for direction (Portner's `â‹†`-update doesn't make `p` preferentially necessary without an "anchor" assumption â€” the well-known asymmetry between `+` and `â‹†`); interrogation update wiring (a third POSW update mode beyond `+`/`â‹†` would be a new arc, not a completion of this one).

## [0.229.887] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`HarmonySystem` now structurally contains its `TierRule`** â€” the bridge architecture from 0.229.884 (parallel `triggerValue` + `toTierRule` + `toTierRule_agrees_with_triggerValue` proving them equivalent under blocker-free, transparent-trigger conditions) is replaced by structural containment. The new `Theories/Phonology/Harmony/Defs.lean` defines `HarmonySystem` as a record carrying a `rule : Phonology.Alternation.TierRule Segment` (the formal core â€” @cite{belth-2026}'s tier-based AGREE rule, the central object in modern computational/learnability phonology) plus `feature : Feature`, `isTarget : Segment â†’ Bool`, and `isBlocker : Segment â†’ Bool` (the @cite{rose-walker-2011} typological "spreading discipline" layered on top). `triggerValue` is now defined as `sys.rule.apply` dispatched on `harmonyDomain sys stem` â€” *true by construction*, no bridge theorem, no disjointness side-conditions, no possibility of the typological wrapper and the formal rule silently disagreeing.
- **`HarmonySystem.mk'` smart constructor** exposes the @cite{rose-walker-2011} six-way decomposition (feature / isTrigger / isTarget / isTransparent / direction / isBlocker) as named arguments; the constructor compiles these into the underlying `TierRule` (tier projects out transparent segments; side derives from direction; context class is the trigger predicate; relation is `.agree`; featureValue extracts `s.spec feature`). This is the public construction API â€” direct positional record syntax (`{ feature := ..., isTrigger := ..., ... }`) no longer works, since `isTrigger`/`isTransparent`/`direction` are no longer top-level fields. Migration: Turkish (`palatalHarmony`, `labialHarmony`), Finnish (`finnishHarmony`), Hungarian (`hungarianPalatalHarmony`, `hungarianLabialHarmony`, `palatalHarmony_eBlocks`) all switched to `HarmonySystem.mk'`.
- **`HarmonyDir.toSide` bridge** (`HarmonyDir â†’ Phonology.Alternation.Side`): rightward / bidirectional â†¦ `.left` (context precedes the suffix slot); leftward â†¦ `.right`. Bidirectional collapses to rightward operationally (the same `harmonyDomain`/`triggerValue` computation handles both); the typological distinction lives only at the `mk'` argument site.
- **Convenience accessor** `Phonology.Harmony.isTrigger sys s := sys.rule.targetIsContext s` recovers the Rose-Walker trigger field from the underlying rule.
- **Deleted**: `HarmonySystem.toTierRule` (now redundant â€” `sys.rule` IS the TierRule), `toTierRule_agrees_with_triggerValue` bridge theorem (true-by-construction now), `filter_through_byClass` private lemma (was only there to support the bridge), and the `direction : HarmonyDir` field on `HarmonySystem` (encoded in `rule.side`; preserved as a smart-constructor argument only). One Hungarian fragment theorem (`hungarian_finnish_same_direction`) restated in terms of `rule.side = .left` rather than the deleted `direction` field.
- **`Theories/Phonology/Alternation.lean` docstring** updated: the wrapping relationship is now described as "Rose-Walker harmony systems structurally contain a `TierRule` as their value-prediction core" (replacing "wrapped via `HarmonySystem.toTierRule`"). The architectural commitment is now visible in the type, not deferred to a bridge theorem.

### Why
The bridge-theorem architecture from 0.229.884 left the relationship between Rose-Walker harmony and Belth-style tier rules implicit: two parallel implementations, glued by an equivalence proof that only held under restrictive hypotheses (no blockers, triggers non-transparent, rightward direction). That dodged exactly the cases where the typology actually does work â€” blockers are the whole point of opaque vs. transparent. The structural containment matches the field's center of gravity: in the modern computational/learnability framing (Heinz/Jardine/Chandlee TSL, @cite{belth-2026}), tier projection plus a local rule on it is the formal primitive; the @cite{rose-walker-2011} trigger/target/transparent/blocker/direction decomposition is the typological lens for cataloguing what languages do, not a separate formal object. So `HarmonySystem` *contains* a `TierRule` (descriptive wrapper around formal core) rather than *bridging to* one (peer relationship via equivalence).

## [0.229.886] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR G (POSW substrate bridge for `Scoreboard`).** `Linglib/Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean` gains a `toPOSW : Scoreboard W â†’ Core.Mood.POSW W` projection: `cs := K.contextSet`, `lt := fun _ _ => True` (trivial preference ordering â€” directive update via `POSW.star` deferred). Two simp lemmas (`toPOSW_cs`, `toPOSW_lt`) characterize the projection. The Stalnakerian assertion principle ("asserting `p` makes `p` informationally necessary") is derived from `Core.Mood.POSW.boxCs_plus_self` via the bridge: `toPOSW_assertion_eq_plus` proves `(K.assertionUpdate p a).toPOSW.cs = (K.toPOSW.plus p).cs` (one-line `simp` + `And.comm` â€” the assertion update *is* POSW's `plus` after projecting); `boxCs_after_assertion` then chains them to derive `(K.assertionUpdate p a).toPOSW.boxCs p`. The CG storage stays as `List (W â†’ Prop)` (move-tracking history is preserved); only the projected POSW substrate is exposed for properties about the context set itself. This completes the 7-PR mood-API unification arc started in PR A; the remaining gap is wiring directive updates through `POSW.star`, which would require deriving a preference ordering from goals (deferred â€” a substantive Cohen-style modeling decision rather than mechanical bridging).

## [0.229.885] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR F (rename local `ClauseType` enums to disambiguate from `Core.Grammar.ClauseForm`).** Six files defined a local `inductive ClauseType` whose name collided with `Core.Grammar.ClauseForm` (the form-level matrix-vs-embedded distinction renamed in PR D) and its `Core.Mood.ClauseType` cousin (the force أ— verbal-mood pairing). Audit revealed that all six are *orthogonal* to clause form â€” they classify embedded-complement subtype, voice construction, argument structure, or fine-grained question subtypes that `ClauseForm` does not make distinctions among. The right fix is naming, not refit: each enum gets a more specific name reflecting what it actually classifies. Six renames: `Fragments/Mixtec/SMPM/Basic.lean` `ClauseType` â†’ `EmbeddedClauseType` (finite vs subjunctive embedded complements; consumer `Phenomena/Control/Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` updated); `Fragments/Malayic/VoiceSystem.lean` `ClauseType` â†’ `VoiceConstruction` (active/diPassive/barePassive/objectExtraction; consumer `Phenomena/FillerGap/Studies/ErlewineSommerlot2025.lean` updated); `Phenomena/Possession/Studies/AissenPolian2025.lean` `ClauseType` â†’ `ArgumentStructureClass` (unaccusative/transitive/unergative â€” vP-layer presence); `Phenomena/WordOrder/Studies/Westergaard2009.lean` `ClauseType` â†’ `V2ClauseType` (closest to `ClauseForm` shape but adds exclamative + embedded-decl that `ClauseForm` doesn't model); `Phenomena/Focus/DiscourseOnly.lean` `ClauseType` â†’ `DiscourseOnlyClauseType` (canonical/biased/rhetorical question subtypes); `Theories/Semantics/Attitudes/Preferential.lean` `ClauseType` â†’ `HighlightingClauseType` (declarative/polar-interrog/constituent-interrog for highlighting analysis). No `Core.Grammar.ClauseForm` indexed-inductive refits attempted â€” `ClauseForm` lacks `exclamative` and several finer-grained question subtypes, so a partial refit would produce more friction than the rename. Naming-only change; no semantic content moved.

## [0.229.884] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Theories/Phonology/Alternation.lean`** â€” canonical SPE-style tier-driven alternation schema. `TierRule خ±` bundles a `Core.Tier خ± خ±` projection, a `Side` (left/right context), a triggering natural-class predicate `targetIsContext : خ± â†’ Bool`, an Agree/Disagree `Relation`, a partial feature extractor `featureValue : خ± â†’ Option Bool` (honestly partial â€” no junk-default sentinels), and an Elsewhere `default : Option Bool`. `apply` consumes both pre- and post-context lists; `applyAt` is the left-only convenience used by Belth's Latin / Turkish-VH / Finnish-VH rules. `Relation.flip_flip` and `flipRelation_flipRelation` are the involutivity simp lemmas. `id_tier_left_is_strict_local` proves the strict-locality special case (when the tier is the identity, the rule reduces to scanning the raw input â€” Belth's eq. 49b voicing-assimilation pattern). This is the single canonical home for tier-driven alternation rules; downstream theories (harmony, Latin liquid dissimilation, Turkish/Finnish VH) compile to or wrap it.
- **`Core.Tier.lastWith` / `Core.Tier.firstWith`** (`Core/StringHom.lean`) â€” derived helpers for "the last/first tier-projected segment matching predicate `q`". Used by `TierRule.apply` to locate the triggering context. Pure conveniences over `(apply T xs).filter q |>.getLast?` / `.head?`; no new abstraction.
- **`HarmonySystem.toTierRule` bridge** (`Theories/Phonology/Harmony/Defs.lean`) â€” Rose-Walker harmony systems compile to `TierRule` via the unified schema: tier projects out transparent segments (`Tier.byClass (آ¬ isTransparent)`), side mirrors `direction` (rightward/bidirectional â†¦ left context, leftward â†¦ right), `targetIsContext := isTrigger`, `relation := .agree`, `featureValue := s.spec sys.feature`. The downstream OT machinery (triggerValue/spreadSuffix/HarmonyConfig) is preserved unchanged â€” the bridge documents the relationship between Rose-Walker and Belth-style schemas without replacing either. Bridge theorem `toTierRule_agrees_with_triggerValue` proves the two compute the same value when (a) direction is rightward, (b) no blockers in the stem, (c) triggers are not transparent. Private lemma `filter_through_byClass` extracts the tier-projection consistency step.
- **`Phenomena/PhonologicalAlternation/Studies/Belth2026.lean` lun-aris empirical-limit demonstration.** Two competing rules: `latinDissimCoarse` (Belth's [+cons] tier â€” the schema in eq. 6) and `latinDissimFine` (Cser-style: [+cons] minus nasals). For `lunaris`, the coarse rule predicts `l` (the last [+cons] segment is /n/, [âˆ’lat], Disagree â†’ [+lat]) but the surface form is `r`; the fine rule excludes /n/ from the tier, so the last tier segment is /l/ (+lat), Disagree â†’ [âˆ’lat] = `r`. Theorems `lunaris_coarse_predicts_l_INCORRECT` and `lunaris_fine_predicts_r` exhibit the contrast; `coarse_fine_disagree_on_lunaris` joins them. Per-datum corpus (5 forms أ— 2 rules = 10 stimulus-contrast theorems via `decide`) plus `latinDissimFine_covers_all` (the fine tier is empirically adequate over the corpus) and `latinDissimCoarse_misses_lunaris` (the coarse tier is not). Also: `LatSeg.isLat : LatSeg â†’ Option Bool` (L â†’ none â€” the underspecified target), replacing the prior junk-default `LatSeg.isLat L = false` sentinel; `LatSeg.fineTier` is the Cser-style projection. `latinFineOCP : Core.OT.NamedConstraint (List LatSeg)` instantiates `mkOCPOnTier` on the fine tier as the OT-side bridge to existing markedness machinery (plus `latinFineOCP_is_markedness`).

### Changed
- **`Theories/Learning/TolerancePrinciple.lean`** â€” extracted `threshold_nonneg : âˆ€ n, 0 â‰¤ threshold n` as a public lemma (was inlined in `tolerates_zero`); `tolerates_zero` now reduces to `simpa [tolerates] using threshold_nonneg n`. Module docstring trimmed: removed the aspirational paragraph claiming the threshold function `خ¸(n) = n / log n` is derived "from information-theoretic considerations" (it's a heuristic from @cite{yang-2016}; the file proves only the elementary monotonicity / nonnegativity properties of `tolerates`).
- **`Phenomena/PhonologicalAlternation/Studies/Belth2026.lean`** â€” module docstring no longer cites equation/section numbers from memory (per CLAUDE.md "Do not cite equation, table, or section numbers from memory" rule). Earlier misattribution of the floralis/legalis [+cons] coarse-tier rule to Cser corrected to Belth (Cser's contribution is the [-nasal] refinement). Per-datum verification theorems use `decide` and structural reduction (no `native_decide`).

## [0.229.883] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR E.5 (German fragment extraction + theory file rename).** `Theories/Semantics/Mood/SentenceMoodUCI.lean` mixed two concerns: language-agnostic UC-style sentence-mood operators (DEONT, EPIS, the E modifier, HKNOW) and a German-specific clause-type taxonomy with mood compositions. Per the dependency-discipline rule (Theories âٹƒ Fragments âٹƒ Phenomena), per-language clause-type inventories belong in `Fragments/<Language>/`. New `Linglib/Fragments/German/ClauseTypes.lean` collects the German bits: `GermanClauseType : ClauseForm â†’ Type` (the indexed inductive from PR E), `dassVLMood`/`v2DeclMood`/`v2InterrogMood`/`vlInterrogMood`, `GermanClauseType.moodStructure`, and the German-specific theorems (`every_clause_has_deont`, `imperative_lacks_epis`, `dassVL_matches_imperative`, `v2_vl_differ_only_in_hknow`, `no_german_echo`, `matrix_question_is_v2_interrog`, `embedded_question_is_vl_interrog`, `hknow_iff_matrix_question`, `matrix_question_has_hknow`, `declarative_fiber_underdetermines_mood`, `dassVL_is_pure_deontic`, `v2_vs_vl_interrog`, `imperative_equals_dassVL`). Theory file slimmed to `MoodContext`/`deont`/`epis`/`episModifier`/`hknow`/`MoodStructure`/`epis_preserves_truth` only. The theory file is also renamed: `Theories/Semantics/Mood/SentenceMoodUCI.lean` â†’ `Theories/Semantics/Mood/Gutzmann.lean`, parallel to `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Iatridou.lean` (author-keyed naming for one-theorist frameworks). Namespace `Semantics.Mood.SentenceMoodUCI` â†’ `Semantics.Mood.Gutzmann`. Consumers (`Fragments/German/ModalParticles.lean`, `Phenomena/SentenceMood/Studies/Gutzmann2015.lean`) gain a `Linglib.Fragments.German.ClauseTypes` import and an `open Fragments.German.ClauseTypes` line for `GermanClauseType`.

## [0.229.882] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR E (`GermanClauseType` as `ClauseForm`-indexed inductive).** Rather than defining `GermanClauseType` as a flat enum with a separate forgetful function `toClauseForm : GermanClauseType â†’ ClauseForm` and bridge theorems, `Theories/Semantics/Mood/SentenceMoodUCI.lean` now defines it as an *indexed inductive* `GermanClauseType : ClauseForm â†’ Type`, with each constructor specifying the `ClauseForm` it refines (dassVL / v2Declarative / imperative â†¦ `.declarative`; v2Interrogative â†¦ `.matrixQuestion`; vlInterrogative â†¦ `.embeddedQuestion`). The "projection to `ClauseForm`" is now the type-level index â€” no separate function, no bridge theorem, no `cases ct <;> rfl` proof obligation. Three structural consequences fall out of the indexing rather than requiring side conditions: (a) `GermanClauseType .echo` is uninhabited by typing (`no_german_echo : IsEmpty (GermanClauseType .echo)`) â€” the German fragment's silence on echo questions is type-enforced; (b) `(ct : GermanClauseType .matrixQuestion)` is *exactly* `v2Interrogative` â€” `cases` produces a single branch (`matrix_question_is_v2_interrog`); (c) `hknow_iff_matrix_question` is restated as a fact about the index (`hasHearerKnowledge â†” f = .matrixQuestion`). The `.declarative` fiber stays many-to-one â€” `declarative_fiber_underdetermines_mood` shows that `(dassVL : GermanClauseType .declarative).moodStructure â‰  (v2Declarative : GermanClauseType .declarative).moodStructure` (both sides have the *same* type by the indexing, sharpening the contrast). Consumers in `Fragments/German/ModalParticles.lean` and `Phenomena/SentenceMood/Studies/Gutzmann2015.lean` migrated: `licensedInClause`, `wohl_iff_epis`, `ja_denn_complementary`, `root_rule`, `wohl_derived_from_epis`, `ja_denn_partition` all gain an implicit `{f : ClauseForm}` parameter (the proof terms are unchanged â€” `cases ct` still does the right thing). This is the mathlib-style way to encode "type X refines type Y": parameterize the refined type by the parent rather than bolting on a forgetful function afterward.

## [0.229.881] - 2026-04-17

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR C+D (TTR `IllocForce` collapse + `ClauseType` â†’ `ClauseForm` rename).** Two cleanups completing the form/force separation begun in PRs Aâ€“B. (PR C) `Theories/Semantics/TypeTheoretic/Discourse.lean`'s parallel four-way `IllocForce` enum (assertion/query/command/acknowledgement) eliminated in favor of `Core.Mood.IllocutionaryMood` â€” the surface distinction is the declarative/interrogative/imperative slice plus a backchannel constructor; backchannels live in `Theories/Pragmatics/Dialogue/KOS/`, not in TTR. `ForcedSign.illoc` now has type `IllocutionaryMood`. (PR D) `Core/Grammar.lean`'s `ClauseType` (the *form*-level distinction: declarative / matrixQuestion / embeddedQuestion / echo) renamed to `ClauseForm` to disambiguate from `Core.Mood.ClauseType` (the *force* أ— verbal-mood pairing). The `clauseType` field on `MinimalPair`/`SentencePair` is preserved as-is â€” only the inductive type renamed. Bridge functions in `Discourse.lean` renamed: `IllocutionaryMood.toClauseType?` â†’ `toClauseForm?`, `IllocutionaryMood.fromClauseType` â†’ `fromClauseForm`. Consumers updated: `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/{Inversion, Formal/Workspace}.lean`, `Theories/Syntax/HPSG/{Inversion, Core/Basic}.lean`, `Theories/Syntax/DependencyGrammar/Core/NetworkIntegration.lean`, plus docstring references in `Phenomena/WordOrder/Studies/Hudson1984.lean` and `Phenomena/FillerGap/Studies/Sag2010.lean`. Files with local `inductive ClauseType` enums (Mixtec/SMPM, Malayic/VoiceSystem, Westergaard 2009, Aissen-Polian 2025, Focus/DiscourseOnly, Attitudes/Preferential) intentionally untouched â€” those are the targets of PR F.

## [0.229.880] - 2026-04-17

### Added
- **Faust 2026 آ§11: Strict-CV hollow-root NCC asymmetry (eq. 13aâ€“c).** New `Theories/Phonology/Templates.lean` infrastructure: `RootTemplateMatch.unfilledCSlots` (lists the C-slot indices left unfilled by all associations) and `RootTemplateMatch.violatesNCC` (the No-Crossing Constraint of @cite{goldsmith-1976}: an intruder at slot `i` violates NCC if any root association sits at slot `j > i`). Constraint definitions: `noCross : NamedConstraint` (binary markedness, plus `noCross_is_markedness` proof) and `fill : NamedConstraint` (FILL of @cite{prince-smolensky-1993}, binary markedness, plus `fill_is_markedness`). New `Phenomena/TemplaticMorphology/Studies/Faust2026.lean` آ§11 derives the asymmetry: âˆڑsma INF [mأ¤smat] (final-empty: [t]-intrusion at right edge satisfies template without crossing); âˆڑsam INF [mأ¤sam] and âˆڑhid INF [mأ¤hid] (medial-empty: hypothetical [t]-intrusion at medial slot would have to cross the final root association). Theorems: `amharic_sma_inf_satisfies`, `amharic_sma_inf_no_ncc`, `amharic_sam_inf_medial_unfilled`, `amharic_hid_inf_medial_unfilled`, `amharic_sam_inf_intrusion_violates_ncc`, `amharic_hid_inf_intrusion_violates_ncc`, plus `hollow_root_intrusion_asymmetry` (joint statement of the (13a) vs (13b/c) contrast).
- **Faust 2026 آ§12: Explicit OT tableau, *Misalign >> FILL.** Two `mkTableau` demonstrations on inductive candidate enums (matching the @cite{mccarthy-prince-1995} pattern in `Phenomena/Reduplication/`). (a) `KljCand` (spread/empty) for âˆڑklj + Hebrew PST.3MSG: `kala_wins_under_misalign_over_fill` proves [kala] is selected under the empirical ranking; `kalal_predicted_under_reversed_ranking` proves the reversed ranking would (incorrectly) select \*[kalal], showing that *Misalignment dominance is doing the empirical work. (b) `TaQTiLCand` (spread/empty/intrude) for âˆڑdmj + Hebrew taQTiL[+c]: both `tadmit_wins_under_misalign_over_fill` and `tadmit_wins_under_fill_over_misalign` show that the intrusion candidate is *strictly better* than both alternatives (0 violations on both constraints), so it wins under any ranking â€” the formal statement of @cite{faust-2026}'s observation that intrusion lets the grammar "have it both ways".

## [0.229.879] - 2026-04-17

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR B (POSW substrate)** â€” `Core/Mood/POSW.lean` introduces `POSW W`, the Portner 2018 substrate that unifies verbal mood, sentence mood, and modal flavor: a context set `cs : W â†’ Prop` paired with a reflexive transitive ordering `lt`. Two updates: `plus` (`+`-update intersects `cs`, leaves `lt` alone â€” assertion أ  la @cite{stalnaker-1978}) and `star` (`â‹†`-update refines `lt` by promoting `p`-worlds, leaves `cs` alone â€” directive أ  la @cite{portner-2004}). Two necessity modals: `boxCs` (informational â€” `believe`, declarative entailment) and `boxLt` (preferential â€” `want`, deontic/bouletic). Algebraic facts: `plus_cs`/`plus_lt`/`star_cs`/`star_lt` simp lemmas; `updates_target_disjoint_components` is the mathematical content of @cite{portner-2018}'s unification thesis (`+` and `â‹†` touch *disjoint* POSW components); `boxCs_plus_self` records the Stalnakerian assertion principle (after `+`-updating with `p`, `p` is informationally necessary); `boxCs_mono`/`boxLt_mono`/`plus_cs_mono` capture standard upward monotonicity.
- **`Core/Mood/POSWTarget.lean`** â€” `POSWTarget` enum (`informational | preferential | partition`) plus `HasPOSWTarget` typeclass with instances for `GramMood` (indicative â†¦ informational, subjunctive â†¦ preferential â€” verbal mood; @cite{portner-2018} eq. 5) and `IllocutionaryMood` (declarative/exclamative â†¦ informational, imperative/promissive â†¦ preferential, interrogative â†¦ partition â€” sentence mood; @cite{portner-2018} eq. 8). `mood_alignment_decl_ind` proves Portner's indicative principle (declarative force and indicative mood target the same POSW component on a canonical `ClauseType.declInd`); `mood_misalignment_polar_question` records the *intentional* misalignment of polar questions (interrogative force partitions `cs` while indicative mood is informational â€” the interrogative reshapes the substrate rather than aligning with it).
- **bib entry** â€” `portner-2018` (Mood, Oxford University Press, Oxford Surveys in Semantics and Pragmatics) added to `blog/data/references.bib`.

## [0.229.878] - 2026-04-17

### Changed
- **Stale `subjectTheta`/`objectTheta` documentation cleanup.** The `subjectTheta`/`objectTheta` label fields and `derivedSubjectTheta`/`derivedObjectTheta` accessors had already been removed from `VerbCore` (verbs now expose `subjectEntailments`/`objectEntailments : Option EntailmentProfile` as the authoritative proto-role representation), but two study-file docstrings still referred to the legacy fields by name. `Phenomena/PsychVerbs/Studies/Kim2024_UPH.lean` rewritten to describe the consistency check in terms of `causalSource` + `opaqueContext` + entailment profiles (the four-field architecture is now three: causal source, opacity, and the proto-role profiles). `Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Studies/Kratzer1996.lean` module docstring rewritten to clarify that the Severing-vs-Lexicalist comparison machinery now lives in `VoiceTheta.lean` and operates on entailment profiles; the orphaned "removed Parts Iâ€“II" inline note retired (the deletion is recorded in this changelog and in MEMORY.md).

## [0.229.877] - 2026-04-17

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR A (`Core/Mood/` consolidation)** â€” gathered the previously scattered mood-category files into a single mathlib-style namespace. Files moved: `Core/GrammaticalMood.lean` â†’ `Core/Mood/Basic.lean` (now `namespace Core.Mood`); `Core/ClauseType.lean` â†’ `Core/Mood/ClauseType.lean` (now `namespace Core.Mood`). Files added: `Core/Mood/IllocutionaryMood.lean` (extracted from `Core/Discourse/IllocutionaryForce.lean`; carries `IllocutionaryMood` + `moodAuthority`); `Core/Discourse/Roles.lean` (extracted `DiscourseRole` + `resolveRole` to break the otherwise-circular Mood â†” Discourse dependency); `Core/Mood.lean` (re-export hub: `import Linglib.Core.Mood` pulls in all three subfiles). After the move, namespace mirrors directory: `Core.Mood.GramMood`, `Core.Mood.IllocutionaryMood`, `Core.Mood.ClauseType`, `Core.Mood.moodAuthority` all live where their file paths suggest.
- **`Core/Discourse/IllocutionaryForce.lean`** is now a strictly pragmatic-act file: `DirectionOfFit`, `SearleClass`, `PreparatoryCondition`, and the dot-method extensions `IllocutionaryMood.searleClass` / `IllocutionaryMood.directionOfFit` (the latter pair re-namespaced to `Core.Mood.IllocutionaryMood` so dot notation works on the relocated enum). The mood category itself, the discourse roles, and `moodAuthority` no longer live here.
- **`Core/Mood/ClauseType.lean`** gains a `UD.Mood â†’ Core.Mood.ClauseType` bridge (`UD.Mood.toClauseType`) plus the projection theorems `Imp_is_imperative` and `Sub_is_subjunctive`, replacing the previous absence of any explicit UDâ†”ClauseType connection.
- **`SAPMood.toIllocutionaryMood` (with the documented `subjunctive â†¦ promissive` hallucination) replaced by `SAPMood.toClauseType : SAPMood â†’ ClauseType`** in `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/SpeechActs.lean`. The new bridge sends S&T's `.subjunctive` (configurational [-finite, hearer does NOT c-command content]) to `âں¨declarative, subjunctiveâں©` rather than to Searle's commissive class â€” the correct framework-agnostic projection. Convenience projection `SAPMood.toForce := toClauseType.force` preserved for backward-compat call sites. `seat_of_knowledge_agrees_with_mood_authority` now goes through `m.toClauseType.authority` instead of `moodAuthority m.toIllocutionaryMood`.
- All importers updated: `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Basic.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Iatridou.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Mood/Basic.lean`, `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Questions.lean`, `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/SpeechActs.lean`, `Theories/Pragmatics/Assertion/Krifka.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Assert.lean`, `Phenomena/Directives/Studies/RuytenbeekEtAl2017.lean`, `Phenomena/Conditionals/Studies/Stalnaker1975.lean`, `Phenomena/Complementation/Studies/Grano2024.lean`, `Fragments/French/Modals.lean`, `Fragments/German/ModalParticles.lean`, `Core/Discourse/Intentionality.lean`, `Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean`, plus `Linglib.lean`.

## [0.229.876] - 2026-04-17

### Added
- **Faust 2026 "Intrusion as Template Satisfaction and the QaTaTâ€“QaTa Problem in Semitic"** @cite{faust-2026} formalized as `Phenomena/TemplaticMorphology/Studies/Faust2026.lean`. Single alignment principle \*Misalignment ("a nonfinal root segment must not occupy the template-final slot") jointly derives (a) the Hebrew QaTaTâ€“QaTa gap (\*[kalal] from âˆڑklj blocked, [kala] surfaces, @cite{faust-2026} (4)), (b) Hebrew taQTiL templatic intrusion ([tadmit] from âˆڑdmj via feminine-gender /t/, @cite{faust-2026} (10)), and (c) Amharic gerund [t]-intrusion ([fأ¤dت¤to] from âˆڑfdj, @cite{faust-2026} (8)) â€” with the verbal/nominal asymmetry attributed structurally to the n[+gen] vs Agr[+gen] split (cross-domain hookup to `Phenomena/Gender/Studies/Kramer2020.lean`). Eight per-derivation `decide` theorems plus three cross-derivation theorems (`hebrew_klj_spreading_blocked_by_misalignment`, `hebrew_intrusion_satisfies_template_without_misalignment`, `misalignment_predicts_all_four_cases`).
- **`Core/Morphology/ConsonantalRoot.lean`** â€” single source of truth for consonantal roots as `Root خ± := { segments : List خ± }`, parametric over the segment type. Methods: `arity`, `isFinal`, `isNonfinal`, `biradical`/`triradical`/`quadriradical`, `finalSegment`, `segmentAt`. Mathlib-style: one carrier shared between phonology, morphology, and fragment lexica; no per-study re-stipulation. Distinguished from `Semantics.Verb.Roots.Root` (LexEntailment bundle) and `Core.Lexical.RootFeatures` (semantic quality dims).
- **`Theories/Phonology/Templates.lean`** â€” McCarthy 1981 / Strict-CV (@cite{lowenstamm-1996}) templatic-morphology infrastructure: `CVSlot` (`.C | .V | .Cspec` for [+consonantal]-specified slots), `Template` (sequence of slots with `length`/`cCount`/`isFinalSlot`/`slotAt`), `AssocSource` (`.root | .intruder` â€” the load-bearing distinction that makes \*Misalignment exempt sister-exponent intruders أ  la @cite{lowenstamm-2014}), `Association` (root-to-slot link, defaulting to `.root`), and `RootTemplateMatch خ±` carrier with derived predicates `isRootFinal`, `isMisaligned`, `allCSlotsFilled`, `satisfies`, `inBounds`. Defines the \*Misalignment constraint of @cite{faust-2026} (2) as `starMisalign := mkAlign "*Misalign" RootTemplateMatch.isMisaligned`. Generic theorems: `isMisaligned_empty`, `isMisaligned_intruder_only`, `starMisalign_is_markedness`.
- **`Fragments/Hebrew/ConsonantalRoots.lean`** â€” Modern Hebrew root inventory typed by `Root String`: [j]-final triradicals (âˆڑklj, âˆڑdmj, âˆڑbnj â€” the QaTaTâ€“QaTa trigger class) and non-glide-final controls (âˆڑktv, âˆڑsbr). Per-root `triradical = true` decide-theorems plus `klj_final_is_j`.
- **`Fragments/Amharic/ConsonantalRoots.lean`** â€” Amharic root inventory typed by `Root String`: [j]-final triradicals (âˆڑfdj, âˆڑhid, âˆڑsma, âˆڑsam â€” the [t]-intrusion class) plus âˆڑsbr (control) and âˆڑwdd (Faust's reanalysis of @cite{broselow-1984}'s "biradical" âˆڑwd as triradical with identical final radicals). Includes `wdd_is_not_biradical` proving Faust's central reanalysis claim against the OCP-violating Broselow representation.
- **bib entries** â€” `faust-2026`, `mccarthy-1981`, `broselow-1984`, `lowenstamm-1996`, `lowenstamm-2014` added to `blog/data/references.bib`.

## [0.229.875] - 2026-04-17

### Added
- **`Core/StringHom.lean`** â€” unified erasing string-homomorphism abstraction. `StringHom خ± خ² := خ± â†’ List خ²` (general letterwise hom, lifted via `List.flatMap`); `Tier خ± خ² := خ± â†’ Option خ²` (the erasing case â€” morphism in the Kleisli category of `Option`, lifted via `List.filterMap`). Constructors: `Tier.id'`, `Tier.empty`, `Tier.byClass`, `Tier.total`, plus Kleisli composition `(âˆکâ‚œ)`. Monoid-hom laws (`apply_append`, `apply_nil`) hold definitionally; `apply_comp`, `id_comp`, `comp_id` round out the category structure. `applyI` carries source indices to match @cite{belth-2026}'s `proj(x, T)`.
- **`Theories/Phonology/Tier.lean`** â€” phonology-flavored re-export of `Core.Tier` with `byFeature`, `byFeaturePred`, and `tonal` constructors. `Phonology.Tier خ± خ²` is *definitionally* `Core.Tier خ± خ²` so phonological constraint constructors (`mkOCP`) accept `Tier.apply T` directly with no adapter glue.
- **`mkOCPOnTier`** (`Phonology/Constraints.lean`) â€” tier-driven OCP adapter: `mkOCPOnTier name T extract = mkOCP name (Tier.apply T âˆک extract)`. Grounding theorem `mkOCPOnTier_eq_mkOCP : rfl`. Markedness-classification theorem `mkOCPOnTier_is_markedness`.

### Changed
- **`BasemapCorrespondence.tonalTier`** now grounds in `Phonology.Tier.apply Phonology.Tier.tonal` (the unified tier interface) instead of inlining `List.map TBU.tone`. New `tonalTier_eq_map` simp lemma re-exposes the historical map form for downstream proofs in `BasemapCorrespondence.lean` and `DominantCophAgreement.lean`.

## [0.229.874] - 2026-04-17

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR B (Stalnaker conditionals around `GramMood`)** â€” replaced the two parallel defs `indicativeConditional` and `subjunctiveConditional` (introduced in 0.229.872, both definitionally equal to `selectionConditional`) with a single `moodedConditional (m : GramMood) (s p q)` plus a separate `Mood.admissibleSelection : GramMood â†’ SelectionFunction W â†’ ContextSet W â†’ Prop`. The mood index is now metadata at the truth-conditional level (single source of truth: `moodedConditional m s p q â‰، selectionConditional s p q` for all `m`); the *semantic* mood difference lives in `admissibleSelection`, which sends `.indicative â†¦ pragmaticConstraint s C` and `.subjunctive â†¦ True`. This makes Stalnaker's actual claim â€” the mood difference is in *which selection functions are admissible*, not in the truth-conditional clause â€” explicit at the type level rather than restated in two def headers and a `rfl` bridge theorem. Replaces `indicative_eq_subjunctive` (now trivial) with `moodedConditional_eq_selectionConditional`.
- **`indicative_eq_material_within_context` â†’ `selectionConditional_eq_material_within_context`** plus a thin `moodedConditional_indicative_eq_material_within_context` specialisation. The general theorem is now keyed on the pragmatic constraint (one assumption, one selection conditional), not on a mood-specific def.
- **`Phenomena/Conditionals/Studies/Stalnaker1975.lean`** updated to use the new API: all four direct-argument theorems (`direct_argument_reasonable`, `direct_argument_reasonableInference`, `direct_argument_not_entailment`, `direct_argument_holds_under_indicative_selection`) now call `moodedConditional .indicative s` instead of the deleted `indicativeConditional s`.

### Added
- **`Iatridou.CounterfactualType.toSubjunctiveType`** â€” bridge from Iatridou's three counterfactual types (FLV, PresCF, PastCF) to framework-agnostic `Core.SubjunctiveType.counterfactual`. All three collapse to the same tag (`all_counterfactuals_are_counterfactual`), making explicit at the type level that they share a unified semantic exponent (modal ExclF) â€” previously a docstring-only claim.

## [0.229.873] - 2026-04-17

### Added
- **`applyAccAgree` and `applyGenAgree` passes** (`DependentCase.lean`) â€” `accMode := .agreeV` and `genMode := .agreeD` now genuinely dispatch instead of being dead enum cases. `applyAccAgree` targets the lowest unmarked visible CP-cycle NP (analogous to `applyNomAgree`'s highest-NP behaviour); `applyGenAgree` marks every unmarked DP-internal NP. The 7-stage pipeline (s0..s7) plumbs both through `assignCasesPhased`, with corresponding `applyAccAgree_length` and `applyGenAgree_length` lemmas extending `assignCasesPhased_length`.
- **`PhasedNP.inDP` field** (`DependentCase.lean`) â€” distinguishes DP-internal NPs (possessors) from clausal arguments. `unmarkedVisible` now filters out `inDP` NPs so possessors are opaque to clausal case rules; `applyGenAgree` consumes them. Implements the GEN-via-D-Agree half of the Sakha case grammar (@cite{baker-vinokurova-2010} آ§4).
- **آ§14 D-Agree GEN demonstration** (`BakerVinokurova2010.lean`) â€” `possessor` constructor + `possessor_gets_gen_via_agree`, `possessed_head_gets_nom`, `possessor_is_opaque_to_clausal_acc`, `all_four_modalities_in_one_clause`. The last theorem exhibits a single derivation in which NOM (Agree-T), ACC (dependent), DAT (dependent), and GEN (Agree-D) all surface together â€” the four-corners empirical claim of B&V's two-modality grammar.
- **5 preservation lemmas** (`DependentCase.lean` آ§15 "Bleeding by Marking") â€” `applyAccRule_preserves_marked_at`, `applyAccAgree_preserves_marked_at`, `applyNomAgree_preserves_marked_at`, `applyGenAgree_preserves_marked_at`, `applyDefault_preserves_marked_at` plus private structural lemma `setCaseAt_get?_ne` and supporting `unmarkedVisible_unmarked`. Lifts `dat_bleeds_acc_on_vp_cycle` from a per-datum decide-proof to the *general* structural fact that no pass in the pipeline ever overwrites a marked NP. Composed in `dat_persists_through_assignCasesPhased`: any DAT-marked NP at the input survives the entire pipeline.
- **Strengthened `two_modalities_required`** (`BakerVinokurova2010.lean`) â€” extended from 4 to 6 conjuncts, adding the causative-cascade wedge: `pureChomsky` cannot derive the causee â†’ DAT alternation under transitive bases (ex 23). The strongest form of the empirical argument that no pure-Agree theory suffices.

### Changed
- **`applyNomAgree` and `applyAccAgree` now match on List directly** (`DependentCase.lean`) â€” refactored from `(unmarkedVisible cycle states).head?` Option-pattern to `match unmarkedVisible cycle states with | first :: _ | []`, mirroring `applyAccRule`. Enables clean `case h_1` / `case h_2` naming in structural proofs.
- **`unmarkedEligible` predicate factored out of `unmarkedVisible`** (`DependentCase.lean`) â€” the per-state filter (visible أ— argumental أ— آ¬inDP أ— آ¬marked) is now a named definition. The `filterMap` lambda uses projection-based access (`p.1`/`p.2`) instead of pattern destructure to avoid Lean's pattern-lambda non-reduction issue inside structural proofs.

## [0.229.872] - 2026-04-17

### Added
- **Stalnaker 1975 "Indicative Conditionals"** @cite{stalnaker-1975} formalized as `Phenomena/Conditionals/Studies/Stalnaker1975.lean` (~233 LOC) + `Theories/Pragmatics/Assertion/ReasonableInference.lean` (~113 LOC).
